Finally, oh fellow human!  
I wish  
Your body, flesh, or belly,  
Or however one wants to call it,  
A long Sanitas,  
And all the health,  
That was ever wished for  
By poets,  
When someone was of age.

label: birthday
translation:
Come, let us be joyful,  
Falderie, faldera, fal lal derie dera,  
Now you are my chosen one,  
Falderie, faldera, fal lal derie dera,  
Today solemnly you are having your birthday,  
Hu, hu, hu,  
Having your birthday;  
I am glad because of that happiness,  
Hu, hu, hu, hu, hu,  
Faldera, fal lal derie dera,  
I am glad because of that happiness,  
Hu, hu, hu, hu, hu.  
Live long, free from sorrow;  
Falderie, faldera, fal lal derie dera,  
Live with a joyful heart;  
Falderie, faldera, fal lal derie dera:  
Always shun useless worry:  
Hu, hu, hu,  
Worry always,  
Disturb yourself with no hatred nor envy,  
Hu, hu, hu, hu, hu,  
Faldera, fal lal derie dera,  
Disturb yourself with no hatred nor envy,  
Hu, hu, hu, hu, hu.

label: birthday
translation:
Should I not  
Cheerfully greet my Celinde with a song,  
Since today is her birthday?  
Oh yes, certainly ://:  
I will not adorn you, mature girl,  
With any flower or myrtle wreath,  
That autumn robs from us,  
To decorate your youthful head. ://:  
A twenty-five year old person!  
If you do not get what I wish for you,  
You shall receive all good  
From the abundance; ://:  
Yes, I will, with the same truth,  
Warm you year after year,  
And always be glad  
When it is your birthday. ://:

label: birthday
translation:
I do not want to save any joy today,  
On my strings;  
I do not want to save any joy today,  
O Harsh one!  
I will chase you away with a hen  
And an apple pie, friend.  
I will [do] you &c.  
Let me, to think of me,  
Give you something more;  
Let me, to think of me,  
(And as proof  
That nothing can wound my happiness)  
Allow that I enrich you.  
That nothing &c.

label: birthday
translation:
What joy, mariners,  
Blow us the fame of the Hague court,  
At our Prince’s birthday,  
Twelve times in praise,  
Sing with a wedding tune,  
Of Prince Weilburg fair,  
With his worthy bride,  
(Carolien)  
Pride of the Netherlands.  

Let us first celebrate  
Prince William’s birthday  
And express our wish,  
May he live long,  
And see his sisters’ seed  
Increased in matrimony,  
By his brother-in-law Nassau,  
(Prince Weilburg)  
Through love and faithfulness.  
Long live, renowned  
William the Fifth of Nassau,  
Netherlands’ pride on earth,  
Rejoice in the marriage,  
Of your own nearest blood,  
Raised for Carel,  
(Carolien)  
Now well matched with Weilburg.  

After the passage of years  
We also hope to see our Prince,  
Joyfully observe pairs,  
Like Carolien,  
So that we happily in spirit,  
Sing his wedding feast,  
As now of the marriage,  
(Prince Weilburg)  
With his worthy wife.  

Then cease all your sighs,  
Maiden of Holland, forget the mourning,  
The lion creates delights,  
Now the princess is the wife,  
His staff and statehood  
United with Nassau blood,  
Through the marriage of  
(Carolien)  
With her beloved man.  

They saw the couple proceed,  
With a procession of honor and praise,  
Bride and bridegroom both,  
From the city palace court  
By rank and nobility,  
With Prince William, the young flower,  
By the Duke of Brunswick,  
(And of Sweet)  
Ladies of state alike,  
The blue guard on horses,  
Led the procession and advanced,  
By the citizens of worth,  
From the Grenadier’s gate,  
Along the Vyverberg with splendor,  
Then over the Royal Square,  
Many thousands gathered  
(To see the distinguished)  
Airy couple.  

Between banners of honor  
The procession rode along Hoogstraat  
Which was seen advancing  
On the Groenmarkt with one,  
Past the city hall to the church  
Where the sweet love-work  
Of the princely lineage  
(To the marriage)  
Was joyfully fulfilled.  

And beneath the vows  
The bells played the praise,  
Wilhelmus of Nassau,  
On the metal surface,  
As now the dear couple  
Was united before the altar,  
After many blessings concluded,  
(The procession moved)  
Out of the church in rank order again.  

What joy did one hear  
With a sweet hooray sound!  
And how did one see then bow  
Bridegroom and bride,  
With respect and with praise,  
To the Old Court,  
Where the wedding feast  
(Was celebrated)  
With cheerfulness of spirit.  
Would you honor joy  
To the distinguished couple,  
Through illumination,  
Show your love to them,  
Their Highnesses are worthy of it,  
Their equal is on earth,  
Planting in the Hague  
(A prince)  
And a princess falcon.  

Love’s duty was shown:  
Rich and poor according to their state,  
The night (through the light)  
Showed the dawn:  
Under the illumination’s face  
Bacchus-God was illuminated,  
From his sweet grape nectar,  
(Sounds and drinks)  
And shouts of hooray, vive la.

label: birthday
translation:
While we now rejoice,
The eighth of March, in joy,
As in former times, we may celebrate,
So shall we, with joy,
Then celebrate this day,
Uplifted with happiness;
Everyone who cares for the welfare of the country,
Sings with us united,
Long may Prince William live!
He is our best friend.
We may now be free again,
Truly content and happy,
Wearing orange ribbons;
Now we live happily again,
Where the God of the Netherlands, in joy,
Wants to turn away our complaints.
Everyone, etc.

***
The Prince was long dishonored,
Slandered and humiliated,
To the pain of his friends;
But He who rules the universe,
Caused him, in distress and pressure,
To find consolation.
Everyone, etc.

***
The Prince was of good cheer,
When he had to live
In adversity and misfortune,
The Prince was of good cheer,
Trusting that God
Would give him salvation.
Everyone, etc.

***
And He has saved him,
Answering the prayer
Of him and his friends;
God has saved the Prince,
To the joy of the Vroomen, who
Continually loved him.
Everyone, etc.

Since this day,
To our joy, may
Forty years be reached,
Let us thank on this day
His Savior that
He wished to spare him in favor.
Everyone, etc.

***
You, Who freed him
From calamities, and gladdened
By Your great deeds,
Show furthermore that You are
His Helper, always willing
To shower him with blessings.
Everyone, etc.

***
Please, for the benefit of the country
And Church, through Your Hand,
Kindly guide him,
Please, with Your Right Hand,
Protect him, and always
Deliver him from pressure
And calamity.
Everyone, etc.

***
Please guide him, his spouse
And offspring, with tender love,
Through Your Spirit;
Please, with tender love,
Gladden this royal house
By Your grace.
Everyone, etc.

Ah, may the Prince rejoice,
To the joy of all the good,
Live many more years,
Please spare him long in Your favor,
For our joy,
And grant him health and prosperity.
Everyone, etc.

***
Let him, his spouse
And offspring, forever praise
You for Your love
And care, that all tenderly love them,
And with us, sincerely,
Always shout, “Orange above!”
Everyone, etc.

label: birthday
translation:
Triumph! my dear Prince and Lord  
Whom now at this time,  
Deprived of joy, as before,  
Everyone calls together in gladness:  
Long live our brave Prince!  
With his Illustrious House,  
Who freed us from tyranny,  
And other burdensome crosses.  
He dwells with Jesus after his death,  
Where he celebrates eternal blessing.

label: birthday
translation:
All pressure is now gone,  
We experience salvation through God's grace,  
We happily meet William,  
Again in Holland's garden on the meadow.  
Now the time has come again,  
That we may gather joy,  
Joy now fits under one,  
For Orange's dignities.

label: birthday
translation:
Friends come to hear some amusement  
That was on the eighth of March,  
Already in the city of The Hague,  
If one looks at it correctly,  
What a joy we experienced again,  
That our God has rejoiced us again.  
With singing, dancing thousands in number, bis.  
What a joy.

label: birthday
translation:
Rejoice free Netherlands,  
At the Prince’s happy birthday;  
He did not break the bond of unity;  
He spares no Britons  
But fields them, the State gives him power,  
Let Dogger Bank bear witness,  
That sea hero made them bow;  
He is despised as wicked. (repeat)  
That long guarded Prince,  
Now thirty-five years,  
Who bears us to joy,  
Let playthings, let the strings  
Now sound to his honor and praise,  
So that his haters be silent,  
God may soon give them their due,  
They sink into the dust. (repeat)  
But we stand for his honor,  
God always wants to crown him;  
His beloved spares the Lord,  
His daughter and his sons,  
His plants in the free garden,  
God will not abandon her,  
For the welfare of the Seven States,  
Thus sounds the land’s praise trumpet. (repeat)  
The glory of the Oranges will never be defiled.

label: birthday
translation:
This Eighth of March, by God’s divine governance,  
For the Prince of the Netherlands, the most glorious hour,  
The Father of our Land,  
Whose desire and zeal burn  
For Freedom, Church, and State,  
Which now is going to ruin.  
The country’s best Friend, and Enemy of the wicked Briton,  
Is this Prince, who has the courage  
To endure all the cruel violence  
Of slander that torments him,  
While the country’s Power  
Is violated by cowardice.  
Come, let us sing in honor of this Illustrious Hero.  
His age counts five times seven:  
He is the bond of Unity,  
Which will bind our Land.  
He is worthy of our Praise Song,  
On every Eighth of March  
Orange extends glory to the Land.

label: birthday
translation:
We shall sing,  
Your Birthday Great Prince:  
The strings burst forth,  
With full chest;  
I sing now gladly and tired,  
Bring you my thanks.  
Viva, Hooray!  
Viva the Prince of Orange, Hooray!  
Our godless ones,  
Crushed by their power,  
Raped by the French gold,  
Viva, Hooray!  
Viva the Prince of Orange, Hooray!  
Viva, Hooray!  
Viva the Prince of Orange, Hooray!

label: birthday
translation:
Should we not sing joyfully  
Now Her High Nobleman is born;  
Or restrain the true joy  
That is connected to pleasure.  
No, my dear Countrymen!  
I see, how the heart, testifies with me!  
How it can increase its splendor,  
When it cheers for Orange.

label: birthday
translation:
Viva, Orange lives!  
One strikes the silver strings,  
Celebrate William's happy birthday,  
One sings that everything trembles,  
Viva Orange lives,  
Despite the Democrats,  
And those who hate Orange,  
We are on the eighth of March,  
Gathered together with joy,  
No devil is born,  
Who can disturb our joy,  
Even if it were Plato himself,  
From the underworld vault.  
Although hell resists him:  
Viva Orange lives,  
The ship of the Seven Lands,  
He protects from stranding,  
The fiercest state hurricane,  
William again withstands,  
Amid the dangers,  
Of the roaring waves,  
He is the refuge,  
Of the free state of the Netherlands.

label: birthday
translation:
Long live this eighth of March!  
That Willem has a birthday again:  
That brave Prince, whose steadfast patience  
Fills the hearts of the brave citizens,  
With joy, with joy;  
That example of Virtue!  
And calls a traitor to the country,  
Or acknowledges as a tyrant,  
Who violates the old Freedom of the Netherlands,  
He will, he will,  
Soon follow to his fall.

label: birthday
translation:
Rejoice, Batavians,  
That the Free Corps is ashamed.  
Those slanderous beasts,  
All that is proper;  
Are scorned by their wickedness!  
Sing, as you mock them,  
Long live, Hurrah,  
Long live the Prince of Orange, Hurrah.  
But now it cheers already:  
Long live, Hurrah,  
Long live the Prince of Orange, Hurrah.

label: birthday
translation:
How brightly the Morning Star shines for us,  
The Orange Sun also shoots from afar,  
Its rays in our eyes,  
Here also the Orange light shines,  
Very glorious in our countenance,  
And sparkles on the arches,  
This announces, tells,  
Joyful things to sing,  
The honor to unfold,  
Of William of Nassau,  
Joyfully meeting one another,  
So we will again here at this place,  
With hand clapping and joyful hooray,  
Often greet Prince William,  
And with voice,  
Give a chorus of praise, may he live long,  
Of Nassau,  
God let us behold You for a long time.

label: birthday
translation:
Prince Willem is having a birthday again,  
Sings joyfully from the heart,  
No harsh March falls sour,  
It is for the Prince of Orange.  
Who can restrain themselves,  
From singing about such a joyful time,  
For our Prince, despite all who hate him?  
Never fear the hatred again.  
His guiltless conscience,  
Never stained with betrayal,  
Never disgraced honor,  
Which serves as his duty.  
With a birth wreath.  
To this prophetic voice.  
Am I deceiving myself?... what eyes  
Does George Frederik raise!  
Is his soul moved?  
Then does his joy rise to the top?

label: birthday
translation:
Prince Willem Frederik celebrates,  
His eleventh birthday today,  
It is joy in the cities of the Netherlands,  
The orange banner waves,  
The country's heir prince highly celebrates,  
Gives everyone desire and courage,  
How your years already climb, twice.  
Prince, from the Orange blood!  
Therein even the grayness, twice.  
That fits well in the throne.

label: birthday
translation:
Who would not with a joyful spirit,  
Now sing all around,  
At William Frederick's celebration of joy,  
The day of his birth.  
For the benefit of Church and State,  
That wisdom, power, unite:  
O Netherlands' reliance.

label: birthday
translation:
Rejoice Batavians;  
Rejoice in heart and spirit,  
Weave laurels again,  
On the birth celebration,  
Of Nassau's eldest son,  
Raise a thankful tone again.  
Viva Huzza,  
Viva the young Fredrik, Huzza.  
But in distress,  
God's divine decree  
Granted another Orange offspring.  
Viva Huzza,  
Viva the House of Orange, Huzza.

label: birthday
translation:
If we could be silent now,  
Now our Hereditary Prince celebrates?  
Who would expect that from us,  
Dutchmen? right by nature:  
Oranje is on top again,  
It is the Hereditary Prince's day,  
Let us praise with joy again.  
Now this may happen to us.  
How those heroes together,  
Wasted goods and blood for the country.

label: birthday
translation:
Sing freely united groups,  
On this birth celebration:  
Raise again voice and strings,  
Joyful with a happy spirit;  
We must praise our Heritage Prince,  
On his birthday,  
How the Lord above gave him  
To this our Netherlands.

label: birthday
translation:
Save, oh Netherlands, save no effort  
In this pleasant time,  
Show now that you are true Sons  
Of true Freedom:  
Nothing can now stop your Joy,  
While you overcome your Enemy,  
In your wall may now conclude,  
The Netherlands' Help, and Comfort and Friend,

label: birthday
translation:
This joyous time now gives us blessings to boast of,
And it fills our hearts with true joy,
One can breathe freely and happily again,
While one again sees the masterpiece of virtue;
Since this princess may celebrate her birthday today,
The Netherlands shows that nothing hinders your joy
If now for her fame unity is preserved,
Then fame will spread her virtues everywhere.
And the God of the Netherlands, on whom you placed your hope,
Has, by His power, humbled your enemies,
And (whoever regrets it) fame will always report,
That William and Sophia triumph!
M.J. van Hoogenhuizen.

label: birthday
translation:
Rejoice now, good Dutchmen,  
Wave with joy the Orange banners,  
It is the happy Eighth of March.  
It is Orange's birthday. (repeat)  
Sing then again as before,  
Break the branch and bend the reed.  
Yet do not mourn Orange. (repeat)  
Show that you are now joyful,  
And also good Citizens are. (repeat)

label: birthday
translation:
Mode: You, who are now with me hunting.

You, who are now my guest, ha! ha!
Know that it suits us all, - ha, ha!
Here, with a modest glass of wine,
In cheerfulness to be together, - ha, ha!

And why is that? Oh, well: a question! - ha, ha!
Well, Pieter/Trijntje is celebrating a birthday today, - ha, ha!
Indeed! Here adds a generous joy
Come! We are then happy with him/her! - ha, ha!

Friendship reaches out to us, gladly tired, - ha, ha!
Generously offers her hands, ha, ha!
It brings us together, those who see its signs,
That we return her homage! - ha, ha!

Come, friends! shout with joyful sound, ha, ha!
The praise of true friendship, - ha, ha!
That it lives in our hearts,
And always shows its strength and influence! - ha, ha!

But also wishes through her happiness, - ha, ha!
Freed from care, worry, and pressure, - ha, ha!
To Pieter/Trijntje who celebrates this day;
For he/she is truly worthy of our respect! - ha, ha!

Happiness, oh friend, many times more, ha, ha!
Appear, with blessing, your anniversary again! ha, ha!
Live well contentedly; always well in peace,
- 
To the benefit and joy of many! ha, ha!

Remain untouched by sickness and pain! - ha, ha!
May prosperity make you happy! - ha, ha!
Even more than this our heart wishes you:
An old age freed from sorrow! ha, ha!

1801.

label: birthday
translation:
How do friends and companions not cheer,  
When gathered at the annual celebration;  
When the old or the young in years,  
Our dear one, happily has a birthday (twice.)  
One cheers, although each birthday  
Brings them ever closer to the grave;  
Thus we remain focused on the present,  
And are disturbed by no future. (twice.)

What would then hinder our cheering tone,  
Now democracy, so worthy to all,  
Again among its laughing children,  
As if it celebrates like a young man? (twice.)

That others fear old age,  
Never does that thought disturb the joy here;  
Our Elder, no matter how old he may be,  
Always retains the powers of youth. (twice.)  
The altar, consecrated to joy,  
Burns here from generation to generation;  
One remains protected from old age,  
As long as one laughs while toiling (twice.)

label: birthday
translation:
When along a thousand winding roads,  
Or a smooth measured path,  
Beside it a tavern is situated,  
The weary traveler knocks there;  
The innkeeper has barely unlocked his door,  
When it echoes to him, with loud shouting,  
The joyful cheer of travel companions:} twice.  
You are welcome here! You are welcome here!} twice.  
That welcome, brother! rolls out  
From every heart, from every mouth;  
The journey, completed along bumpy roads,  
Leads you to a feast in this moment:  
And would you then forget the Innkeeper,  
Who laughingly let you in,  
On the world’s round, a better knowing,} twice.  
Than Democritus? Than Democritus?} twice.  

If over there the sun was hidden from you,  
Here awaits you the glowing evening red,  
And cheerfully you cast down all your worries  
Into the friends’ lap:  
For whoever sings you the festive song,  
No one voices the jubilation song like  
The cheerful followers} twice.  
Of Democritus! Of Democritus!} twice.  
Come, brothers! Fill the friends’ cup  
With grape-blood, to the brim:  
It counts as a sacred drink for sure;  
Come, all grasp it in your hand!  
May, brother! happiness shine upon you,  
May friendship’s rose adorn your steps,  
And may we often repeat the cry:} twice.  
You are welcome here! You are welcome here!} twice.

label: birthday
translation:
Oh yes, I want to believe it,  
My art deserves complaint,  
And yet, I let myself be heard,  
On your birthday.  
Accept, beloved host!  
My pure greeting of blessing,  
It is that of us all,  
Inspired by a single glow.

I do not predict for you any increase  
Of gold or silver money,  
But may it flow to you abundantly,  
If you desire it!  
I do not spell out titles for you,  
No knighthood's seal,  
Yet it can give you joy,  
May you delight in the pleasure!  
I wish you no permanent  
Lodging here on earth,  
That would be profaning,  
Which I never do.  
But whatever number of years  
May be allotted to you,  
Live them full of joy,  
Free from sorrow and sadness!

Always rejoice in my friendship!  
I value yours highly too,  
So that, where time gnaws,  
It may never succumb!  
And to that we drink,  
The glass filled to the brim!  
And let us reach out to each other,  
Sincerely, the hand of friends!

label: birthday
translation:
Those born in January, stand up! repeat.  
Those born in January,  
You are welcome at this table of friends!  
Stand up, stand up, stand up!

Those who conclude their year in that month, raise your glass! repeat.  
Those who conclude their year in that month,  
Drink your glass down to the bottom.  
Drink up, drink up, drink up!

The cold month is really harsh, yes, yes! repeat.  
The cold month is really harsh,  
But the little wine also tastes good by the fire.  
Sit down, sit down, sit down!

label: birthday
translation:
Long live the King, long live the King,  
So sound voice and strings,  
May God grant him joy and prosperity,  
And many tens of years,  
Blessed be no ruler like him.  
That we sing and that we pray. (repeat)  

Hail, hail, you dawn,  
Hail from every home,  
You see no more loyal subjects,  
You find no braver King;  
Than he who may rise for the Netherlands,  
You bring no happier feast day. (repeat)  

Rise up, rise on day of prayer!  
God’s temples are opened,  
There flags and joy flags flutter,  
There the salute shots fire;  
The neighbor hears at border and beach,  
The festive cheers of the Netherlands. (repeat)  

The Belgian also thunders with bursts of regret,  
Our loud cheers in their ears,  
He grants us nothing but fearful screams,  
To their regret they must hear it:  
But we think in our song of joy,  
Not of those ungrateful fools. (repeat)  

It’s a celebration, it’s a celebration, thanks be to God, praise to God,  
We acknowledge heaven’s blessing,  
From city and village, from hut and courtyard,  
Sounds praise and thanks to Him,  
And echoes a song or flows a tear,  
God has heard, God has understood. (repeat)  

You know, oh King! what wish it is,  
That we brought to you stammering,  
No children more devoted than we,  
Than those who watch more faithfully,  
No ruler and people so closely related,  
As Nassau and the Netherlands. (repeat)  

In distress and death, in love and sorrow,  
We are bound to each other,  
The offspring keeps the fathers’ oath,  
Oh King! inviolate,  
Our goods and blood, disposed and determined,  
We give the gold and grasp the steel. (repeat)  

Let far from here the revolt flag  
Be set in the ruins of thrones,  
Give boldly and without penalty the subject,  
His King as master’s laws,  
Who with that plague would approach us,  
Here he will hit a wall of loyalty. (repeat)  

Creep into state interest in town and city,  
Along a thousand crooked paths,  
You King go straight across the sea,  
Against the spring tide with us;  
However black falsehood over there may be,  
How brightly, Netherlands! you shine. (repeat)  

The Father of the country lives, his offspring lives,  
So cheer harp and strings,  
That God may grant them joy and prosperity,  
And many tens of years,  
Unbreakable be our bond of unity,  
That sings and prays the Fatherland. (repeat)

label: birthday
translation:
Come, boys, now the unfolded flag,  
Raised from your home,  
In honor of the King.  
Again he celebrates his birthday,  
Wanting to bring him your wishes,  
To join you in the chorus,  
Long live, long live, long live the King.  
This day brings joy to all the people again,  
Now that William was given,  
Another year happily to live,  
Everyone sings and is delighted,  
On the King's annual celebration,  
It sounds in every home,  
Long live, long live, long live the King.

Let us together pray for our Sovereign,  
Now from Heaven,  
May he stay long among us,  
So it sounds from moved hearts,  
May God spare him for a long time,  
Grant him many more years,  
Long live, long live, long live the King.

label: birthday
translation:
The king lives! the king lives!  
Thus sound voice and strings,  
May God grant him joy and prosperity!  
For many tens of years;  
Blessed be no ruler like him, repeat.  
That we sing and that we pray. repeat.

Greetings, greetings you dawn,  
Greetings from every home,  
You see no braver subject,  
You find no truer king:  
You lead no happier celebration,  
Than that which rises for the Netherlands.

Rise up, rise up, day of prayer!  
God's temples are opened;  
There wave banners and flags of joy,  
There the honor shots explode!  
The neighbor hears, at border and shore,  
The festive cheers in the Netherlands.

Also the Belgian, glowing with regret, rumbles,  
Our loud cheers in his ears,  
He grants us nothing but fearful cries,  
And must hear our jubilation;  
Yet we think in our song of joy  
Not of those ungrateful fools.

It is a feast, it is a feast! God thanks, God praise!  
We acknowledge Heaven's blessing,  
From city and village, and hut and courtyard,  
Sounds praise and thanks to Him;  
And a song echoes, or a tear flows,  
God has understood it well too.

You know, O Prince, what wish it is  
That we strive to express to You,  
No children more devoted than we,  
No wave of loyalty watches more faithfully,  
No Prince and People so closely related,  
As Nassau is and the Netherlands.

In distress and death, in love and sorrow,  
We are bound to each other;  
The offspring keeps the Fathers' oath,  
O King! intact.  
Our goods, our blood... command, decide,  
We give the gold, and grasp the steel.

Let far from here a rebellion rise  
Set in the shadows of thrones,  
Grant boldly and without punishment the subject,  
His Prince as master of laws;  
Who with that plague would approach us,  
Here he strikes upon a wall of loyalty.

Crawl, state interest, in war and peace,  
Along a thousand crooked roads,  
You, King! you go straight through seas  
Against the spring tide with us;  
However darker falsehood may be there,  
Brighter, Netherlands! you shine.

The country's Father lives, the country's Mother lives!  
So cheer heart and strings,  
May God grant them joy and prosperity  
For many tens of years;  
Unbreakable be our bond of unity:  
That sings and prays the fatherland.

label: birthday
translation:
1. On the day of your birth,  
we follow you with our song! Freely may you stand before us,  
whether it offers thankful joy to you.  

2. What we wish for you in prayer,  
may it be good and trusted by God! He never feels deceived,  
who builds on this Rock.  

3. May blessings be scattered over you from among us,  
richly to the children! That the Lord may protect you on our prayers  
with his angelic guard!  

4. Taste in your heart heavenly peace, and spread happiness around!  
May God fulfill this blessing prayer  
to the end of your life!  

5. Yes, when the earth covers your dust,  
may your spirit continue to bless,  
and may you awaken thanks in us until then,  
even though you live in a happier place!

label: birthday
translation:
1. Long shall he live (it can also be she)  
Long shall he live  
Long shall he live in glory  
In glory  2x

2. Blessed shall he die  
Blessed shall he die  
Blessed shall he die in glory  
In glory  2x

3. Heaven shall he inherit  
Heaven shall he inherit  
Heaven shall he inherit in glory  
In glory  2x

4. High shall he sit  
High shall he sit  
High shall he sit in glory  
In glory  2x

5. Mary shall crown him  
Mary shall crown him  
Mary shall crown him in glory  
In glory  2x

6. We all shall be there  
We all shall be there  
We all shall be there in glory  
In glory  2x

label: children
translation:
1.  
Did Cupido strike again?  
Tell me, Beuling, isn’t it dough?  
Or are your little wings clipped,  
Is your little cook pot empty again?  
Or are your arrows without tips,  
Hey, tell me, disconsolate child,  
Or is your Love-sick Mother  
Blowing with Mars in the wind again?  

2.  
Or is your bowstring weak,  
Is the little string broken again?  
I see your torch isn’t burning:  
Oh yes, you are full of sorrow!  
The fire of love has gone out,  
The god of love is completely unsettled,  
Cupido is robbed of his weapon,  
Who does not mourn this hero?  

3.  
But little dwarf, do not cry like that,  
Be brave, my best mate:  
Hey, spare your tears of sorrow,  
Come, come, I still have advice,  
Light your torches from the flame,  
That Phillis has kindled,  
From the fire that came from her little eyes,  
And still smokes in my heart.  

4.  
Do you still lack arrows and bow?  
Borrow them from the light,  
From my goddess’s radiant eye:  
Every glance is an arrow,  
That surely hits, what more do you want!  
Thus, you get into posture,  
The whole world will burn again,  
Through the fire you have stolen.

label: children
translation:
1.  
As long as I must lose Amaril,  
So must I die:  
As long as I must lose Amaril,  
So must I go;  
Ah! if only I could gain comfort,  
For my lamenting and misery.  

2.  
What last moved my soul,  
With tears in the eyes,  
What last moved my soul,  
Under heavy pressure;  
You alas! without mercy,  
Give another my happiness,  

3.  
The first thing that made mine dawn,  
Was your glances,  
The first thing that made mine dawn,  
Was your face,  
That with sparkling sparks,  
Has kindled a fire in my heart.  

4.  
Can I then not break your heart,  
By sighing and pleading?  
Can I then not break your heart,  
My little angel,  
Give my hope of love a sign,  
That at the end I shall prevail.

label: children
translation:
1.

Point of sorrow in our life,  
By young years adds much joy,  
Banish melancholy,  
For being sad does not suit youth.  

2.  
What can one do in old age,  
Then one is stiff in throat and foot,  
Without kindness nor tenderness,  
Where many a person must sigh.  

3.  
Let us avoid our pride,  
I then walk with the beggar's sack,  
For frankly in poetry,  
That sometimes causes much discomfort.  

4.  
So let us sing at the birth,  
For those who celebrate their birthday today,  
Never change in kindness,  
And also consider them worthy of it.  

5.  
May we always see the sky favorable,  
I wish what you may wish,  
Who in mood and good delight,  
A gem of the Amstel youth.

label: children
translation:
1.  
Three men went to the Orient,  
Is it not a wondrous thing,  
Our Deacon chews currants,  
Very wonderfully sweet in taste,  
The thief has gone running,  
They tie him to the mast,  
With fifteen dozen knots:  
Is that not fitting well.

2.  
We are three great fellows,  
We come from far away,  
A wagon with four wheels,  
The oldest bears the star.  
The youngest of the three,  
Is a bit dark,  
And the one standing there beside him,  
Lives near the market.

3.  
We came before Herod,  
It was in the evening dusk,  
His wife was in a good mood,  
She gave each of us a flag,  
And then it got so loud,  
What’s on my mind,  
The star is beautiful, the star was joyful,  
There is a candle burning.

4.  
The star would not go further,  
And Jappe struck his wife,  
And Melse wore a patterned coat,  
And Balte turned so stiff,  
Then we came to a bakery,  
And nothing else at all,  
There we stole two or three loaves of bread,  
And the stars were made of paper.

5.  
Two rascals like a mustard,  
Hard as a stone,  
Salted meat with mustard,  
Is that not suitable?  
My lord, how much would you like,  
It shines just like the sun.  
And Coridon the herdsman,  
And the herdsman Coridon.

6.  
The rooster stands on the tower,  
And the smoke flies out of the chimney;  
My money I have lost,  
And I fear my wife,  
And Rhine wine with sauerkraut,  
In a soiled pair of trousers,  
The moon shines true red,  
Just like that gingerbread.

label: children
translation:
1.  
I sat last time by a tree,  
By a crystalline stream,  
Seated on the banks,  
There, with sorrowful sound,  
I poured out my anxious complaints.  
Have I wronged Cloris in any way?

2.  
Tell me, leafy tree,  
Cheerful brook that flows here,  
Fish that float in the water,  
Birds that warble here,  
And swing from branch to twig,  
Have I wronged Cloris in any way?

3.  
Then tell the white-woolly cattle,  
How I here at this place,  
Many times gave Cloris kisses,  
In the clover-rich grass,  
When she was still kind to me,  
Have I wronged her in any way?

4.  
Cloris dear, do you not recall,  
What once did happen:  
When I was allowed to live in your favor,  
Did you not swear that you would  
Never break your vow,  
Have I wronged you in any way?

5.  
Ah! what can be the reason,  
That I, so full of love’s pain,  
Must tremble at your sullen look?  
That I complain day and night,  
And question myself,  
Have I wronged Cloris in any way?

6.  
Cloris, who unexpectedly,  
Silently listened to his complaint,  
Cried out: ah shepherd, spare your life,  
I love, I love you alone,  
You are mine. No, oh no,  
Cloris, you have wronged nothing.

label: children
translation:
1.  
What joy, what joy,  
O reckless youth!  
Do you really engage in love?  
Your sweetness soon turns to soot,  
If you want to enjoy splendid blood,  
Win it before you regret it.  

2.  
Trouble and sorrow, trouble and sorrow,  
Felt by your youthful heart,  
As soon as you get married;  
The fiddler is close to the roof,  
Your freedom immediately cracks,  
So beware of women.  

3.  
Indoors, indoors,  
You encounter the most hardship,  
You must constantly be a servant:  
Like a donkey, horse, or goat,  
Or you feel daily with a stick,  
Your entire body suffering.  

4.  
Every day, every day,  
You must with deep reverence,  
Follow her commands:  
For if even one hair is out of place,  
The pig immediately bites you,  
And yells at you in loud voices.  

5.  
When you are a child, when you are a child,  
Overcome by her,  
You must constantly be rocked,  
Like a fool or simpleton,  
Does that suit a free-born man?  
Don’t let yourself be deceived like that.  

6.  
Whatever you do, whatever you do,  
It is never good or right,  
You must be called a lazybones,  
And the proverb is all too true,  
“A man must every day of the year,  
Eat what his wife likes.”  

7.  
Most sorrow, most sorrow,  
What one often sees,  
(I barely dare reveal it)  
Is that another joyfully picks,  
The flowers of her early youth,  
With which they crown your hair.  

8.  
Therefore beware, therefore beware,  
Of the female race,  
Never marry,  
So you live freed from this plague,  
In freedom and in happiness,  
Which you will never regret.

label: children
translation:
1.  
For a long time already,  
Tryn, that chubby girl,  
My word, my word,  
That I alone possessed her heart,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
But Tryn is deceiving me somewhat.

2.  
It's all just false talk,  
Because last night late,  
On the street, on the street,  
I found her standing right by Krelis,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
That’s where the game began.

3.  
I got very angry and jealous,  
He grabbed her by the neck,  
Fair and tender, fair and tender,  
She seemed to look at him so green,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
And he gave her kiss after kiss.

4.  
I thought that was not right,  
I beat the rascal Tryn,  
The dirty swine, the dirty swine,  
While she rents out her lover for money,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
I had nicely spied on that.

5.  
Now I am free once again,  
From the slavery of love,  
In the affair, in the affair,  
It always starts over again,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
No one has ever done that.

6.  
The Seine, and the Rhine,  
Provide me medicine,  
For my pain, for my pain,  
I calmly smoke a pipe of tobacco,  
Do you understand me well,  
Do you understand me well,  
Then I feel more ease.

label: children
translation:
1.  
Gun my dog my dear Dorinde,  
A little kiss from your mouth,  
So that I may find through it,  
Tala rela rela dira...  
Help for my love wound.  

2.  
Whether the young men think,  
That one must be kissed,  
Has their pain ever truly disappeared,  
Tala rela rela dira...  
One kiss does not extinguish flames.  

3.  
Hey my Dorinde, it can’t hurt,  
That you bite your little mouth on me,  
That little fountain of grace,  
Tala rela rela dira...  
Always springs and never lessens.  

4.  
Well now Titer, hold your fun,  
But treat me gently,  
If you make it too rough with kisses,  
Tala rela rela dira...  
Then you will never get any more.

label: children
translation:
Come, let us sing of the Lord;  
That KING! and of His glory!  
Whom thousands steadily surround,  
Who is worthy that His Host,  
His possession, stand there,  
And sing new songs before HIM!

label: children
translation:
God has so impressed Himself in His works,  
That He is clearly recognized in everything.  
They all bear signs and marks,  
For all His works speak of their Maker,  
Although no one grasps it, yet sees and hears it,  
Unless God Himself makes it understood.  
Yes, it is in vain, what the mind imagines,  
When God Himself hides from man.

label: children
translation:
Heaven, Earth, and Sea  
Speak of their LORD  
You, oh Soul, along with them:  
Think of Your God!  
Let everything in you  
Turn to His glory!  
Pray to HIM,  
Even with closed eyes;  
Remain standing respectfully,  
Before His Majesty,  
In humility,  
Childishly bowed!  
With that holy host,  
Also seeing from there,  
The homeland from afar!

label: children
translation:
The great Creator Almighty  
Did what was not there before:  
And, what is now visible to the eyes:  
Came forth quickly at His word,  
Heaven and Earth!  
There everything was gathered  
Above and below;  
When the WORD spoke to them,  
When the SPIRIT separated them,  
Each went to its place.  
O wise Maker, who set the poles  
That mark all the boundaries of this sphere,  
All the heights, all the valleys,  
Every hour, every moment,  
Thus would limit!  
Let our heart always notice,  
How nothing happens by chance,  
But according to eternal Wisdom,  
Which leads all to its praise,  
Comes, or shall come!

label: children
translation:
SOo whoever does not see the Creator,  
Only looks at the creatures:  
If one does not know God through faith,  
One clings to appearances:  
But when the LORD, in grace, inclines,  
He enlightens soul and senses;  
That Divine sun makes  
The heart flame from within.  
O Highest GOOD, so agreeable!  
You alone are desirable!  
And Your worshipful Name  
Is wondrous and glorious!  
That, besides You, all bores me!  
That I seek not to live,  
Except to give myself, forever, entirely  
To Your service!

label: children
translation:
What goodness does not share so much beauty!  
And does it not give its servants reward!  
Even here on this earthly ground?  
What else would it mean, after this time and place,  
Not to be, in the high heavenly realm,  
That blessed upper city!  
How lovely there! There, undisturbed,  
Its praise is heard in unity,  
And Hallelujahs resound!  
Make me steadfast, good, and pious:  
So that I, in that Olive tree,  
In JESUS CHRIST! remain!  
And thus for your honor and glory  
In Eden, like a beautiful flower,  
Planted, remain forever!

label: children
translation:
Merciful God! and LORD above all!  
Who, according to Your merciful pleasure,  
Have willed to take me into Your Son;  
And, before I myself was born,  
Have mercifully chosen me as heir,  
How should I not stand in deep humility!  
Yet, it was Your Eye that fixed itself upon me,  
And recalled me from my wandering,  
You gave me a different spirit and mind!  
Thus You came to snatch me from Satan’s nets,  
You made me bow down before Your Feet,  
You led me into Your school!

label: children
translation:
What do I bring You, compassionate BEING!
Who has healed my sickness,
Yes, Who has brought me out of the power of hell
To Your blessed light!
At last, through Your omnipotence,
My soul was as if extracted
From Satan’s deceit and desire for sin
And that I tasted a quiet rest!
Given to Your service!
Disturb even that ease,
Which still comes from self-will!

label: children
translation:
Certainly true is before the sight,  
The dreadful and penetrating lightning light,  
And the clattering of thunder in the clouds!  
When this is heard on Earth among the peoples,  
Man thinks with reverence of his God;  
The sinner then fears a sorrowful fate,  
Now he thinks that perhaps one of these strikes  
Will soon bring him before God's judgment.  
Often too, all thundering voices,  
Which rightly oppress the heart of the sinner:  
There sometimes shoots a lightning bolt from his word  
So deep into the heart, that it pierces it completely,  
To first overcome it with pain,  
And then to heal it into a better state.  
Consume in them the foolish and sinful self,  
So that all that is within them may incline  
To Your Name, to Your glory,  
To praise now, and forevermore!  
This belongs to You, on Earth, and above;  
May all that has breath praise the LORD!

label: children
translation:
Though it is granted to him,  
Who may enter  
Into the inner chambers;  
And in God’s Temple behold  
The ornament of the Sanctuary,  
The Strength of the Kingdom,  
And put his trust in His Name!  
Mercy is there the portion of the soul,  
Mercy completely stills the heart;  
It is like food from above:  
When one is brought into the Sanctuary,  
The fainted heart will again  
Joyfully praise the Lord.  
O that my Soul then indeed,  
With David, at the break of dawn,  
Through deep longing of the heart,  
Would awake from sluggishness and sorrow,  
And rightly bow to God in humility,  
To ever cling to HIM!  
I am a poor powerless child!  
A leaf driven by the wind,  
Unless You spare me;  
A turtledove in the cleft!  
I acknowledge the smallness of the faith,  
That dwells in my heart.  
My flesh (the hut in which I live)  
That formerly had a vain beauty,  
Hardly wants to carry me;  
Unless the Soul, strengthened by hope,  
And worked upon by Your life-power,  
Sets me as if on a chariot!  
And therefore, O my GOD and Lord,  
She turns only to You,  
As to her Soul’s beloved!  
Your Love, however, sets measure and time,  
That no one experiences too much suffering,  
But finds rest again!

label: children
translation:
What a great Mystery it is! that everything stands still for it!  
What depth, without foundation! if one were to sound it here!  
God's SON (the Man of His Counsel) comes down to us!  
HE, who dwells in the light, comes into the darkness!  
HE, before whose high Throne the blessed spirits swirl,  
Descends down into a stable of senseless animals!  
HE, who with high Authority shall guard Heathendom,  
Must, oh wondrous thing! first descend, and humble himself,  
To bear witness of denial and scorn!  
Thus the greatest LORD is now himself a lowliest Servant!  
HE, to whom Justice belongs, here pays Justice himself!  
HE, who must strengthen Kings on their Throne,  
Will here suffer himself to be written down as a subject!  
Bethlehem Ephrath, so much despised from outside,  
Causes this ROOT to sprout from its barren earth!  
Then one sees how HE became such a rejection,  
For poor Adam’s child, (unworthy of anything)  
To re-admit into the assembly of Heaven;  
And thus to clear the dark night for them;  
That those who swarm like a nest of all evil  
Might by HIM be purified, so that they may please GOD!

label: children
translation:
If the spotless LAMB were to step to the slaughter,
First would come a pure Incense of Prayers,
From His hand, before GOD, and His Throne:
Although one heard choirs of all Angels,
Never came incense from those Spices,
Like THIS prayer, THIS incense of God's Son!
For us bound, mocked, and sorely beaten,
Receive the praise for Your labor's Reward!

label: children
translation:
Holy Jesus! Image of clarity!  
Sanctified for Your people,  
Also sanctify in the truth,  
Give to the cloud of Witnesses,  
That still walks on earth  
And stands for Your Name,  
That they faithfully avoid evil,  
And fight as Your servants.  
That they may still gather a people,  
That, in true holiness,  
Set Your exemplary conduct  
Before themselves every day,  
And demonstrate with their deeds,  
That they remain in Your ways!

label: children
translation:
Once your heart longs to sing,  
Of yetzs, in which one sweetly rests;  
Then let it sing of the union  
Of the souls with the Bridegroom;  
His blessed Power; His Kingdom;  
And what His Saints experience!  
Oh! how much He did all that time,  
With watchfulness, prayer, diligence, and struggle,  
Until the hour of death came;  
And then still through a bitter death,  
Delivering His people from their distress,  
He caused a full blessing to flow!  
The humble, and, in the eye, insignificant,  
Understand it more through experience,  
Than through much teaching-learning:  
It belongs to those hidden things!  
For see, how highly He places her rank!  
He places her at His Right Hand;  
He Himself crowns her with a crown;  
He names her after His own Name;  
He makes her capable through His Spirit,  
So that she may live with HIM eternally!  
Yes, hallowed, drunk with THAT Love?  
Who has raised her out of a pit of misery,  
To that honor seat!  
Was ever anything so great bestowed?  
The day on which one shall hear  
That joyful, that beautiful ringing,  
That the LAMB’s Marriage has come!  
His Wife is now wholly ready,  
And stands in the pure white garment;  
There is then the Blessed feast of the Virgins!

label: children
translation:
Once your heart has the desire to sing,  
Of something in which one lovingly rests;  
Then let it sing, of the union  
Of the souls with the Bridegroom;  
His blessed power; His kingdom;  
And what His saints experience!  
Oh! How much He did all that time,  
With watching, praying, diligence, and struggle,  
Until the hour of death had come;  
And then still, through a bitter death,  
Delivering His people from their distress,  
He caused a full blessing to flow!  
The simple, and, in the eyes, the lowly,  
Understand it through experience more,  
Than through much teaching and learning:  
It belongs to those hidden things!  
For see, how highly He esteems her position!  
He places her at His right hand;  
He Himself crowns her with a crown;  
He calls her by His own Name;  
He makes her capable through His Spirit,  
So that she may dwell eternally with Him!  
Yes, holy drunk from THAT love?  
She has been raised, from a pit of misery,  
To that seat of honor!  
Has anything ever been given so great?  
That day, on which one shall hear  
That joyful, that beautiful sound,  
Of the LAMB’s wedding has come!  
His wife is now completely ready,  
And stands in the pure white garment;  
There is then the blessed feast of the Virgins!

label: children
translation:
How precious is that place, O LORD!  
There Your Name, there Your Honor,  
The subject of song is continual,  
And nothing is heard but Your Praise!  
The voices of the multitude flow sweetly,  
Who stand with the Lamb on Zion;  
There one says, with equal voice,  
GOD be the glory, praise, forever, HIM!

label: children
translation:
How precious is that pure BLOOD!  
Of the LAMB, who was slaughtered for us,  
His Blood has willingly flowed,  
So that the soul, defiled and wicked,  
Might receive salvation from eternal death,  
Through God's grace!  
Happy then, is he who has felt  
What this precious blood intends!  
The cleansing from stripes and wounds:  
That soul experienced thereby,  
How it is freely acquitted  
From the deadly consequence of sins!  
Let the balm of your wounds  
Be our medicine for so long,  
Until we are found completely purified  
From the venom of death!

label: children
translation:
Living LORD! full of grace,  
No souls do You reject,  
Those fainthearted, or burdened,  
Who come to You in distress!  
Though faith, in which one stands,  
Is like a mustard seed,  
Yet it cannot prevent  
That one could move mountains.  
Look with fatherly mercy,  
If I were to waver and go astray!  
Support me, as with eternal arms,  
If I were to stand discouraged!  
Let no tongue condemn me!  
Let the enemy not boast either,  
That He could bring down one  
Who placed his hope in God!

label: children
translation:
Dearest JESUS, most beloved!  
That I may find You Yourself!  
I feel that my heart  
Is still seeking You, with pain!  
You are the SOUL-SUN!  
The only delight and LIFE'S-FOUNTAIN!  
Do not hide too much from me!  
My soul is fainting, O LORD!

label: children
translation:
O JESUS! Truth, Way, and Life,  
For those who turn to God;  
Ah, be so revealed to me!  
So that I may give up everything,  
Yet You possess wholly and entirely!  
To You, the SOUL'S SHEPHERD,  
Bring those who are on the way ever closer,  
Until they become one with You!  
Illuminated by You, the Son of Life!  
Then darkness and harshness flee,  
Just as when the day began.  
So that I shall not fall again  
Into false paths down below;  
And perish with a great number!

label: children
translation:
Happy are those who find the Lord:  
Fleeing the earthly noise!  
Those who receive JESUS, as the soul's beloved,  
Into their house;  
Those who, in the days of grace,  
Confirm the Covenant!  
And, sincerely, to His question  
Say yes with their mouth!  
Ah! great SHEPHERD of the soul,  
Who is always the FIRST!  
O! that it may also please You,  
In this time of finding,  
To so awaken the hearts of many,  
That in humility,  
They may be perfected by Your drawing,  
And led to You!

label: children
translation:
Glorified HEAD! who now, in Heaven's dwelling,  
Sits full of honor! Anointed as Sight-King,  
Oh let my heart be for You, at all times!  
And that You would grant me the anointing  
Of Your Spirit, so that my entire life,  
May be dedicated to You and Your service!  
Help (faithful SAVIOR) all Your weak members;  
Soon put an end to the enemy's power,  
And cast them down beneath our feet!  
Take, by Your power, completely from within,  
Whatever is unclean, or vain in the senses;  
Thus we may, through Your goodness, become good!  
Let me also, for You, humbly confess  
Those whom I sometimes hear say,  
That I pray for Your good for them!  
I present them before Your Feet in every way!  
Grant to those who are sincere a blessing  
That they lack nothing of what they need.  
Then finally let us with all the pious,  
Together come up yonder into the Air;  
So that we, pure, yes spotless,  
May one day stand steadfast before Your throne!  
And, wholly renewed inwardly by Your Spirit,  
Remain perfectly united forever!

label: children
translation:
How deeply I long, God and King!  
That once Your Spirit may dwell in me,  
May establish, to honor Your name!  
And I may be a living manifestation  
Of Christ, my Lord!  
What light, what help, and new strength  
Must I receive to strive  
To follow the footsteps my Shepherd went!  
Teach me to constantly watch for Him,  
For otherwise I cannot do a single thing!  
And not again to destruction!  
For this must also serve as a sign  
That God is perfect in love!

label: children
translation:
O Jesus! great Lord Supreme,  
Who offered help to so many!  
Turn Your ear to me,  
I want to complain about my distress!  
Sometimes I sit as if bound,  
When I am yearning for freedom:  
I get distracted by my own thoughts,  
Instead of turning to You!  
Take me completely as Your own:  
Let Your pleasure rule me!  
Let Your power incline everything in me,  
That I may fall down before Your feet!

label: children
translation:
O JESUS! who have fed such great crowds  
With Miracle-bread;  
How Blessed! is he who may still experience,  
That YOU are the helper in distress!  
Many a soul has truly felt,  
How it lacks the true BREAD;  
And therefore always with earnest intention,  
To be saved from that distress!  
MOSES already pointed to You,  
When the people were fed by the Manna:  
From DAVID, it is read of You,  
That YOU share Your Goodness with the poor.  
Merciful JESUS! once again turn Your eyes  
In compassion!  
Behold that deep inability,  
Of such a great throng of people!

label: children
translation:
O Silent LAMB! gentle Being!
When will I be so accustomed,
That one can read from my face,
That your Image is imprinted on me!
YOU, who once tamed the stormy waves
Of the raging sea well;
Could also make the soul experience,
That You hold everything by Your Power!

label: children
translation:
Bow (Zion!) down before the LAMB:
And give honor to Your KING!
It belongs to HIM from all peoples;
Yes, from the blessed choir of angels:
That applies to us also in this time;
His love will also command it of you.
But, (oh) it often embarrasses us,
That sometimes something, as if made of steel,
Has not fully bowed, and quietly:
Forgive, merciful SUPREME LORD!
And quickly cast down that enemy,
So that opposition may be silent!

label: children
translation:
MYRRH is valued for its use and worth;  
Then it purifies us from the corruption of the Earth:  
But JESUS has Immortality,  
And a power of Incorruptibility;  
He who once died now lives on,  
To cause us to escape destruction.  
So that Death does not harm us!  
Nor eternal deathly stench makes us loathsome;  
But that we, like a holy Priesthood,  
May present ourselves before You!  
Even if sometimes we stand faint and helpless;  
So that we do not perish completely:  
Oh! that one may seek that noble Balm’s fragrance,  
That holy Anointing, in us!

label: children
translation:
Dearest JESUS! in the time  
Of your humble existence,  
One once heard You confess,  
Power has gone out from me:  
Oh, if only that power could still flow!  
If that stream could pour down;  
Now YOU sit in glory,  
On the Throne of Majesty!  
Indeed, all power  
Has been entrusted into your hands;  
And, to the ends of the earth,  
Your Name is highly esteemed:  
Nations are already witnesses;  
All that is high must bow;  
Even the last enemy must  
Finally be under Your foot!

label: children
translation:
CHRISTIANS walk here below,
Not as citizens of this Earth:
CHRISTIANS are content,
That another's treasure they seek;
Their longing, and soul's desire
Is to inherit that good,
Which, invisible to man,
Causes loss of other things.
Well, my MASTER, and my LORD!
I pray You, give me an earnest eye!
That what seems good to me does not harm me,
And that I thus do not deceive myself!
That I am ready to sell all my goods,
Myself with joy, to go;
Yes, (even if it must cost my blood)
Willingly may I go there!

label: children
translation:
Lovable beautiful IMMANUEL!  
The Glory of Israel!  
And the Light for blind Heathendom!  
O shine soon, with Your radiance everywhere!  
Lord JESUS! great WONDERSON!  
Previously descended from the Father's throne;  
Descend again in spirit as to us,  
And draw our hearts back to You!

label: children
translation:
O blessed Bread! that gives us life,  
Oh share Yourself with us, food within!  
So that the heart, living by grace,  
May gain further growth through You!  
Hope also becomes ever strong in us!  
May the Peace of God preserve our heart and mind!  
How safe it was! how beautifully I was received!  
How gratefully my lips would praise HIM!

label: children
translation:
So nourish us then, O Heavenly Bread!  
And Drink! given by God!  
Your power frees us from death!  
And makes us live for You!  
Clothe us in Your Righteousness!  
So that we may walk in white,  
And through renewed purity,  
Act according to Your will!  
We also willingly bear ourselves together now, O KING!  
May our heart be, and remain before You  
A place of dwelling!  
There is the hand! the heart! the eye!  
It surrenders everything here!  
Send YOU the powers from above,  
That forever make us live!

label: children
translation:
O JESUS! origin of our life!  
Who with Your power has created us;  
You, like the VINE, come to give us  
That which makes us fruitful further;  
That we act faithfully,  
That we walk worthily,  
The time of grace!  
Let us live for You,  
Let us cling to You,  
Who is the ROOT!

label: children
translation:
How blessed are all those who are Yours, Lord!  
Though wondrous indeed!  
How could You ever do otherwise, one might think,  
When Your faithfulness remains constant?  
If the path seems crooked to us, it is nevertheless right;  
It is precisely the way to go.  
Though our reason sometimes stands in doubt,  
All is wisely resolved by the outcome.  
What wickedness it would be if reason were to speak against it,  
Or shake its head at Your way!  
Ah! If this were to happen again, You would break it,  
So that pride quickly lays itself low!  
I do not wish for my mind to be judge;  
Break in me what still seeks itself by Your power!  
All that once was condemned to mastery!  
O that Your will be done, not mine!

label: children
translation:
Beloved beauty is the LORD's will,  
Yes, even a refuge in times of need;  
How safe it is there! how quiet!  
I then lay myself in that LAP!  
Reason, and sense rightly hate discipline,  
And self-life murmurs;  
Inwardly I am nevertheless granted,  
And see how self is consumed.  
Ah! help me kill self-life;  
That bitter power of flesh and blood!  
That I may wholly surrender myself,  
Your WILL alone must be done!

label: children
translation:
The Lord's way is a wonder with His people!  
It sometimes seems as if He lets it all go;  
Or that a dark cloud has come between,  
So that one can hardly perceive Him;  
But see! when He seems to be the farthest away,  
He suddenly comes to make Himself known.  
Why do you struggle against wind and current?  
You must rely on My doing.

label: children
translation:
Ah! The soul was entrusted to GOD!  
How it almost found an end  
To the sorrowful wandering!  
Ah! It took courage in faith;  
And fled to its protector,  
In His loving embrace!  
David fled in his fearful time;  
(Pursued by Saul's godless envy)  
To this SHELTER;  
O soul! follow this footprint,  
So that it may also go well with you,  
And stop weeping.  
My GOD, (who forms the spirit)  
Once make my soul rightly turned:  
So that it may rest in You!  
I acknowledge, this is also Your gift;  
And pray, that You refresh me with Your goodness,  
Through springs of delight!

label: children
translation:
GY (great PRINCE of the CHIEFS)  
Who once led Your People,  
In the midst of the great waves,  
Have also consecrated JACOB’S inheritance:  
The Love, which formerly descended  
Into our low Humanity,  
Bring Your favor to us again,  
As if Heaven’s bow still appeared!

label: children
translation:
Give yourself (my soul) completely quietly,
In GOD'S well-pleasure,
And blindly seek the Lord
To offer as a sacrifice!

Discipline consumes the sinful:
The fire causes much burning;
Evil does not harm the soul,
That places itself in GOD'S hands.
It is only a short time,
Before we attain the prize,
And after the ended struggle,
To eternally bask in blessing!

label: children
translation:
JESUS! O my soul's life!  
To whom I want to give myself in service,  
Support my weak effort!  
Set me once truly free.  
Make me thoroughly righteous in good,  
Completely unable in evil,  
Full of Love, quiet, and gentle,  
Shining in a crooked lineage!  
Let Your Spirit strengthen me so,  
That in stillness and in action,  
I may nevertheless distinguish Your WILL  
From my own desires and nature;  
Become yet without You conceived,  
And begun from Flesh and Blood,  
Grant that I never again  
Hinder Your Work or Will.

label: children
translation:
Oh Lord! strengthen the weak faith,
Where Satan has plotted,
That he may steal it from us once more!
For without You there is no power!

label: children
translation:
How precious is such a lot,  
Whose walk may be with GOD!  
Who constantly experiences His closeness;  
How lovely is the place above,  
Where one hears the Lord's voice;  
There the saints gather together;  
The Temple of God in the Spirit,  
Where one praises and fears His Name!  
Ah LORD, God of hosts!  
To whom all the created belongs,  
To whom the angels serve at His word!  
May my soul truly experience  
How precious this place is!  
Ah LORD, LORD, think of me!  
And grant strength against weariness!  
One who may rest here  
Can endure storms and tempest,  
He never fears any enemy:  
The LORD of the HOSTS  
Hides the souls in danger.

label: children
translation:
How blessed is a child of man,  
Who finds strength in the LORD;  
And can rely on His Power:  
Whatever needs and hardships  
May befall him here below,  
He finds himself calm, resigned to GOD.  
The LORD knows what is best;  
The soul relies on His word.  
A soul that rightly considers this,  
Is never completely harmed;  
What harms others can help itself,  
It feels, as if, no lack,  
Because the Lord is with it!

label: children
translation:
When at last, with joyful sound,  
The tidings shall once more be found,  
That GOD is granting liberation;  
When Babel shall fall to pieces,  
And the yoke of oppression ceases,  
What joy then will be experienced!  
How long, LORD, how long!  
Does the poor people sit in Babel’s throng?  
Deliver them once from their chains!  
It is for Your power an easy case;  
Oh, that it may once come to pass,  
That You send the Spirit to the lands!

label: children
translation:
Ah, how dark does one find the Earth?  
By the splendor of Heaven!  
The world's beauty holds no value,  
When Heaven's radiance spreads:  
It was wondrously beautiful on Tabor!  
Saints shone there in their dwelling!  
Oh, how lovely that sight!  
If only one could build a hut there!  
Meanwhile, remain, O LORD!  
In grace with your people!  
That your love may honor us here,  
With those voices from the cloud,  
Who from above the throne,  
Declare you to be GOD'S SON;  
And, with divine rays,  
Descend into the hearts!

label: children
translation:
Consider, O Soul! consider the wonders,  
The miracles of that exceedingly beautiful City;  
That CITY, which never has experienced its downfall;  
It is worthy that Zion's ornament shall be adorned!  
What privilege! honor! beauty! good gifts!  
Receive them then, O Zion, from your Lord!  
For this favor, your place is so highly praised!  
How streams flow in you for refreshment!  
Yes, more than that, scarcely any peoples are there,  
Or one begins to see a dawn;  
They begin to offer tribute  
To Him who swiftly travels on a cloud.  
O beautiful DAY! the Day of delight,  
The Festival Day of Jesus and His BRIDE,  
The Day of the covenant, on which the Hero sets out,  
Riding prosperously on a chariot of blessing.

label: children
translation:
O Holy LAMB! we bow down,
Before You, as our only Lord,
For YOU are worthy to break open,
The Book, sealed within,
The promised good, and the threatening evil,
Wherewith YOU shall avenge the wicked!

label: children
translation:
Come then, O Soul-BRIDEGROOM!  
Come LORD with Your Kingdom!  
And establish Your Realm here below!  
All who hear it, say Come quickly!  
That all may hasten to You on foot!  
And Your Name be confessed!  
This word is truly lovely,  
I come, I come quickly,  
We say AMEN in trust!  
And call out (as the SPIRIT and BRIDE)  
With heart and mouth, to You,  
Yes LORD! That we may soon behold You!

label: children
translation:
My soul now thinks of the secret fate  
Of HENOCH'S walk with GOD.  
Oh! that surpasses everything,  
That anyone in the World ever gave!  
Her food here is mere chaff,  
That walk is alone to be praised!  
Ah, that my heart may be entirely kindled,  
By everything that You truly mean,  
Never follow the flow of vain people;  
But always cling behind You,  
Willing to go to no place,  
Except where Your voice shall command me!

label: children
translation:
Happy, those who wander,
Who live in the world,
But lose Heaven's Peace,
Give themselves rest and quiet;
And in holy solitude,
Respectfully wait for GOD!
Blessed is he, who by waterways,
Or in a quiet place,
May pour out his heart when it is full,
Becoming tender before the LORD;
Yes, relieve his spirit,
In a warm flood of tears!

label: children
translation:
Does not a Christian strive  
To live the life that suits a Christian?  
And carefully watch himself,  
Lest he be burdened with that weight,  
Of vanities! which so many  
With deceit (yes, even under the guise of good)  
Steal the senses and the heart,  
And cause them to flow away like a flood?  
Come, let us with firm steps,  
Hasten! always forward, on!  
Forgetting what has passed;  
A little more! then it is done:  
Now it is our post to watch;  
Watch and pray, was the Savior’s counsel  
That you should not fall into temptation,  
And see your weakness too late!  
How then shall those weak tongues,  
That here, by fickle fate,  
Sometimes wept, sometimes sang,  
Full of joyful praise to God!  
And once with that company of the flock,  
That is with the Lamb above,  
Perfectly united together:  
O precious treasure and inheritance!

label: children
translation:
Sit Jesus, oh JESUS! Yourself have become the DOOR  
To the heavenly city!  
Ah, that it may happen to us through You,  
To come to such a great treasure!  
That we may also soon turn to You,  
So poor, so bare we may be;  
With our sins, our miseries,  
With our sorrows, our pain!

label: children
translation:
Is not a humble heart, bowed in deep humility  
Before the LORD of all Lords,  
Fit to honor HIM most highly?  
Well! Then fall before HIM, O Man, as such.  
Oh, he said with constant lament,  
How bold Reason (if one lets it have free rein)  
Makes man equal to GOD like the beasts,  
And stand like a mountain before the Lord’s presence!

label: children
translation:
O LORD, you chose a little Child,  
While men quarrelled over an outside;  
Oh, if only I were like this Child!  
Who did not cling to high or rich,  
But made room where You, oh Lord,  
Placed it before You!  
May no earthly turmoil hinder me!  
But set me down at Your Feet,  
My Soul hears You in the silence!  
Give me there an open ear:  
Maria chose this better part,  
For Martha still worried too much!

label: children
translation:
To love CHRIST, to seek CHRIST,  
Surpasses knowledge,  
Which is gathered from many books,  
Nothing has of the Life’s Sap;  
The Sap, that alone makes the spirit  
Joyful, and unhindered:  
Well then; to consider Love,  
One must regard above all.  
What is to be done, what to be left undone,  
One then learns in abundance:  
Yes, one finds from day to day,  
What Love is not capable of;  
For it wills, that works and words  
Are like the cords woven together.  
Pure, graceful, full of clarity,  
Resistant even to Death and Hell themselves!  
GOD of love! I now wish,  
That I may increasingly show  
This beautiful radiance,  
This ornament, this crown!

label: children
translation:
Oh that I may be truly Yours, my LORD!  
To walk in Your way, to learn according to truth!  
It is a complete mystery to reason:  
Who understands rightly, except those to whom YOU wish to reveal it?  
And to whom You want to extend Your hands!  
Ah! then make me truly convinced of that truth.  
How blessed is the fate of those  
Who unite themselves with You through faith;  
How Your Communion gives everything according to desire!  
Let me obediently follow Your will!  
In holiness, sincerely, always walk:  
Once I have paid homage to You;  
Oh that I may pay my duty,  
And also tell Your Power to others,  
So that they may pray to You as their King!

label: children
translation:
A true Christian soul's adornment,  
In which He stands for his Lord,  
Is by no means external clothing,  
Or nobility, status, human honor:  
Oh no! It is a Hidden man,  
A man hidden in the heart,  
Feeling a constant desire,  
And pull towards GOD, with loving pain.  
See there the soul, and quiet spirit,  
Which is found precious to GOD:  
Thus a Christian lives unafraid,  
And has refuge at all times!

label: children
translation:
YOUR LIFE, like a pure light,  
Shows me, oh Lord! my duty:  
But alas! what a difference still,  
Compared to uniformity!  
Yet, when it concerned Your FATHER'S honor,  
Your heart was wonderfully tender:  
You did not hold anyone back on that path,  
No matter how rich, how wise, how strong he was.  
Then I take everything from GOD'S hand,  
Not according to the ways of foolish men.

label: children
translation:
Come, O JESUS! come to me!  
Your presence first makes free:  
Come, O MAKER! and MAN!  
Whom the heart cannot do without!  
Oh! only by Your GRACE  
Does the heart find healing and benefit!

label: children
translation:
Awaken us, O LORD;  
So soon we may reach rest,  
Or draw into slumber,  
And be unaware of it:  
Call to us with Your voice,  
Awake! stand guard!  
Oh that Your Almighty power may stop  
Satan’s cunning and might!  
Give against all wickedness  
The Shield of true Faith:  
Yes, that we may lift up our heads,  
Like true warriors by nature:  
And break through right and left,  
As guarded by the Helmet.  
That each may be an observer  
Of the commanded watch:  
Thus we shall dare it,  
Armed with patience;  
And shall not lose heart,  
No matter how fiercely the Lion roars!

label: children
translation:
How swiftly man hastens to eternity,
As if someone had prepared him,
To pass through here in haste!
Ah! if only the soul considered it well!
And paid attention to all its ways,
To stretch itself upward!
Then turn away, my eyes;
Grant that I may loathe the rush
Of sins and desires:
My soul remains like a chaste maiden;
And, like one who carries Your vessels,
In words, deeds, and pure manners!
Teach me further to stand watchful;
Teach me to fix my eyes on You.
Tell me that You will be my STRENGTH:
So faith, love, hope grow,
And after a short passage of time,
Your SALVATION will once fully heal me!

label: children
translation:
Along the dew-covered green roads,  
As I sought my night's rest,  
I came across only my Phillis.  
When I thought the least of her,  
I saw the tears on her cheeks,  
Despite the most beautiful pearls hanging,  
Those brought from the East:  
She seemed busy in thoughts,  
With her eyes towards the sky,  
Yes, she seemed almost to wither,  
And she sighed deeply again and again.

2. I wanted to speak to her,  
Went my way very gently,  
But I greeted from the side  
Good evening, with a word:  
Good evening, how are you alone?  
Phillis, are you so lonely?  
That is not as it should be:  
Have you suffered any pain?  

With this she stood quietly,  
Came somewhat closer to me,  
And she looked at me sadly.

3. I said, “Phillis, why do you sigh?  
Are you still mocking my love?”  
She said, “Damon, why do you rush?  
Are you still so out of your mind?  
That you, to say a word,  
Would immediately dismiss me,  
Do you find such a crime in that?  
Would you be so cruel by nature,  
That you hate me, your Phillis?  
No! you could not be so depraved,  
That you completely abandon me.”

4. I said, “I would always praise you,  
As the most beautiful hereabout,  
If you could only prove to me  
That you are without falsehood.”  
“Ah!” she said, “I do not want to lose you:  
But I want to be yours and to die:  
I am already accustomed to your love.”  
And after many other words,  
I kissed her again,  
Whereupon she so charmed me,  
That I forgave her fault.

5. It has been a short time ago,  
At night in the dark hand in hand,  
I saw Phillis coming along  
With a drunken, foolish fellow:  
I confess it struck me so,  
That I thus forsook my love,  
And cursed town and country:  
I have heard that she is wasting away,  
But I care little about it,  
Though she should disappear in the mire,  
It had to be this way with Phillis.

label: children
translation:
1.  
Just like a leaf, caught by the wind,  
Is driven, and now here, and then there scattered,  
Just like a leaf, caught by the wind,  
Is now here, and then there seized,  
So I become, full of fear,  
Steadily carried away.  
Now I am free, then I see myself  
Once again firmly bound  
To the net of love, for my Shepherdess  
Feeds my love with hope and fear.  

2.  
But like a tree,  
Growing without hesitation,  
With its rustling leaves by a stream of water.  
But like a tree, growing without hesitation  
On the side of a water stream.  
So I grow, so I bloom,  
I full of courage and full of pride,  
And my heart bears the pain  
Like a hard rock.  
Yet I love faithfully, although my Shepherdess  
Feeds my love with hope and fear.

label: children
translation:
While the Youth in our days,
Ignites Love and shows her boldness,
Come let us then enjoy the sweet delight,
That this occasion offers you with pleasure,
Tasting it, that no day
May pass by untested.
2. The sweet little time of life
Compels us not, that we forsake happiness,
Now that it presents itself so beautifully,
To let it slip away, but to devote ourselves
To enjoying, every day,
The joy that may happen to us.
3. Let us leave to him who, through vile means,
Is dulled to these pleasures
And constantly deprived of all joy,
Tormented by Envy and Malice,
Not completely but every day
Here in his existence, while he may.

label: children
translation:
When I behold the lovely face  
Of Clorenia’s eyes:  
How gloriously that sunlight shines;  
How great is the power  
With which she enchants my heart,  
And with such allure pierces my soul,  
Before which all must yield.  
My freedom, which loses its strength,  
Must immediately bow before the power  
Of those exalted lights.  

2. But if I once kiss that dear mouth  
And soft rose lips,  
From which (wounded by love)  
So many kisses slipped away,  
That grant me so much sweetness  
And soul’s delight in abundance,  
I still praise my bonds;  
And think with joy of that day,  
When I gently stranded my freedom  
Through a smile upon those rocks.  

3. When I look at the lily-white  
And lovely roses,  
That possess her soft cheek;  
The lily seems to blush:  
The rose seems quite pale with regret,  
While the red that lies on her cheeks  
Dims her luster.  
Who could not, by such beauty,  
When she shows mutual love,  
Let their freedom be robbed?  

4. Then I gaze, filled with the sweetness  
Of a thousand dear delights,  
With which she steadily nourishes my love  
And playful kisses,  
A little lower than her neck  
Down to her bosom, and when  
I taste the affection there,  
It seems to me that I have entered  
Cytherea’s pleasure pavilion  
Full of joy.  

5. But gently, my soul! before you stray  
Into all these sweets:  
It is best that you limit yourself here,  
And do not wish to step further.  
My Clorenia, my little angel,  
Is then my only idol,  
Whom I will always love:  
Whom I will, despite misfortune and accident,  
Despite envy, acknowledge  
As guardian of my senses.

label: children
translation:
My dearest Phillis, despise me,  
Though you scorn my faithful love,  
I cannot abandon you,  
No matter how cold you have become,  
You remain, even into eternity,  
My son, my soul, my life;  
Fly free, from me, cruel Phillis,  
If that is your will, so be it:  
But remorse will soon follow you.  

2. I know that my misfortune  
Will move you with pity,  
Though you thus rejected me:  
And through your feigned love,  
Always led me as if to Heaven,  
Who envies me that happiness.  
Fly free, from me, cruel Phillis,  
If that is your will, I shall lose you:  
But I nevertheless want to die for you.  

3. I wander and roam through the open air,  
And the Echo laments with a sorrowful sigh  
To raise this cry to Heaven:  
Return, dear Phillis! return,  
You showed me your favor before:  
Will you now deny me that?  
Come free, to me, dear Phillis;  
If that is your will, as before,  
You alone can charm me.  

4. Is it to see my Soul's Mistress,  
Whether I also love you steadfastly?  
Ah! Please free me from that delusion:  
Do not put me to such cruel tests,  
And mix your severity with some sweetness,  
Or you would cause me to die:  
Set free, upon me, dear Phillis,  
If that is your will, your trust:  
I shall hold my love forever.

label: children
translation:
O Cloris, to what sad state  
I find myself now brought;  
I feel that through your loving face  
My freedom has been violated,  
Your presence makes me sigh,  
And when I miss you again,  
I live full of discontent,  
And full of worry.  

2. Your beauty holds my soul captive:  
While from day to day,  
My love steadily grows  
And I may not complain.  
I fear to displease you;  
Although necessity commands me  
To offer you my service,  
Still, I do not lose heart.  

3. Well then, my soul! finally resume,  
Once again your courage,  
Make Cloris your beloved known  
And fall at her feet;  
Thus can you find happiness,  
When the beauty knows  
That she is your beloved,  
And the cause of your suffering.

label: children
translation:
If there is Joy to be found,  
It is in love;  
And he who has never loved,  
Is deprived of Sense.  
Love dispels all dissatisfaction,  
Loving makes sweet Youth  
Always rejoice,  
And drives away sorrowful sighs,  
Loving always gives Youth  
New cause for Joy.

2. Fresh green Trees,  
Sweet Nightingale,  
Clear streams of water,  
All little Nymphs,  
From now on spare no lovely pains;  
Cease now your sad lament,  
Under my power,  
I want to share this joy with you,  
Under my power,  
I have brought Phillis' heart.

3. When I think of her kisses,  
My pain vanishes  
Through those sweet bonds.  
I then offer my heart  
To her all-conquering Eyes,  
And her soul’s enchantments,  
For on my thigh,  
She moved her heart to love,  
And on my thigh,  
She melts soul and soul into one.

4. When I then pick the roses  
From her cheeks,  
Which there sweetly blush,  
I boast of my luck,  
I hold it with my dear Leysje,  
That’s a Girl like a flag;  
What a sweet smile  
That soul-enchanting girl has!  
What a sweet smile,  
More beautiful than a Spring Day.

5. Come on young Senses,  
Exert your powers,  
To overcome her  
Through sweet Love,  
But forgive me, laughing rose,  
If I pluck you before your time,  
For if it lasts longer,  
Everyone’s eye will praise your blush;  
For if it lasts longer,  
Surely I will lose you.

label: children
translation:
What need do I then have to fear any longer,  
Since in sweetness both reside  
In Cloris; and her loving nature  
Shows me nothing but sweetness?  
2. What joy does not await me,  
If she once rewards my service?  
I already taste it in my thoughts,  
Although she has not yet shown me love.  
3. I now find it sweet to love her:  
I now find happiness in my burden.  
But if I can once win her heart,  
How great! how great then is my fortune!

label: children
translation:
What is sadder in love,  
Alas, what has more pain,  
Than the parting of two lovers once close at heart?  
Consider how your lover suffers,  
My Angel, as you are his:  
And all the calamity and sad torment,  
Follows that heart’s farewell.  
Ah! There is no pain so great,  
Nothing that deprives me of joy so much,  
As this alone, farewell, I go away,  
This is worse than death.  

2. For besides missing one's desire,  
In the departure we are unaware  
When we will be kissed again,  
This is the pain that pierces the heart,  
This is the sorrow that so deeply,  
Almost pulls the tears from my eyes.  
Endure then, oh endure,  
My dear Elisa, that with pleasure  
I was kissed by you once more,  
Before the sweetness, the love’s glow,  
In us is extinguished.  

3. Your little eyes there that radiate love,  
From which my soul draws its nourishment,  
Are a sun for me, under which my misery falls,  
Return then, angel! Return,  
Before I am consumed by my sorrow,  
And make that I do not long alone  
Mourn your sad departure.  
Show me soon again your sweet face,  
That object of my love and duty,  
Live with me in, the sweet love,  
Where all joy resounds.

label: children
translation:
I must unfortunately wither away  
Through all my sorrow and misery,  
Shall I then never see the end  
Of my pain and suffering in eternity.  

2. Why do I, with so many worries,
