label: children
translation:
We carry, carry, carry  
Our cart to Sint-Truien;  
Beets so juicy and red as blood,  
Don’t you bring powder, and don’t you have courage?

We hurry, hurry, hurry!  
So, just listen to it roll?  
The cart bounces and dances so fast,  
Often the beets are swollen from the juice!

We row, row, row!  
Always cheerfully through all times  
We hurry and roll, quick and happy,  
Straight with the load to the sugar factory!...

We sweat, sweat, sweat!  
Yes, everyone should know it!  
Sweating and panting, that is healthy;  
It already brings the lump to our mouth!

label: children
translation:
The early farmer goes out to the field,  
Everything is still sleeping outside;  
Hear him swiftly strike his clogs,  
Hear him cheerfully whistle:  
Starting work early is healthy,  
It brings food to our mouths!

In the rich farmland soil  
He digs his spade deeply;  
Breaks the clods, turns them over,  
Pierces through worm and grub.  
The sun burns his face,  
But quickly all the work is done!  
Now swiftly the wooden harrow goes through,  
With its pointed pins;  
Soon he can begin with eager swings  
To sow the seeds...  
Look! There flies in abundance  
The seed that must sprout tomorrow!

Quickly a little flag here and there  
Against greedy stomachs;  
The sun blazes and a breeze falls,  
The little farmer will not complain...  
Before the month has passed,  
His field will smile green and fresh!

See how the grain shoots up high,  
Breaking out of the furrows;  
Before the July sun sees it,  
The ripe grain rustles.  
The farmer sings, the farmer whistles,  
Above the song of the birds!

The scythe swings shining through the grain,  
That it might dazzle you;  
Men and children run alongside,  
Binding it into sheaves...  
Happy the grain goes into the barns;  
Now it’s a celebration in the household!

label: children
translation:
When I was a very small child  
And lay in my little cradle,  
My mother softly sang me to sleep  
With a little song every day.  
She sang so peacefully  
Of child-do;  
She sang so cheerfully  
Of do-de-ri-do.

And when I came out of my cradle  
And close by Mother's side,  
Hesitantly I put one foot forward  
And the other one gently beside it;  
Then she sang again:  
‘My child, be brave!’  
Then she sang tenderly:  
‘How well you are doing!’

And when I grew a bit older,  
Then I rode on her knee;  
Then I learned with Mommy of o and a.  
And counting one, two, three.  
Then she sang joyfully:  
‘My angel, my child!’  
And laughed happily:  
‘How quickly you learn!’  
She sang me, when I was good,  
A song she made up;  
I listened softly and whispered along  
Until I knew it completely.  
We sang together  
Of love and affection;  
That always made us  
Cheerful and happy in spirit!

label: children
children
children
translation:
We wear a worn-out suit,  
When we play on our own side,  
Rough trousers, a crooked cap,  
But we hardly care.  
We don't care a bit for finesse and beauty;  
But being strong and healthy, that is our pleasure!

We live purely from the street.  
From playing and roughhousing;  
Among us, everyone is a comrade  
And shares scrapes and bruises.  
Even if there are hits raining here and there,  
We later make double the fun!  
We don’t whine about bumps and scratches  
That we get while playing;  
Our legs are never stiff and rigid  
Or do we think about being bored.  
Whoever comes here looking will always find pleasure;  
That is the hallmark of the neighborhood, the Schipperskwartier!

And if you think us somewhat coarse of thread,  
Because we sometimes speak with force;  
And if you sometimes fear our mischief,  
Our little rascal tricks;  
That’s in our blood, that’s our joy and pleasure;  
That’s the hallmark of the boys from the Schipperskwartier!

But if we are the first on the street  
In pranks and jokes,  
You will never catch us, a comrade,  
In lies.  
No neighborhood is as cheeky as this one;  
That is why we are proud of our Schipperskwartier!

label: children
translation:
Hurry, children, quickly outside,  
For the fair is in town;  
Joyfully cheered, freely laughed,  
Now dance and jump hand in hand.  
Swing your skirts,  
Clack your wooden clogs,  
The organ that roars and thunders for the youth;  
Come! The more souls, the greater the joy!

The carillon will sing along  
At the joyful fair celebration,  
Old, new, pleasant songs,  
The most beautiful and the most.  
Hear: it tingles!  
Hear: it rings!  
Girls and boys, you are invited to dance;  
So twirl and swing, you only get one chance!

Joy makes the children come alive,  
Lays a blush upon their cheeks;  
Happiness strengthens their soul, their heart:  
So live freely and go your way!  
Dancing, spinning,  
Twisting, swinging,  
Joking and cheering, that’s life, hooray!  
Fair only once; so who will join in?

label: children
translation:
There once was a crank,
Who rode off to the town,
Already on an old donkey
That she had received.
From digge-digge-deine,
From digge-digge-dom;
But how and from whom, oh,
She did not care!

She pulled on the donkey’s ears,
Because he went so slow;
But the animal did not want to listen,
Because it had such an empty stomach.
From digge-digge-deine,
From digge-digge-dom;
Long-ear had no food,
She did not care!

She wanted to hit and push him,
She complained loudly;
He stomped with his hooves
And knocked her down in the sand.
From digge-digge-deine,
From digge-digge-dom;
She cursed, but the donkey
Did not care!
He started running,
Straight to his old stable;
She shouted, ‘He will pay for this!’
And went half crazy, half mad.
From digge-digge-deine,
From digge-digge-dom;
The rascals cheered
And laughed themselves crooked!

label: children
translation:
I was dozing,
And I dreamed in the sun;
The little bell that played in the tower.
It sang so happily
Of tinge-ting-tang!
It sounded across the heath
Of klinge-ling-klang,
So fresh and so youthful to hear!
What galloping!
What tripping!
What joyful song!
The nightingale does not sing like this in the foliage!

I was dozing
And I dreamed in the sun,
Until I knew that little song completely.

label: children
translation:
I am the blacksmith,
I hammer by the glow;
The smithy is dirty and ghostly,
My face black as soot.
Yet his face and hands are black,
My mind is hell, and pure is the heart!

To hammer and to forge,
It was difficult at the start;
I had to learn it,
And now it goes my way.
Who can work and persevere,
Becomes a strong man like me!

The fire glows in the oven,
And the iron is hot,
Then it is hammered and forged
By my hammer.
Who thinks like the smith in his smithy,
Forges when the iron is glowing!

label: children
translation:
The sun has risen  
In the fresh morning hour;  
There it blazes in the sky  
Like a glowing coal fire.  
It warms the fields;  
It shines along forest and meadow,  
Which, happily awakening, rejoice:  
Long live dear May!

The flower unfolds its bud;  
The wind rustles in the green;  
The bee gathers its honey,  
And hums as bees do.  
The spotted cows graze;  
The trees rustle happily,  
And all the birds sing:  
Long live dear May!

The sun has risen  
And shines so bright, so warm;  
The children cheer, jump  
And walk, arm in arm.  
Their young hearts sing  
Along forest, field, and meadow;  
Spring brings them this joy:  
Long live dear May!

label: children
translation:
A little shepherd drove his sheep,  
In the evening, in the valley;  
The wind blew, the sun sank,  
The little bell was already ringing.  
It called with its clap, clap, clap,  
The boy went faster;  
The sheep bells played happily  
With tingelingeling!

The little herdsman searched for his cows,  
That were grazing in the valley;  
He blew so freely, he blew so joyfully  
His lively horn call.  
It sounded so bright in the evening hour,  
And the echo took its course;  
The cows rang along nicely  
With klingelingelang!  
The girl from the mountains brought  
Her goats into the valley;  
She sang her evening song so clear,  
That it echoed everywhere.  
And the bell, that hung  
Around each goat’s white neck,  
It jingled and sang along  
With tingelingeling!

And the girl found the shepherd  
And the herdsman in the valley;  
So together again they drove  
Their herds to the stable.  
It sounded on the tower: clap, clap, clap,  
Still joyful singing in the valley,...  
Until finally it was silent, the tingelingeling  
And the klingelingelang!

label: children
translation:
Hooray! The year is over again!  
Done is the school grumbling,  
With the teacher's jokes and quarrels,  
With work and lessons and punishments!  
Vacation time! It's a celebration, hooray!  
Away with pencil and paper!  
Let everyone cheer and rejoice freely  
And celebrate in their own way!...

We keep the Hague school all day long;  
No teacher may punish us!  
First comes wild fun,  
That refreshes body, mind, and heart!  
Jackets off, clogs off,  
And now let's go strong into the forest!  
Through the bushes, up the fir trees,  
And rolling over the moss!

We jump over ditches and canals,  
The birds sing, the sun laughs;  
We dance merrily in the greenery,  
Or take a shady nap.  
Thus our cheeks gain a lovely color  
And our hearts blaze with joy;...  
Thus after the cheerful play,  
We do justice to the meal at home...  
So the sunny time passes by,  
Until everyone again, free and happy,  
Gathers paper, pen, and pencil  
And hurries off to school.  
With heart and soul we then tackle  
Work, tired from playing;  
Thus both body and mind  
Awaken again, fresh and strong!

label: children
translation:
Dirty, dirty, baby!  
Porridge stands in the spinning wheel;  
Milk from the spotted cow,  
Childman, close your little eyes!  
If you do not want to be quiet,  
Then I'll get a stick  
And hit you right on your bare bottom,  
Because you start to grin.  
Come, little sweetie! sit down,  
Each on a chair,  
And give that beautiful girl  
A kiss on each cheek!  
What does Maria have on her lap?  
A little child.  
What does that child have in hand?  
A beautiful book.  
What is written inside?  
For father and mother to sleep alone,  
And I lie in a little cradle.  
The cradle can be made,  
Then I sleep in a sheet;  
The sheet is in the wash,  
Then I sleep in a puddle;  
The puddle is too deep,  
Then I sleep in the reed;  
The reed is too long,  
Then I sleep by the snake;  
The snake is too wicked,  
Then I sleep by the lizard;  
The lizard is too beautiful,  
Then I sleep in the hay.  
I’ve already been such a customer,  
I am still such a customer,  
And whoever has something to say about it,  
Can just come up with it.  
A hat with plumes!  
A skirt made of parchment.  
The whole city is burned down  
All because of that crazy man.  
Hoppelepop  
My little bread is gone.  
My little bag is to the mill.  
I still have a little penny,  
That’s not a cent too much.  
One, two, three, four, five, six, seven.  
Jan van den Beld came by  
Already with a basket on his back.  
What was that little basket worth?  
Two golden pins.  
A little coal fire,  
A little coal fire  
Is already too expensive.  
Then give me a slap on the head,  
A slap on the head hurts too much,  
Just give me both little ears again!  
They came last  
Alone in a wooden tub.  
Soapwood!  
Beat on a bone,  
Beat on an ash or two,  
I will throw you into the sea again.  
If you then come ashore,  
I will throw you with sand.  
If you then come onto the dike again,  
I will throw you into the mud.  
Whole or half!  
Just bite the head off the beetle!  
What is needed?  
A little coal fire.  
I have no fire.  
I saw your chimney smoking, though.  
That was at the neighbor’s.  
May I come in briefly?  
Too dirty clogs.  
Take off clogs?  
Too dirty pants.  
Pull off pants?  
Too dirty feet.  
Wash the feet?  
First put on the pretty little shoes.  
Wife, how your house looks!  
Get a broom and clean it out!  
What beautiful children do you have!  
It suits you quite well!  
May I have one of them?  
What will you give them to eat?  
All the treats that I have.  
Then just give her one!  
In a while I will bring you a pot of snakes!  
Go away, you ugly witch!  
Spin, spin, Lisje!  
Every day a little pile,  
Every day a little web,  
That goes towards Lisje’s dress.  
In Voassen already on the Rollekoord,  
Haven’t you heard about it?  
Of three pretty girls?  
One sat knitting,  
The other sat braiding,  
The third was spinning,  
And the very smallest little lady,  
Won the most money!  
Spin, Lisje! spin!  
The suitors are arriving.  
Little ball, little ball of yarn!  
Beautiful girl of eighteen years!  
Beautiful girl has already turned around,  
That she learned from father and mother.  
Then came Jan the butcher,  
Who said: ‘The yard was thin.’  
Then came Jan the carpenter,  
Who nailed the dog’s tail back on.

label: children
translation:
Mietje once went to fetch water  
Mietje once went pumping  
She walked on her clogs  
Without socks on her legs  
Dance(!) she through the puddles  
From your ras, ras, ras dance (!) one happily through the puddle  
From your forward, forward, forward dance (!) one happily through the gate  
From your ras, ras, ras dance (!) one happily through the puddle  
From your one, two, three

label: children
translation: Hossen forests little horse, With your flowing tail, This is how the gentlemen ride With their colorful clothes, This is how the women ride With their colorful sleeves, This is how the farmer rides With his little horse behind him.

label: children
translation:
Good evening Aunt Betjen  
Good evening uncle Jan  
And my mother asked me to ask you  
If you could come sometime  
With the little Poppedeine  
And the big Bombam?  
Good evening Aunt Betjen  
Good evening uncle Jan

label: children
translation: What time is it? Twelve o’clock. Who says that? The maid. Where is she? In the cellar. What is she doing? She is brewing. For whom? For the little mistress and the big monsieur who has a pair of trousers with a tear that the buttocks hang through.

label: children
translation:
I. God loves the child.  
From the German by W. Hey. Music by Silcher.  
From heaven tenderly,  
The Lord God looks down  
Upon the children  
And governs their fate.  

2. He hears their prayer,  
Faithfully day and night,  
Keeps watch over them  
At every step.  

3. He mercifully gives them  
Their daily bread,  
Closes them in His arms,  
Saves them from distress.  

4. Tell all the children  
That God loves them,  
Who with pleasure  
Looks down upon a child.

label: children
translation:
II. Child's Joy. From the Little Garden of Children

Oh we children, we live,  
Like little birds happy,  
For the Lord above there  
Cares for you and for me.  

2. To give us everything  
That Lord never gets tired;  
Every day a thousand gifts  
He kindly scatters to us.  

3. When the sun in the morning  
Kisses us from the twilight,  
We rise without worries,  
After a safe rest.  

4. And in the evening then we are  
So tired from playing;  
And then we close our eyes  
Without worries again.  

5. Oh what richness of blessing  
Spirit, that friendly God!  
May my heart always cling  
To His word and command!

label: children
translation:
III. Singing and Praying  
From the German by W. HEY.  
Music by the Author.  

The little bell rings, the bird sings,  
Everyone in their own way,  
Child! you too  
Sing along,  
To the Lord’s praise and honor.  

2. Pray and sing,  
For nothing  
Goes well without praying;  
Every child  
Who loves God,  
Sings to Him with a joyful heart.  

3. Learn, oh learn,  
To the Lord  
To sing, to pray, at every moment;  
God gladly  
Enjoys a song,  
Rising from the children's mouth.

label: children
translation:
IV The Friend of Children.  
From the German of Lieth.  
Music by Gläzer.  

A faithful friend of children goes  
Through all the lands,  
No eye can behold Him,  
But He sees every child.  
Heaven is His homeland,  
He is the Lord’s messenger.  

2. He comes into every home,  
And wherever a joyful child  
Loves his father and his mother  
And God the Lord,  
There He gladly dwells day and night,  
And watches over the child.  

3. There He plays with the children  
So kindly and so gently;  
He shares in their joy,  
And listens to their complaint;  
And the child who follows His lessons  
Becomes gentle and kind in spirit.  

4. And when the child goes to rest,  
That Friend does not leave him;  
Faithfully He guards his little bed,  
So that no harm befalls him,  
And wakes him in the morning hour,  
And makes him cheerful and healthy.

label: children
translation:
V. Trust. From the German of Boxe.  
Folk Wisdom.  

1. The Lord, who dwells in heaven,  
And reigns in every humble heart,  
Is near me by day and night,  
Faithfully keeps watch over me.  

2. He feeds the sparrows on the roof,  
The birds on twig and branch,  
He adorns the field with flowers and plants,  
From his generous fatherly hand.  

3. Not a hair falls from my head,  
But it happens according to his will;  
He knows my sitting and my standing,  
My lying down and my going.  

4. O Lord! who so faithfully guards me,  
Give me a heart soft and good,  
Make me pious like the angels,  
So that I may come to heaven!  

NB. The last lines of each stanza should be repeated.

label: children
translation:
VI. Reading. Words from the Author, Folk Song

The Lord be praised,  
Because I can read  
In book and in leaf;  
Now I can also learn  
What the richness of the Lord  
The Bible contains,  
The Bible contains.

2.  
Now I can also read  
How God has loved me  
Long before;  
How Jesus gave His life  
For me,  
Although I forgot Him.

3.  
Come, cheerful and diligent,  
Not proud and regretful,  
Let’s begin reading;  
Lord, make me skilled  
To understand properly  
All that is written.

label: children
translation:
VII. The House. Words from the Author. Folk Wisdom.

A house of wood, a house of stone,  
A large home, a small home,  
Whether on meager fare or abundance,  
Just as the Lord gives it is good.  

2. A person needs very little,  
As long as he lives on this earth;  
What does he do with a great palace  
On this brief journey of life?  

3. And when he dies, weary of life,  
He can manage with even less;  
A patched-up plank or so  
Is then his entire house and treasure.  

4. If only the Lord dwells with me,  
Crowning my labor with His peace,  
Though my dwelling then be a cell,  
He makes it into a king’s house.  

5. A house of wood, a house of stone,  
Everything passes away with the world;  
A small hut, a poor cell—  
There is the eternal father’s house!

label: children
translation: X. The Fruits. Words and Music by the Author. God gives us a thousand things, That taste good and delicious, For our nourishment; So that we may learn, That He, the Lord of lords, Is a God of gentleness, A God of gentleness. 2. The berries and raspberries, The plums, the apricots, The cherries in May, The pears in September, The medlar in November, The walnut and strawberry, 3. The peach, the lemons, The grapes and melons And the apples sweet and sour, Peeled and then eaten, Or with the midday meal, Stewed on the coal fire. 4. The sugar from the West Indies, For chestnut fudge and coconut candy, For syrup in milk and porridge, The coconut and chestnut, And the apples from orange, The honey from the bee. 5. What precious things, That taste good and delicious, The good God gives us! So that we never become gluttonous, But at His time and in moderation, We praise Him in enjoyment. 6. For, although I am surrounded By the most tasty things, The sweetest delicacies, It is all of no use to me, Unless He in His grace Walks by my side. NB. The last line of each verse is to be repeated.

label: children
translation: XI. The Little Songbird. Words and Music by the Author. There is a nice little bird In our neighbor’s garden, He sings his song cheerfully, to the Lord’s praise and glory. 2. He sings of all the little flowers, The little flowers white and red; He sings of all the little trees, The little trees small and tall. 3. He sings of all the little children, Who are sweet and gentle; Sing on, oh! dear little singer, We sing along with you.

label: children
translation:
XVI. Evening Song. Words and Music by the Author.

Tired from work and tired from play,  
In the evening I incline myself to rest,  
When sleep gently strokes my eyes,  
I lie according to my heart’s desire  
In my bed, clean and soft,  
I slumber the whole night through.

2.  
I close my eyes, I close my ears,  
I hope to dream pleasantly,  
When I see and when I hear,  
I cannot fall asleep.  
Therefore, friends: speak softly!  
I am going to sleep! Good night!

3.  
Oh, what kindness  
The Lord sends down again,  
Who has spread my little bed,  
Who gives sleep to my limbs.  
He, who has completed everything,  
Also grants me this calm night.

4.  
Leaning on His dear blood,  
I lie peacefully bowed down.  
It is the Lord who watches over me,  
When I have closed my eyes.  
The Lord’s angel keeps watch  
Over my bed the whole night through.

label: children
translation:
XXIII. At the beginning of the School.  
Chorale: Christ is my life.  
For 4 children's voices.

1. O Lord, who are so gracious,  
Show us Your face,  
Give Your precious blessing,  
Now at the instruction.  

2. Teach us to read with understanding,  
And to write beautifully and correctly,  
Teach us to pay attention  
To what the teacher says.  

3. Preserve us from thoughts  
That are evil and wrong,  
Grant wisdom, desire, and strength  
To the teacher who teaches us.  

4. O! Grant us to be so capable  
According to Your lessons, Lord!  
And place us altogether  
At Your feet!

label: children
translation:
XXXIII. DO YOU KNOW HOW MANY.  
Translated from the German by Hey. Folk wisdom.  

Do you know how many stars cling  
To the blue arch of heaven?  
Do you know how many clouds drift  
Above all the high mountains?  
All those thousands together,  
The Lord calls by their names.  
And not one escapes His eye,  
And not one escapes His eye.  

2. Do you know how many little mosquitoes dart  
In the hot sunshine?  
Do you know how many little fish splash  
In the silver flood of water?  
All those thousands together  
The Lord calls by their names:  
It is the Lord who feeds them.  

3. Do you know how many children rise  
From their little beds each time,  
So that they may give thanks  
To the faithful Lord of heaven?  
To those thousands upon thousands  
That God shows favor,  
And He also tenderly loves you.  

Note: The last line of each stanza should be repeated.

label: children
translation:
LV. CHILDREN AND ANGELS. Words and Music by the Author.  
1. The children sing what is good and true,  
And the angels above listen to it.  
And the angels above listen to it.  
2. The children sing  
So joyfully of Jesus,  
And the angels play  
The harps along with it.  
3. The children sing  
In love and in peace,  
And the angels above  
Sing along as well.  
4. Thus the children sing  
Together with the angels,  
To the praise of the Lord’s  
Most holy Name.  
5. And the angels above  
The children below,  
And God so gladly inclines  
His ear to them.  

NB. The last line of each verse should be repeated.

label: children
translation:
LVI. BOYS' GAMES.  
Words and Music by the Author.  

The spinning tops! The spinning tops! They whir around;  
We throw them down buzzing,  
Then they spin back and forth,  
And then roll over the ground.  
And then roll over the ground.  

2. The marbles! The marbles! The marbles are made of stone;  
We know how to flick them,  
And with our thumbs to tip them,  
Then they shoot rolling away.  

3. The balls! The balls! The balls are made of leather;  
We bounce them away like a bullet,  
Or shoot them up like a bird,  
And catch them again as they fall.  

4. The kite! The kite! The kite is crazy and square!  
We let it rise and hover  
Far above the houses lifted,  
And hold the string in our hand.  

5. The hoop! The hoop! The hoop is round!  
We strike it while running past,  
And chase it over the stones,  
Then it jingles over the ground.  

NB. The last line of each verse should be repeated.

label: children
translation:
LXIII. Responsorial Psalm. Words by Mr. Is. Da Costa. Music by the Author.

O Jesus, who through the bitterest suffering  
Have earned life for sinners!  
Our hearts may also rejoice  
Because Your name is Friend of Children!  
Yes! From the mouths of infants  
You have prepared praise for Yourself!  
Then teach us to joyfully sing  
Of Your grace and glory!

2. To praise, serve, and honor You,  
Renew our childlike spirit!  
Grant us a blessing in learning!  
Remain gracious and good to those who do good!  
Prepare us once here below  
Each for the work assigned to him,  
Especially to be lively members  
Of Your ransomed Church!

3. You found delight in weakness—  
O! also have mercy on us!  
And what we ask of You in psalm-singing,  
O! Yourself cause us to understand!  
Our stammering prayer has no value,  
Yet You have promised us blessings!  
You! Yourself a child for us on earth,  
You God with us for eternity!

label: children
translation:
LXVIII. The Most Beautiful Treasure. Duet.  
Even if my marbles were ruby and sapphire,  
Even if my kite were made of silver paper,  
Even if I had a hobby horse  
Entirely graceful and beautiful,  
Even if I had a siskin  
In a golden cage.  

Even if I had a spinning top  
Made of the most precious wood;  
Even if I had a hoop  
With golden bells;  
Even if I had two goats  
With horns so long,  
With silver reins  
And a golden rod;  

Even if my piggy bank  
Were the richest in the land;  
Even if I were the smartest  
In skill and mind;  
It would all be of no use to me,  
And it would not be a treasure,  
If I did not have the love  
Of the Lord.

label: children
translation:
LXIX. Closing Song. Words by Mr. Is. da Costa. Music by the Author. Three-part harmony.  
To our God and Father,  
The eternal source of life,  
Be thanks and honor and praise,  
May His lovely blessing  
Sprinkle like the rain  
The education received,  
The education received.  
Forgiveness of all sins,  
May it also be found for us  
In the blood of Jesus’ cross,  
His good Spirit guides us,  
Convert us and prepare us  
For the fatherly house,  
For the fatherly house.

label: children
translation:
LXXIII. Children's Prayer. Words and Music by the Author.  
O Lord! who so tenderly Loves the children,  
Hear in mercy The prayer of a child!  
2. Forgive my sins,  
Enable me to do good,  
And incline to heaven  
My youthful mind!

label: children
translation:
LXXIV. God's Parental Care. Words from the Children's Friend. Music by the Author.  
Look down at the dear little flowers  
That hide in the green grass!  
And at the mosquito, that back and forth  
Knows so adorably and cheerfully how to dance!  
Though they are small, their fate  
Is guarded by the all-seeing eye of God.  
Though they are small, still their fate  
Is guarded by the all-seeing eye of God.

2. And would God not care for you?  
Dear child, who bows your little knee before Him!  
And in the evening, in the morning,  
In Jesus you express your thanks to Him!  
Oh He, who looks from heaven,  
Does not forget your needs either.

NB. The last two lines of each verse are to be repeated.

label: children
translation:
Naatjen Pappegaaitjen  
What time is it, etc.  
For the little Poppedeine  
And the big bombam  
Good evening Aunt Betje  
Good evening Uncle Jan

label: children
translation:
What time is it?  
Twelve o'clock  
Who says that?  
The girl  
Where is she?  
In the basement  
What is she doing?  
She is knitting  
For whom?  
For you  
For the little lady  
For the big sir  
There is a tear in your pants  
And the buttocks are shaking  
From peeling the potatoes

label: children
translation: Maaike Parrot What time is it Eleven o'clock Who said that Pretty girl Where is she In the little room What is she doing She is knitting For whom For the big one For the little one Fideldijne Fideldom Just turn around

label: children
translation:
It’s raining,  
It’s raining,  
The roof tiles are getting wet  
The farmers walk with bare feet

label: children
translation: It's raining, it's raining, the paths are getting wet, then two little farmer girls came who fell on their bottoms.

label: children
translation:
It’s raining, it’s blessing  
The tiles are getting wet  
And then Mama doesn’t leave the city.  
It’s raining, it’s blessing  
The tiles are getting wet.

label: children
translation:
It’s raining, it’s raining  
It sputters and it splashes  
Two peasant women came.  
They fell into the wet.

label: children
translation:
Good evening Aunt Betje  
Good evening Uncle Jan  
And my mother sends her regards  
Asking if you could come by sometime  
With the little doll  
And the big bimbam  
Good evening Aunt Betje  
Good evening Uncle Jan

label: children
translation:
Little Parrot  
What time is it  
Eleven o’clock  
Who says that  
Little mouse  
Where is he sitting  
In his little house  
What is he doing  
He is working  
For whom  
For the little Poppedeine  
And the big Bombam  
Good evening, player

label: children
translation: What time is it? Ten o’clock. Who says that? The girl. Where is she? In the kitchen. What is she doing? She is knitting. For whom? For the little Poppedein. For the big ones, bimbom. She climbs the cathedral. She climbs the door. Good evening, sir.

label: children
translation: What time is it Twelve o’clock Who says that The girl Where is she In the kitchen What is she doing She is knitting For whom For little Poppedeine For the big bombam Good evening, player

label: children
translation:
Good morning, Aunt Kaatje,  
Good morning, Uncle Jan,  
compliments from Daddy  
asking if you could come  
with the little doll  
with the big bimbam

label: children
translation:
Choose whomever you want  
Then choose Sofie in the circle  
And then we sing along  
From 1, 2, 3.

label: children
translation:
Dear Lemmers was still alive  
Yes sir, I am still here  
I have eaten my food  
And I still have my drink

label: children
translation: The teacher says Jantje may go home. No, Jantje has done something bad. Up in the attic, they are playing rough and tumble. And in the back of the cemetery, they are hitting Pietje on the head or something.

label: children
translation:
Between here and between Leiden  
I saw a flea being cut to pieces  
It was a little animal like an ox.  
Its blood is collected in pots  
To use it for bloodletting  
Since it was the slaughtering time (repeat)

label: children
translation:
First I came to Rome  
There was a whale in the trees  
Reading the Kampen newspaper  
With a sandwich in its hand.  
Then I came to Lombardy  
Guess what I heard there  
The cow sat by the fire and spun  
The calf lay in the cradle and sang  
The cat churned the butter.  
The dog washed the dish.  
The bat swept the house.  
All with their golden little wings  
Aren't those big little lies?  
(coughing song)

label: children
translation:
Jan trip Jan tree  
Who is coming with us?  
Who is coming with me?  
Then I saw the bride going to the church  
Then I called out bride, bride, throw a handful of sugar out.  
The first year an apple red  
The second year a child on the lap  
The third year about five  
That will probably be twelve.  
Then I called bride, bride!  
Throw a handful of sugar out.

label: children
translation:
Counting rhyme:  
1. In the garden there hung a rose,  
In that rose there lay a letter,  
In that letter was written  
You must be honest.

label: children
translation: 2. I am free From the lottery Coffee thick Away I am

label: children
translation: 3. Ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, sixty, seventy, eighty, ninety, one hundred. One hundred jumps over the sea All the water flows along. Ice mush, porridge, Whoever gets the last clap Must be fair to him.

label: children
translation: 4. Under the bridge there lay a bottle, On the bottle was written: You must be honest.

label: children
translation:
Ring game:
5. Push, push little cart,
Push it along now,
I have such a nice little cart,
That belongs to you.
Where shall I look for it,
Here in every corner,
I don't know what to do.
I have already found it,
And have already lost it again.
Come here, my dearest little lamb.
Come behind me.

label: children
translation:
Playing Kaatsballen:  
6. Farmer, farmer, I am not a farmer,  
What are you then?  
A merchant.  
Where is your house?  
Just behind the sluice.  
What is on it?  
A little rooster.  
What is your wife's name?  
Adriaantje.  
What is your husband's name?  
Jan, Jan.

label: children
translation: 7. Apricot basket, Mom, what should I do? Go milk the cows. Mom, I have no shoe. Then put on father's boots. Mom, they are too big. Then cut off a piece. Mom, then I am a Jew. I do not want to be a Jew, I do not want to be a Jew. Jews drink gin and we drink wine.

label: children
translation: 8. Katrijntje sat behind the curtain. What was she doing there? She was combing her hair. She washed her little hands, her mouth and her little teeth, she dried them off. Put them at her side and knelt down beside them. She stood up again, a big doll made of porcelain. She became so big, so small.

label: children
translation: Jump rope: 9. In spin The bow goes in. Out spray, The bow goes out.

label: children
translation: Round Circle: 10. I have a beautiful flower basket, To whom shall I give it? The one who stands closest to me, I will give those flowers. Oh beautiful woman, Those flowers are for you.

label: children
translation: 1. There was a little man in the moon, hooray! He was wearing chamois leather pants, hooray! He had eaten so much syrup, and that’s why in the pants... Hooray, hooray, hooray! hooray, hooray, hooray. (Kaatsbal).

label: children
translation: 2. In The Hague, Sofie lives there, married to Willem Three. Willem Three gave her a kiss, and she couldn't do anything about it. (Kaatsbal).

label: children
translation:
3. Hariejet, jet, jet
Is the coffee already made? Hariejet has run away,
Who will make the coffee now?
Not me.
Who then?
Our little Janneman.
(Batting ball).

label: children
translation: 4. The school is a monkey house, hooray! The monkeys sit two by two, hooray! The biggest monkey stands in front of the class And hits them on the ribcage, Hooray, hooray, hooray! Hooray, hooray, hooray. (Handball).

label: children
translation: 5. One, two, blow away. (Tag or hide and seek).

label: children
translation: 6. Little soldier, stretch your knee. Little soldier, drop your handkerchief. On the count of one two three Little soldier, stretch your knee. Little soldier, pick up your handkerchief. One two three. (Skipping rope).

label: children

label: funeral
translation:
Full of joy in life;  
Full of joys in death;  
Full of joy in the churchyard,  
Full of joy in Charon’s boat,  
With playing and drinking,  
Lied the musician through life,  
Until they let him sink,  
With the dead in the grave.  

He was a master in playing,  
On bass, violin, and flute,  
As well as on the dulcimer,  
That makes a sweet sound:  
The strings sounded merrily,  
Where Bacchus takes hold,  
Venus sits to admire.  

The musician, undisguised,  
Was betrothed in faithfulness,  
With a beautiful flower, as a bridegroom,  
And had not the least sorrow;  
He played without mourning,  
With his bow and violin,  
While many a capriole  
Danced to tears.  

While the strings resounded,  
The legs went round,  
They quickly brought the musician a glass,  
Filled with Bacchus’ liquid:  
Then he struck bravely,  
But the quint broke in two,  
The musician succumbed,  
Without speaking, oh alas.  

They wanted to help him,  
But he was so situated,  
He was already gone, he drew a chord,  
To the Elysian Fields,  
What do you think of that little tale,  
Is that not a fine discourse?  

He played of beautiful Aeltje,  
When his soul flew out of the body.  

Before he was buried,  
They gave him without leaving,  
The violin and the bow there,  
Together in his coffin,  
And on the cloth for protection,  
Hung dulcimer and bass,  
With which he played his time,  
While he was a musician.  

Fourteen white roses more,  
Were placed on the cloth,  
The bearers of the musician’s corpse,  
Were already all complete,  
With cloaks and with boots,  
Grasped the corpse thus with the bier,  
Carried him smoothly,  
To the churchyard, indeed.  

The bearers wished to listen,  
Each had a bottle,  
Filled with brandy from the canteen,  
And always drank,  
The followers wished to understand,  
Bravely blowing off smoke and steam,  
From fine long pipes,  
Until they came to the grave.  

They unloaded the load,  
From the shoulders,  
And let the dead body,  
Descend straight into the grave,  
Then one still heard sounding,  
Strings as proof of thanks,  
The bearers went drinking  
At the churchyard from their drink.  

Then they departed together,  
From the churchyard again,  
It was surely a burial,  
Of musician and bridegroom,  
They drank undisguised,  
And played there with vigor,  
On basses and violins,  
And they parted at midnight.

label: funeral
translation:
Many Jews came with Martha and Mary,  
To comfort her over the death of Lazarus her brother,  
And Mary meanwhile heard of Jesus’ coming,  
She ran to meet him, Mary remained on the floor.  
2. Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, had you been here,  
My brother would not have died.  
But I know that whatever you ask from God,  
He will give you, even if it is very great.”  
3. Lord Jesus said, “God will give your brother life again.”  
And Martha said, “I know this in the resurrection.”  
Lord Jesus said, “I am the resurrection and the life,  
Whoever believes in me will live, even if he has died.”  
4. “Whoever lives and believes in me will never die forever.  
Do you believe this?” she said, “I believe it, Lord,  
For you truly are the Christ, who has come down here  
From the living God, his Son.”  
5. When she said this, she went to Mary.  
She said, “The Master is coming and is calling you;”  
When she understood this, she became glad,  
Therefore she got up and went to meet Lord Jesus.  
6. The Jews, who meant to comfort her at home,  
Seeing this all went along with her and thought otherwise,  
That she was going to the grave to mourn her sorrow.  
She fell at Jesus’ feet as soon as she saw him.  
7. And she said to Jesus, “Lord, had you been here,  
My brother would not have died.”  
When Jesus saw her weeping so much,  
He himself was deeply moved, stirred in the Spirit.  
8. “Where have you laid him?” he asked. They said, “Lord,  
Come and see,” and Jesus cried.  
The Jews said, “See how much he loved him.”  
But some of them said:  
9. “He who opened the eyes of one born blind,  
Couldn’t he have kept this man from dying?”  
And Jesus again was deeply moved, as before;  
He came to the tomb, where a stone had been placed.  
10. “Remove the stone,” he said, and Martha said, “Lord,  
It stinks, it has been four days since his death.”  
“Did I not tell you,” he said to her, “If you believe  
You will see the glory of God.”  
11. They removed the stone, and Jesus stood up.  
“Father, I thank you,” he said, “  
That the people standing here may believe,  
I know that you always hear me.”  
12. When he had said this, he shouted with a loud voice,  
“Lazarus, come out!” and he came into the light,  
Bound hand and foot, with a sweat cloth over his eyes.  
And Jesus said, “Untie him, so that he may go free.”  
13. Thus many of those who had come with Martha and Mary  
Believed; and some went away quickly to tell  
The Pharisees what Lord Jesus had done.  
14. We believe in Jesus, that you are sent from God,  
That you can raise the dead from death:  
Whoever believes in you has already found life,  
Whoever lives in you need not fear death.

label: funeral
translation:
We see the olive branch blooming  
That your Brothers saw growing on your grave,  
In quiet melancholy.  
We almost pluck the little flower off.  

Rest softly: you have fought your fight;  
You were faithful until death.  
The cause for which you suffered  
Was, like your spirit, elevated, great.  
That spirit must strive endlessly forward:  
It cannot perish in the earth.  
Death grants you a higher life:  
There dawns a more beautiful morning.  

The future you made us anticipate  
Floats in a more blessed horizon.  
See how we gather at your grave:  
Rest, Brother; but do not leave us.  

Look down on us with a little smile,  
From a more perfect, more beautiful place.  
Oh yes, we will see you again one day!  
‘The future’ is the Brother’s word.

translation:
1. Rest peacefully in the heart of God  
Who now has called you to Himself  
Who, for a perishable fate,  
Offered you eternal joy  
Though it is a prayer full of sorrow  
That now surrounds your resting place  
Yet the believing heart takes comfort  
As soon you will live again in heaven.  
Chorus:  
Rest peacefully in the heart  )  2x  
Rest peacefully in the heart of God  
Who, after earthly strife  
Will grant blessed joy  
Offering you endless delight.  

2. You have fought here on earth  
So courageously for the good  
Therefore, the crown is reserved for you  
And God grants you joy for your tears  
Sweetly shone as you, weary of dying,  
Laid your head to rest  
The eye of your Savior upon you

In eternal peace.

translation:

Angels carry them on your pens  
To the realm of love and light  
That can still be seen and known  
Before the overcast face.  
Rest guarded by the Most High  
The humble seed sown by Him  
To one day harvest the pure grain  
Where death reaps no stalks.  
Rest here beneath withered leaves  
Slumbers under gravestone or sand  
The breath of God still hovers over it—

Tears drop on the graves

Life, life, enduring life  
Sprouts on the field of death  
The nightly veil is lifted— (repeat twice)  
Only then does the dawn glow shine— (repeat three times)

label: lullaby
translation:
1.  
It is so lovely to be the Bride,  
The Groom, without fail.  
But here is the cradle so fine:  
Would you like to rock it gently?  
Rock gently with your legs,  
Your child will not cry yet.  
This dear Couple,  
Within the year,  
I wish the Groom a Father.  

2.  
If it is not within the year,  
What difference would it make?  
Or if Father is not a Man?  
Or would it matter to Mother?  
I tell you with conviction,  
You must investigate:  
If Mother is a bit delicate,  
You find more,  
But sometimes it returns again.  

3.  
Another year around this time,  
When your child is born,  
And you lose your color a bit:  
Do not lose courage!  
With rocking and singing  
To bring our child to sleep!  
And rock thus  
And sing always: ‘Hush!  
Why does our little one cry so?’  

4.  
If our child then does not want to be silent,  
Rock a bit more wildly:  
‘Hush, hush, my little child, hush,  
Or are the flies tormenting you?’  
Then Father speaks anxiously:  
‘Oh wife, does this come from marriage?’  
‘Oh dear man,  
That is what it’s from!  
What advice do we have then?’  

5.  
Oh, if my little brother knew this game,  
I know he would keep watch.  
But I don’t dare tell him,  
He would also laugh with me!  
Because he would not believe it,  
He may try it himself.  
That no one sees,  
He doesn’t believe,  
Until he is in sorrow.  

6.  
‘But wife, I still know some advice,  
We shall rock the cradle some more.  
Yes, tomorrow when the day rises,  
We will buy the child bullets.  
A porridge without poison  
We shall give our child.’  
‘Your advice is good.  
Your porridge is sweet,  
Tomorrow the child is healthy.’  

7.  
With this the Little Song is finished.  
Listen without shame:  
It is for the Groom and Bride  
And those who have come here.  
When Mother sits by the fire,  
Come Father, warm your ears!  
The porridge pot here,  
A jug with beer:  
Do they not have pleasure?

label: lullaby
translation:
1  
Now their little child, why does he cry to me so?  
Mother puts it in the pot with pieces in front of it,  
The dear child screams wildly,  
If the man wants to eat;  
There is no need to eat,  
Mother comes with porridge and honey,  
The child must eat something,  
It is wildly hungry for porridge now.  

2  
Now my little troublesome one, now, don’t make a fuss,  
Stop crying now, don’t break out of his mouth again;  
Now now, be calm then,  
I don’t know what I do, poor man,  
With the child making trouble,  
It’s pressing on, because it’s breaking,  
Breaking his dear heart,  
I don’t know what’s wrong with the child.  

3  
I want to talk to the doctor that there is something wrong;  
It’s been stirring for twelve weeks, it is not getting better.  
Cutting the ground like a stone,  
It’s lying with terrible pain.  
Now my love, you must  
Be silent: it lies with the child,  
And without knowing,  
That I will be responsible for it.  

3 (repeat)  
There I want to end, my love,  
In silence, and in peace, and rest,  
And solitude, that is now my desire:  
I will never leave there,  
They would call me again, to mock me,  
Old Grumbler.

label: lullaby
translation:
Who the devil is knocking at my house,
Is it a rat or is it a mouse,
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband,
Sleep my sweet little child,
And close your little eyes.
Dear wife, what are you singing then,
Oh my dearest husband,
May I not sing what I can,
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband,
Sleep my sweet little child,
And close your little eyes.

label: lullaby
translation:
Sleep, sleep, little child  
Close your little eyes,  
Lay your little head down.  
All that crying does no good  
My dear child;  
For your eyes are too tender.  
And rest nourishes you more  
Than food does:  
Therefore, sleep peacefully,  
Peacefully, my dear delight,  
All those sobs  
Are my greatest pain  
And they make me very uneasy.  

2. Close, my dearest rascal.  
Hey dear, close  
Quickly your dove-like eyes tight,  
How else could he be comfortable?  
I truly believe  
That here the light is too strong.  
Therefore, quickly bring me here  
The cradle and the child.  
Darling, sleep, sleep so,  
Sleep so, you are too tired.  
Rock a little with ease:  
His eyes are already tight,  
And just like that they are closed.  

3. Gentle west wind, rustle  
Without any noise,  
Now my dear child sleeps.  
Sweet little parrot, do not  
Make any squawking,  
For my little cat is napping.  
Go past here very softly  
And on your toes:  
When he sleeps, his little heart  
Grows, grows.  
And his eyes are  
Then free from all the pain  
That he gets from the tears.

label: lullaby
translation:
Now sleep my little Mietje;  
Come, happily, close your eyes...  
That is again the old song!  
Yes, looking at the light.  
But you’re laughing!  
Oh, I know what you want from me again.  

I will complain to Mama;  
The one who gave you to me.  
So to tease little darling!  
Can’t you stop it during the day?  
But you’re laughing;  
Yes, I know what you want from me again.  

Now you will come to me,  
My angelic child!  
So you will be taken  
By the one who loves you:  
But you’re laughing!  
Yes, I know what you want from me again.  

Look there, satisfy your desires,  
Oh, was my love thirsty?  
What kisses does this little hand get,  
So resting on my chest!  
Oh, you’re laughing;  
I hope you haven’t waited too long.  

How the sweet little girl blushes!  
How those eyes look so sweet;  
What a friendly little face;  
How she looks mild, and good!  
Yes, you’re laughing?  
I can almost always guess what you want.  
I have lost my child.  
What a grief that caused me!  
I am not allowed to hear about it yet,  
So it touches my heart.  
When she laughs,  
What haven’t I expected from that.  

See, now into the little nightgown.  
Night, dear lovely treasure! ...  
Oh, are you starting to talk again?  
Not to me, do you understand that!  
Even if you laugh,  
Our Mietje must now sleep, because it is night.

label: lullaby
translation:
Way: Without love, without wine.

Great Creator, gladly weary,  
I bring to You a joyful song of thanks,  
I have received my wish;  
I have a dear young sprout,  
The goal of my desire;  
Let the child's tender sound  
Be replaced by my singing.

Hope and fear were all around us,  
When my dear spouse  
Was to enter the anxious lying-in bed,  
I prayed to You, God! mercy -  
Be gracious in her need,  
Hear her amid the moaning,  
Give her the little one, in her lap,  
To embrace motherly.

My prayer, directed to You,  
May succeed for me,  
May I press a dear and tender little one  
To my bosom,  
I embrace my brave wife,  
I call: my delight, my life!  
See the pledge of faithfulness  
Given to us by God!

I have dedicated to You, who knows all,  
The little one,  
May he be protected by You from evil, suffering,  
And danger;  
He who will go into the world  
Must fear temptation,  
Therefore will You be his guide  
On that narrow path.

Then the child will be our joy,  
Will live to Your honor,  
And, as the possession of virtue,  
Strive for truth;....  
But it seems to me, the child cries,  
Would it then hear me?  
Softly - I do not want to disturb him  
With any cheerfulness while sleeping.

M.N.

label: lullaby
translation:
Way: Where or my Rozalijntje stays.

Sleep, sleep peacefully, my little child sleep,  
My little Jan, do not cry anymore,  
What is troubling you, my dear lamb?  
You are crying your little eyes sore,  
For no care yet pains your little heart;  
You know no evil yet;  
What is it then, that frightens you,  
That you will not go to sleep?

Your father works with pleasure and joy,  
And earns the bread for you.  
God blesses him, and rewards his virtue.  
Is this happiness not great?  
And mother is not a wealthy woman,  
But yet lives peacefully.  
She cares for you with love and faithfulness,  
You are the joy of her heart.

If God spares your dear life,  
And fulfills our wish,  
Then He will give you a gentle nature,  
A virtuous, pure mind,  
That always finds its joy in doing good:  
Never marveling at appearances:  
Then God will also bless my child....  
But softly... my little Jan is sleeping.

Ma.V.H.

label: lullaby
translation:
Sleep, my dear young Guurt;  
Your crying can be heard through the neighborhood!  
Sleep, sleep young Guurtje, oh do not cry;  
Soon brother Piet will come, and little aunt too, if you see that;  
Otherwise, you will cause mother sorrow.

Mother will bring you sweet cake  
In her apron cloth.  
Sleep, etc.

You will quench  
Your hot thirst at a full breast.  
Sleep, etc.

Then, then you will get a tasty treat,  
And from godmother a doll.  
Sleep, etc.

There, now my dear child is quiet,  
As my housework begins again.  
Sleep, etc.

V.O.

label: lullaby
translation:
Sleep now peacefully, my dear little one  
Sleep, delight of my tender heart,  
Close, close your dear, your sweet little face.  
Sleep unhindered, free from pain,  
You are now in your most blessed place,  
Where you feel neither sorrow nor worry:  
No regret stirs in your little heart.  
Oh! May your state of bliss last forever!  
But, as the years grow, so will care and pain grow too,  
Sleep gently, sleep gently, my dear lamb,  
And may you be happy.

Sleep now peacefully, my dear little one,  
Sleep until the early morning stands.  
Close, close your dear, your sweet little face.  
Sleep is healthy for you.  
Oh dear delight! of your Mother;  
Oh little Angel, in whom appears  
The image of your good Father.  
My heart entrusts you to the Almighty Guardian!  
Grow up in all virtue, to honor the Fatherland.  
The state, the state, watches through time,  
And also relies on your hand.

Sleep now peacefully, my dear little one.  
And rest your little pure loan.  
You have already closed your sweet little face,  
Still unaware of the opposite.  
One can see in your charming being,  
Where in the sweet rose of Youth,  
That delights the Motherly heart,  
Is shown, calm peace read.  
Rest gently, my dear little Lamb, fulfill fulfill your desire.  
Sleep gently, sleep gently, your Mother  
Yearns beside you for rest.

label: lullaby
translation:
Yes, flattering little Heart Thief!  
You reach out your little arms to me;  
I understand the innocent little complaints,  
And lovingly tearful little smiles;  
You are weary of your cradle. (repeat)

But, kindly blushing Angel!  
Your mother longs for rest;  
And father, who stroked you,  
When you played on my bosom,  
Had kissed you to sleep.  
What disturbs your carefree sleep?  
My playful little child flirts,  
To frolic on my lap,  
Laughing, playing to exhaustion; -  
This is why you cry.

You stare with blinking little eyes  
At the fading night light;  
Oh! sleep, my blushing little girl:  
That eye-charming little light  
Must be behind the curtain.

I give, kneeling by your cradle,  
You another sweet kiss;  
Come, sleep will help you grow,  
It will, in its gentle bonds,  
Develop your strength.

Your pale—your kitten-like little hands  
Stroke joy into my heart;  
Oh Dear God! your kindness  
Gives me the purest sweetness,  
After anxious heartache.  
You continue to watch over my child;  
My God! it bears your image,  
Your image—that in its little looks,  
With pure love flickers,  
So soul-touching plays.

This laughing blossom bud  
Unfolds, as freely as happily,  
The reward of the dew,  
Once again the finest honey  
For the Netherlands society.

My blooming Spring Rose!  
Unfolds in the meadow of freedom;  
It spreads unadulterated colors  
And offers scents of love,  
Estranged from revenge and deceit.

Hope ignites my urge to sing;  
But gently... my little one rests.  
Yes, Darling! sleep a little,  
But before I close the cradle clothes,  
Once more a gentle kiss.

label: lullaby
translation:
Sleep, sleep peacefully, my little child sleep,  
My little Jan! do not cry anymore,  
What is troubling you, my dear lamb,  
You are making your little eyes sore,  
For no care yet troubles your little heart;  
You do not know of any harm;  
Then what is it that unsettles you,  
That you will not go to sleep?

label: lullaby
translation:
Sleep peacefully, my dear little one,  
Gently lay your little head down,  
Let this little blanket surround you,  
So that no draft comes your way,  
Sleep softly then, my dear little one,  
Until the morning sun awakens you.  
Rest until the morning light,  
While it nourishes you.

There you lie, my dear little rose,  
With two cheeks red in color,  
Rest softly then, my dear darling;  
I am surrounded by the scent of balm,  
That descends upon your little lips:  
From your rosy cheeks,  
They are still but tiny spots,  
Which they wish to give to mine.

When the night spreads its pale wings  
Over your bed,  
Then Luna will strengthen it again;  
With her silver shining moon,  
That no harm may approach you!  
In this quiet approaching night,  
So rest well on all sides,  
On your bed, sweet and soft.

God! oh Father of all souls,  
Look down on this little one as well,  
I want to kneel down in prayer;  
Rising with thanks; good Lord,  
Keep this child safe tonight;  
From weather or worry and sorrow:  
And free him from dangers,  
You, oh God! who sees everything.  
I still gaze at my dear child  
With a tearful eye,  
That no cold or breeze  
May disturb his rest,  
That my dear sweet little lamb,  
May not have his sleep disturbed,  
And that tomorrow I may hold  
The dear boy again in my arms.

label: lullaby
translation:
They once laid me down,  
Young and tender,  
In the rocking cradle;  
I was soothed,  
Caressed, kissed,  
Cherished, tenderly cared for,  
So be careful,  
I never suffered,  
From cold, draft, or wind;  
They rocked me, }  bis.  
And sang along: } bis.  
Of suja, suja child. } bis.

Thus I grew up,  
And received a doll  
With which I played happily,  
Nothing that I had,  
And with my treasure,  
Did not kindly share the doll;  
I got for her,  
Now here then there,  
A ribbon, cloth, or bow,  
At the cradle sang, }  bis.  
My childish tongue, }  bis.  
Of suja, suja child. } bis.  
But I soon noticed,  
When I was older,  
That doll bored me,  
I felt clearly,  
That I was missing something,  
And did not know what was wrong with me,  
Ah, it was love,  
That soul and mind  
Had suddenly enchanted me;  
For with one glance, }  bis.  
Frederik had } bis.  
Conquered my youthful heart. } bis.

It was his wish,  
My wish as well,  
To give him my hand,  
So it soon came  
To suit me well;  
To think of a cradle;  
Now I watch over  
A dear doll,  
And take care of drafts and wind,  
And sing, where the child }  bis,  
Loves the song, }  bis,  
In my turn: suja child } bis,

label: lullaby
translation:
Slumber softly! To the tender mother's heart  
No infant feels the sorrow that the parent bears,  
Oh, your dreams know no pain,  
And your world is the mother's breast.  

Ah! How blissful is the dream of those times,  
When one lives solely on a mother's love,  
Which, like a heartbeat, slips away from us,  
And no life, however full of blessings, returns!  

Thrice is the evil man granted,  
Thrice is the sweetest happiness prepared for him,  
That he, once born for Heaven,  
Here receives love in open arms!  
Love grants him the very first blessing,  
And the infant blooms—still free from pain,  
Everything smiles back at the bright gaze...  
Love adorns him at the mother's heart.  

And when, at life's midday radiance,  
The youth's fire kindles in noble passion,  
Then, then his sun shines through clear tears,  
And the rose of the tenderest love blooms.  

Yes, love takes him a second time,  
Then, as a lover, holds him in its elfin arms,  
So that its radiant light never falls from him—  
But always lovingly caresses and warms!  
Yet the storm wind breaks the rose’s stem,  
The human heart is no less spared...  
Then love appears as an angel of peace—  
And joyfully carries him heavenward!

label: lullaby
translation:
It is getting dark, my little Rose, come, play no more,  
Cease, darling, your restlessness, lie down calmly,  
You lie in your little cradle, so warm, so soft,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night,  
There she lies, my treasure, tired from frolicking,  
Now she closes her little mouth, her eyes go shut,  
But; I still read on her being that she smiles innocently,  
May God watch over her mother, my child, good night,  
You still live, my Angel, in carefree youth,  
My breast is your heaven, a bell is your joy,  
Your desires still sleep; one day you will feel their power,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night.  
Happy, my little Rose, who dozes as you do,  
Oh! May it always be so through your innocence,  
He dozes so softly, who fulfills his duties,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night.

label: lullaby
translation:
The sun is rising, my little Rose awakens,  
You slept so peacefully after the lively play.  
Come stretch and stretch out once more, my child,  
That way you grow my little heart, my darling, my sprout.  

Awake now, my little Rose! It is a bright day,  
It is time to play . . . your innocent laughter,  
Strokes your mother’s heart, your friendly glance,  
Always makes her feel that you are content.  

Come play now, my little Rose, my darling, my treasure,  
Every piece of toy is grasped by you,  
And precious wealth, a caressing amusement,  
Come play now, my little Rose, the game is your affair.  

God's blessing guide you, my little Rose, my child,  
Grow up playing virtuously, loved by all,  
O comfort in my life, O joy of my heart,  
May God protect you from sorrow and pain.  

My little Rose, my angel, my delight and amusement,  
How you caress my senses, where my heart glows,  
Driven by joy, by feeling of love,  
If God protects you, I achieve my goal.  

And if your days climb one day, my little Rose,  
How my respect and tone of gratitude then rise to the top,  
And if those days then become a year,  
My heart beats with joy so clearly.  

Grow up then, my little Rose, in innocence and virtue,  
So that father and mother’s heart is delighted,  
And gambol and play just like that on my lap.  
May God keep you, then you will soon grow up.

label: lullaby
translation:
It is getting dark, my little Rose, come now do not play anymore,  
Stop, darling, this tossing, rest quietly down,  
You lie in your cradle so warm and so soft,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night.

There she lies, my treasure, tired from frolicking,  
Now she closes her little mouth, her little eyes go shut,  
But I still read on her being that she smiles innocently,  
May God watch over her mother, my child, good night.  
You still live, my angel! in carefree youth.  
My breast is your heaven, your joy a hell,  
Your passions still sleep, one day you will feel their power,  
May God watch over you, my child! good night.

Happy, my little Rose! who slumbers like you,  
Oh! may this forever be through your innocence,  
He slumbers so gently who fulfills his duties,  
May God watch over you, my child! good night.

label: lullaby
translation:
It’s getting dark, my little Rose, come don’t play anymore now,  
Stop, darling, that tossing, lie down calmly,  
You lie in your little cradle, so warm and soft,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night. (repeat)  

There she lies, my treasure, tired from frolicking,  
Now she closes her little mouth, her little eyes close,  
But I still read on her being, that she laughs innocently:  
Watch over her mother, God; my child, good night. (repeat)  
You still live, my angel, in carefree youth,  
My breast is your heaven, a bell is your joy,  
Your passions still sleep, one day you will feel their power,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night. (repeat)  

Happy, my little Rose, who slumbers like you,  
Oh! May this always be so through your innocence;  
He who fulfills his duties sleeps so gently,  
May God watch over you, my child, good night. (repeat)

label: lullaby
translation:
Once upon a time,  
They rocked me,  
Young and tender,  
In the swinging cradle;  
I was soothed,  
Stroked, kissed,  
Cherished, gently cared for;  
So be careful,  
I never suffered  
From cold, draft, or breeze;  
They rocked me,  
And sang along:  
Suja, suja, little child.

Thus I grew up,  
And got a doll,  
With which I played happily;  
Nothing I had,  
I did not share kindly  
With my treasure,  
The doll:  
For her, now here, then there,  
I received a ribbon, a cloth, or a lace;  
By the cradle  
My child’s tongue sang  
Suja, suja little child.  
But I soon noticed,  
When I was older,  
That the doll bored me;  
I felt clearly,  
That something was missing,  
And did not know what ailed me.  
Ah! It was love,  
Which soul and mind  
Suddenly enchanted me;  
For with one glance,  
Frederik  
Had conquered my youthful heart.

It was his wish,  
My desire as well,  
To give him my hand.  
So soon it suited me  
To think of a cradle;  
Now I take care of  
A dear little doll,  
And protect from draft and breeze,  
And sing, where the child  
Loves the song,  
In turn, Suja little child.

label: lullaby
translation:
Our Advent star shone,  
which illuminated us so bright.  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

That house had so many holes,  
where Christ was born.  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

She put that child on her lap,  
she kissed his little mouth red,  
it was so sweet.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

She put that child on her knees,  
she said: Great honor must be done to you!  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

She put that child on her arm,  
with great joy she looked at him,  
it was so sweet.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

The mother made the child a bed,  
how lovely that he sat in it!  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

That little child splattered with his hand,  
so that water splashed from the basin.  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe.  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

The ox and also the little donkey  
who worshiped the sweet little child.  
Well was she then.  
Susa ninna susa noe,  
Jesus, love, spoke to Mary.

label: lullaby
translation:
Who the devil is knocking at my house?
Is it a rat or is it a mouse?
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband!
Sleep, my sweet little chin,
And close your eyes!
When it rains, then it is wet,
Then my husband does not leave the town.
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband!
Sleep, my dear little chin,
And close your eyes!
When it rains, then it is dirty weather,
Go to your house, and come back tomorrow!
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband!
Sleep, my sweet little lamb,
And close your eyes!
"Dear lady, what are you singing?"
Oh, my dearest husband,
May I not sing what I can?
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband!
Sleep, my sweet little chin,
And close your eyes!
Under my spiral staircase
There is a tray with wine tapped.
My husband is home, my husband is home,
My husband, my husband, my little husband!
Sleep, my little chin,
And close your eyes!

translation:
It is getting darker, my Rose! come, don’t play any longer:
Stop, darling, that tossing, lie down quietly.
You lie in your little cradle so warm and so soft,
that God pro-

translation:
Slumber, little witch, slumber - but softly;  
tired - yet - loving

tired - dear - one

label: lullaby
translation:
Allegretto.

Michaelis.
Sleep child—sleep, Soon become a clever boy,

And once you get married, Then find a very dear
woman, Who pleases you by her own beauty, And never wears false hair. Sleep child, sleep, Soon become a clever boy.

label: lullaby
translation:
1. Sleep, little heart-thief, for now you are tired; Come, close those bright little eyes! Everything is quiet and still around your cradle, Nothing will disturb you, no mosquito or fly.  
2. Sleep, my boy, your mother watches; She will protect you if you suffer harm; For when I think of what awaits you in life, Dear child! I do not sleep a single night.  
3. Why, my angel, do you smile at me like that? Do you not believe the words of your mother? Do you think, little boy, your mouth looks so happy: Someone watches, dear mother, even better than you?  
4. Son, then you are completely right, That makes your mother so blessed and rich; When I pray to Him for protection from sorrow, I slumber safely with you close to my heart.

label: lullaby
translation:
It is getting dark, my little Rose, do not play anymore,  
Stop, dear, that fidgeting, lay down quietly,  
You lie in your little cradle so warm and so soft,

label: lullaby
translation:
Good night! Good night!  
Sleep, my beautiful one! Slumber softly!  
Who, who can sleep better,  
Than the one who has innocence as a weapon,  
And love as his strength?  
Good night! Good night!

label: lullaby
lullaby
lullaby
translation:
I = C. Not too fast  
It’s getting dark, my Rose—oh! come, don’t play  
anymore;  
Stop, my love! that restlessness, lie down quietly.  
You lie so warm and soft in your little cradle.  
May God watch over you! My child, good night,  
May God watch over you! My child, good night!  

2.  

There she lies, my treasure! tired from frolicking,  
Now that little mouth closes, her eyes go shut;  
But I still read on the being that smiles innocently:  
“Watch, God! over my mother!”           (refrain)  
My child, good night!           (refrain)

label: lullaby
translation:
I = C.  
Sleep now, my sister – you, you are after all tired;  
Quickly close your bright little eyes.  
All around your cradle everything is silent now,  
No one among us wants to disturb your sleep.  

2.  

We have played so wonderfully together,  
We certainly would not have been bored for a long time yet,  
If dear Mom had not said: "Now it is time!"  
And softly led little sister into the cradle.  

3.  

Sleep now, my little sister, peacefully through the night,  
Mother keeps watch by the cradle;  
Tomorrow our fun will start again,  
When Mom fetches the children again from bed.

jumped, they jumped and sang:
‘It is cer - tain, that a dear lit - tle child is born;’
la la la la la la la la
‘That a dear
lit - tle child is born.’

label: lullaby
translation:
Kinneken, you cautiously close your eyes  
Why did you want to cry so much?  
Didn't want that little girl and washed  
You must not deceive yourself  
So don’t play with the bulls [?]  
Sleep, which brings rest  
I must dig, mother must sleep  
Are we not enough tormented  
So, little pullet dear [?]  
Where has your little mother gone  
Be silent, my little lamb, she will provide  
[A laever / Aen liever?] little mother give  
Little mother, if you [?] have a task  
Do that lamb a favor  
After crying came yawning  
Sleep, it had too big a watch

label: lullaby
translation:
XXXI. LULLABY.
Words by the Author.
Folk Wisdom

Sleep, little child,  
The angels keep watch:  
Their little wings will cover you,  
Until they joyfully wake you again;  
Sleep, little child.

2. Sleep, little child,  
The Lord gives you strength,  
No harm will befall you,  
The Lord will watch over you well.  
Sleep, little child.

label: lullaby
translation:
What time is it? Twelve o'clock.  
Who says that?  
The maid.  
Where is she?  
In the kitchen.  
She is knitting.  
For whom?  
For the little mister  
And the big bimbam.

label: lullaby
translation:
Gr: It was a nice time, though. Did you know that nowadays they hardly ever sing anymore? Yes, they always shout, but actually just singing is no longer done. I find that quite a shame. There used to be a sense of togetherness. We had a similar kind of song, but I can recall even more of a verse from it: 16:50 - 18:18

Mother is sewing  
On a coat hem  
Little sister must now be rocked  
Sweetie, sleep and dream  
Little brother, little brother, little brother mine  
Let me, let me be your mother.

Gr. can no longer remember the following verses.  
T: And what is "driegen"?  
G: Driegen? Sewing. [At M's request, G sings it once more].

label: st_nicholas
translation:
1.  
That Sinterklaas,  
That old boss,  
Has to run for Saint Felten, for Saint Felten.  
He only helps with a great commotion,  
Turning the whole city upside down.  
Otherwise, he doesn’t do anything, Filis, Filis,  
Otherwise, he doesn’t do anything,  
As you can see.  

2.  
He said  
He would give  
A good girl  
To me, to give,  
Which he withholds from me year after year:  
Is that fair, I wonder?  
Speak clearly, Filis, Filis,  
Speak clearly,  
Is it not broken?  

3.  
When I was a child  
He seemed my friend,  
And gave me Spanish clicks, Spanish clicks,  
With new balls, skates and ribbon,  
A sled to poke with:  
But now he drives me, Filis, Filis,  
But now he steadily drives me  
Past by.  

4.  
Another cage,  
There he has nicely  
Looked out something good, looked out,  
Only to throw some rubbish in my hand in the end  
That he will miss, Filis, Filis,  
That he will miss  
In this case.  

5.  
I don’t want, I don’t want,  
Any nonsense,  
No face full of wrinkles, full of wrinkles,  
No little hunchback with glasses,  
Hooked on the pimples.  
Nor an angry face, Filis, Filis,  
Nor an angry face,  
Nor a scarecrow.  

6.  
I am never prepared  
For ugliness  
That makes me chatter my teeth, chatter my teeth,  
Where a well-made girl  
Makes me burn like a firebrand:  
Yet a pure glow, Filis, Filis,  
Yet a pure glow  
Ignites my blood!  

7.  
Good holy man,  
That old fellow, can  
Deny me his favor, deny his favor,  
And cast me forever under the ban:  
He will never deny me  
That I love you, Filis, Filis,  
That I love you,  
With soul and mind.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
Saint Nicholas, good holy man,  
Hear my prayers and my pleas,  
For those who call on you in need,  
I call to you very sadly:  
Not for sweet cakes or figs,  
Or for children's toys,  
Let me just receive a suitor,  
Who fulfills my desire for love.  

You always, every year,  
When I confess my need to you,  
Do not fail to send me  
What I long for with pleasure:  
If I ask you for two pendants,  
Or for a golden needle,  
Or for lace handkerchiefs,  
Your favor has never failed me.  

Whatever my heart has desired,  
I have received from you every year,  
But now I must explain to you,  
What pains my heart now:  
I pray, hear your servant,  
Who burns with desires of love,  
And inclines toward a suitor,  
That he may give me his faithful hand.  

Grant me a good man's child,  
Not too old or young in years,  
One experienced in the art of love,  
Who loves me as his own soul:  
Not a gambler or cheater,  
Nor a drunkard or trifler,  
But one who is pure and chaste,  
And sensible with limbs.  

Grant me such a man,  
Who can govern himself well,  
And who knows how to honor his wife,  
And can properly receive her in bed:  
Who in his dear arms,  
With sweet loving caresses,  
Often tries to warm me,  
Oh! then I would be happy.  

If I then have a young son,  
When I will lay him in the cradle,  
I will say with a joyful voice,  
Saint Nicholas is my patron:  
Saint Nicholas will then be godfather,  
The child's name will be Klaesje,  
Because by him I have healed,  
From my pain and love-sorrow.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
Give us, O Good Saint Nicholas!  
A gift that delights us:  
Calls, Papist youth, foolish and mad,  
Righteous foolish joy:  
A gift from the Elders in their time,  
Draws children thankful;  
I will be most glad  
If Father’s favor befits me.  

If Father’s wisdom looks otherwise,  
Then I am too strange;  
If I did not receive it, would I not thereby  
Walk the right path?  
Oh no, that is not the opinion,  
No, certainly not my mind  
Flies higher than all that one sees,  
Than all the sugar-coated goods.  

Nothing brings more blessing to it,  
Than that one learns to pray,  
And that one diligently wants to go to school.  
Reign, O Lord, reign  
How more and more your own child,  
Unveil your favor now:  
Fie Saint Nicholas your fame is wind,  
Sugar-coated Papist deceit.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
There is already Amusement and Joy  
To be seen on Saint Nicholas,  
How Merry is then the Youth,  
Who is still so foolish,  
That he does not confess along with me,  
That in the whole Year,  
There is no time so pleasant,  
Briefly sweet and rare.  
And everyone buys as they please:  
Household fathers give you through the City,  
With the little Woman beside you,  
And buy until the purse is flat,  
And make your Children happy.  
Well, do not neglect time, you must  
Go out with the Girl.  
The agreement has been brought to an end,  
Saint Nicholas is out,  
Who has now properly looked after the Girl,  
She will get him as her Bridegroom,  
So this day can give nothing else,  
Than Joy and Merriment,  
It helps many a Girl to get a Man,  
And young men to get the Girl.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
1.  
O Saint Nicholas, good holy man,  
We sing to your honor,  
Old, young, rich, poor, all who can,  
Servant, maid, farmer, citizen, lord,  
We all bring our shoe,  
In one way or another,  
We care for you, oh Klaus!  
And give you the praise,  
Oh Klaus! The noise fills old and young,  
That is heard along the street,  
The baker laughs long, the little boy cries,  
But listen to how it continues,  
Look at old Hillebrand,  
With Maalken Tiereluur,  
They still perform many strange rituals  
On Saint Nicholas Day.  
Although they eat no pastry,  
They still drink a sip,  
Soon they go drunken to their bed  
And sleep like a goat,  
Oh Klaus! The noise, etc.  
Married maids create joy,  
And walk with pleasure,  
Up and down the streets, glad  
To be so, is their business now,  
They lie in wait for Saint Nicholas  
Who walks with a chain,  
And makes a terribly loud noise  
While he buys jokes,  
Oh Klaus! The noise, etc.  
The youthful folk have fun,  
Jan kisses his dear girl,  
And says you are my everything here,  
My Saint Klaus! I vow,  
My whole body to you,  
My darling my Caproen,  
Where I hope to creep in,  
Just put your shoe there,  
Oh Klaus! The noise, etc.  
The children have the most fun  
Though they are tired,  
And early at night go to bed,  
Before Klaus with his horse,  
They do sing well, Holy man,  
And look into their shoe,  
Yet it is found nowhere else  
But for doing the biscuit,  
Oh Klaus! The noise, etc.

6.  
The merchant’s Saint Klaus is money,  
So is the craftsman’s,  
But it is devilishly badly arranged,  
No soul has a chance,  
To win money, Saint Klaus,  
Who rides past many,  
The farmer alone is the boss,  
He lives always free and happy,  
Oh Klaus! The noise, etc.

7.  
O Saint Nicholas, good holy man,  
We ask for no cookie,  
For no pastry, not a chance,  
But give us in our pants,  
But Schyven, oh then we buy,  
Sausage, ham, cheese, bread, and bacon,  
And meat and pudding and rice porridge,  
And sugar for the lack,  
Oh Klaus, indeed give! As long as I live,  
Then your holy day  
Is celebrated by us, with wine and punch,  
Singing, shouting, laughter.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
Saint Nicholas! good holy man!
Hear my prayers and my pleas,
For those who call on you in need,
I call to you very sadly;
Not for sweet cake or figs,
Or for children's doll toys;
Let me have a suitor
Who satisfies my love desire.

You usually every year,
When I confided my need to you,
Do not fail to send me
That which I eagerly desire.
I prayed to you for two pendants,
Or a golden needle,
Or a handkerchief with lace.
Your favor has never failed me,
Whatever my heart desired.
I received from you every year,
But now I must explain to you,
What now pains my heart,
I prayed, hear your servant,
Who burns with love desire,
And tends towards a suitor
Who will give me his hand in fidelity,
Grant me a good man's child,
Not too old or young in years,
And experienced in the art of love,
Who loves me like his soul.
No gambler or cheat,
Nor drunkard or scoundrel,
But one who conducts himself pure and chaste,
And is quick of limbs,
Grant me such a man,
Who can govern himself well,
And knows how to honor his wife,
And can entertain her in bed;
Who in his loving arms,
With sweet caresses,
Often tries to warm me,
Oh! then I would be happy.
If I then have a young son,
When I lay in childbirth,
I will say with a happier voice:
Saint Nicholas is my patron;
Saint Claus will then be godfather,
The child's name will be Klaasje,
For through him I am healed
Of my pain and love sorrow.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
I = A.  
Look, the moon shines through the trees,  
Friends, cease your wild noise!  
Friends, cease your wild noise!  
The glorious evening has come,  
The evening of Saint Nicholas. The evening of Saint Nicholas,  
Our hearts beat with anticipation,  
Who will get the cookie, who will get the rod;  
Our hearts beat with anticipation,  
Who will get the cookie, who will get the rod.

2.

Oh, what fun it will be to play  
with that colorful harlequin.  
We will share everything fairly,  
sweets and marzipan.  
But oh woe, what bitter sorrow,  
we got a rod instead of a cookie! (repeat)

3.

But I do not fear that we will complain:  
Father, Mother, are so good.  
Even if we were not good every day,  
we were sweet many times.  
So banish fear from your heart,  
I wager there is not a single rod. (repeat)

label: st_nicholas
translation:
Saint Nicholas, good, good, good,  
Put something in my empty barrel,  
Put something in my little boot -  
Thank you, Saint Nicholas.

label: st_nicholas
translation:
Sinterklaas, that good sir,  
He comes back every year,
