label: wedding
translation:
Bruytje heard, What has indeed captivated you,
To join in marriage?
Since your word
Always was I will gladly continue
We without man content.
But certainly says a little journey,
Or is it not, Was she who called you there
The Goosewife of the girls.
2 So always, Fear not for such choice,
Where your virginity must fall,
It is a rose, That in a short while,

label: wedding
translation:
The true marriage is full of honor,  
It is indeed sacred,  
Thereafter stretches the sweet desire  
Of that united pair,  
Who by bonds of love  
Have entered into marriage,  
So that they now hand in hand  
Do sincerely promise fidelity.  

2 Groom, yet give your ear  
To God’s holy Word,  
So that you do not live in darkness,  
Proceed diligently:  
And clear the way of peace,  
That is a pure path,  
So you may come into the Cities,  
The city of eternal Life.  

3 Wife, Bride, also hasten quickly  
After your Groom and Lord,  
For you must well obey him,  
According to the holy doctrine.  
As all your Wife Bride companions  
Show more love at all times,  
And you both shall then find  
On a firm ground.

label: wedding
translation:
Cheerful gentlemen, who in flourishing,
And growing from kindled fire,
After much pain, you have hastened
To the blessed bond of Marriage.
I wish that the Lord, O! All-giver of all goodness,
With His abundance may graciously bestow upon you
That no heartbreak, nor disastrous wrath,
Nor grumbling mood
May deprive you of your joy:
But that you may, as two united lions,
Love each other and share faithful affection.
The hour is approaching that you, with ardor,
Shall enjoy wished-for joy,
A loving gaze with a thousand kisses
Fulfilling your duty and sweet youth.
My Song-goddess accompanies you with her rhymes
To the bed where Hymen’s torchlight shyly fades,
Where Love will teach your lips to unite.

label: wedding
translation:
I wish much happiness to this united couple:  
Mr. Bridegroom and Mrs. Bride,  
We all wish you  
Happiness in marriage, also prosperity in this state,

label: wedding
translation:
What is the innocent loneliness?  
What is the chaste virgin life  
A bitter joy, that laughs while crying:  
A water bubble driven by the wind.  
2 The careful little duck that in the brood  
Swims alone finds no pleasure;  
Her worth is like a head and comfort  
And if she misses it, it will soon quack.  
3 The simple little dove is sad,  
If it must walk alone in the cage:  
But if one shoots the little turtledove,  
How easily then its beak opens.  
4 It chirps, it turns, it scratches, it digs,

label: wedding
translation: Just as a ship comes sailing through the waves from the uncharted sea,

label: wedding
translation:
Sweet Girls set your minds  
To love,  
Those who first offer you their love,  
So you may also rest as you wish,

label: wedding
translation:
Guests, who have come  
To the Wedding, to be joyful  
I pray you will think quietly,

label: wedding
translation:
When the wedding vow has been exchanged,  
Common people follow the pair,  
Parting also has its time,  
As does the sweet gathering:  
So too does the wedding end,  
When the meal is finished,  
Everyone must prepare themselves,

label: wedding
translation:
Among all States  
None as good as the Genuine state,  
Let the Nuns talk  
Of the Monastic attire:  
Of false virtue and purity,  
And what else weighs on the heart.  
The Monastery cannot build Cities,  
But in Marriage it is preserved,  
By each one's Majesty.  
2 Eva, let yourself be guided,  
Follow not the path of pleasures:  
Love is full of grace,  
Come Sophia, follow love,  
Lead the noble Bride to the Bridal bed,  
Do not hinder her Bridegroom,  
Then kiss her good night, for the last time,  
Give the rest to the Couple for the best,  
According to the ancient law.

label: wedding
translation:
Man, a creature of God's hand,  
And the noblest to be found,  
Endowed with glorious understanding,  
Which from the beginning governs  
Everything given,  
Enjoys no peace or rest,  
Until here,  
To his pleasure,  
It has kissed delight.  

2 An example the Bridegroom will give,  
Who for a while  
In love’s storm, back and forth,  
Wandered, powerless,  
(Despite those who envy it)  
Has enjoyed his will,  
And to his desire,  
Kisses his beloved,  
Which makes envy tremble.  

3 No state on earth is perfect  
Unless the Marriage decides:  
He who marries has entered a state,  
That takes away all joys,  
But not, unless one sells oneself.

label: wedding
translation:
Sings in honor of the Bride,  
Now the Bride; locks her chamber doors.  
And her splendor // robs us,  
From the Hall // and dims  
All the glimmers of the Wedding feast.  
Go, brave two // and take with you  
God's Peace,  
He protects you together from adversity,  
Until death // removes Sorrow,  
And the need // of the grave.  
It comes to take life away.

label: wedding
translation:
Venus Little Bride  
Has the Bride  
In her little house, well reached;  
Therefore today, now she is going to walk  
Where she tastes peaceful delight;  
The bridegroom lies and drools,  
And thanks that little Stokebrand.

label: wedding
translation:
Groom and Bride who have today
Entered
Into the marital state,
I wish that love and peace and fidelity,
May build
Always the upper hand with you.
2 Love is valued the highest,
Here on earth,
(With good reason) well esteemed:
Because it gives the greatest joy,
Where virtue
Is in its full strength.
3 And her sister, noble Peace,
If she finds a place
In both your hearts,
She will drive away quarrel and ugly strife from you,

label: wedding
translation: Sweet Games,

label: wedding
translation: I see the bride's headband with ornaments.

label: wedding
translation:
Adam planted the first love  
In the spacious Paradise,  
Girls, don’t be too wise,  
Eva soon set her senses,  
To a chaste wedding feast,  
Now show a joyful spirit too.  

2 Grandfather, what was the reason  
That your heart chases after her?  
Was it not by nature’s movement,  
Which still in Man and Woman  
Is the cause of true fidelity?  

3 The sweetest word spoken to you  
By that great and wise Teacher  
Is greatly multiplied,  
Often interrupted by many,  
Unchanged, sincerely experienced,  
As the deed reveals it.  

4 Bridegroom, do not be discouraged,  
Think it has happened many times before,  
For you are not the first,  
Grandma taught Mother to marry,  
Who sang the same tune,  
As the old sing, so chirps the young.

label: wedding
translation:
Now to the bridegroom of the house  
The heavenly light has long been descending  
And the little love flame is  
With its torch-light in your halls,  
To kindly invite you in,  
And your bride is ready  
To slip away from you with a leap,  
Just to pluck her hair while walking,  
Or she’s gone before you know it.  
2 Bride, why do you sit so dull,  
Between two such happy maids?  
Does reflecting dull you  
About your lavish days,  
Fears of the bridegroom’s love breezes,

label: wedding
translation:
Who has ever rightly and justly described  
The sweet sweetness,  
Which through Marriage the man and woman enjoy,  
Like a man who is virtuous by nature,  
Has received from Heaven  
Such a Woman who steadfastly shuns and flees sins,

label: wedding
translation:
It is so lovely to be the Bride,  
The Bridegroom without fail,  
But here is a fine cradle-string  
You want to gently pull,  
Gently rocking with your arms,  
Your child will then not cry,  
This dear couple, within the year,  
I wish the Bridegroom there.  

2 So if not within the year’s end,  
What would it matter then?  
Whether Father is not a man  
Or if it would trouble Mother?  
I tell you with conviction,  
You must investigate it,  
If Mother is a bit tender, people find more,  
But sometimes it happens again.  

3 Another year around this time,  
When your child is born,  
And you then lose your color,  
Do not lose courage,  
With rocking and with singing  
To bring our child to sleep,  
And rocking thus, and singing softly,  
How does our little child cry then?

label: wedding
translation:
He who marries does not marry for six days,  
But for his dear whole life long,  
One must never ask for the passport,  
Even if this service were once so strict.  
He who has it has not obtained it,  
Does not sleep alone,  
Whether it goes well or against him,  
Shows himself to be content.  
2 And if he takes his rights in his arms,  
Giving his hearing its demand,  
Thus one must warm love in love,  
Thus tempers love’s flame,  
This is the rudder to steer the ship.

label: wedding
translation:
Playmates, sweet animals,  
Don’t you hear how they speak?  
Of fighting, strange in manners,  
Without anyone dying or being executed, oh no:  
Those who choose this fight  
Are wind when they lose it.  
2 Do you want to avoid it: not to fight,  
Or do you not have a party?  
That’s too bad, what a thousand disasters!  
See who sits here at your side? A breast  
That thirsts (if only it could endure it)  
To go to war with you.

label: wedding
translation: O Bridegroom’s Consolation

label: wedding
translation:
Well Vrysters, what's the matter, [how] do you sit so still,
And cheerful? Be merry, play tag, come dance,
And feet trot along.
Oh come quickly;
We still have to go to the dance this evening.
2 Quiet, hear no bass, no violin, not even a flute,
No playing, no teasing, no lively sound?
Then you must be deaf:
Or as I believe,
Everyone thinks: I was the bride at the wedding.

label: wedding
translation:
Afterwards, brides blush shyly  
And do not gossip,

label: wedding
translation:
Now the first night has passed,
That the Bride was so afraid of,
But the worries have vanished
Because of the little rascal in the bag.
2 See how the little eyes are burning now,
Oh, they're shaking their heads;
Love makes them drool,

Sure, I wouldn't have believed it.
3 Goodness, what dirty tricks
He wanted to try first yesterday.
Lord, how the Bridegroom had to rage,
Before he wanted to serve her.
4 Oh, oh, they say, come Players,
Help me, help me, I am in trouble,
The Bridegroom wants my virginity
Oh! what advice? Please assist me.
5 I swear even if it cost me my neck,
It did not go as recklessly as it seemed.

label: wedding
translation:
Now indeed, you who are here  
Guests at the wedding, of a virtuous pair,  
Let us joyfully spend this time,  
In sweetness together,  
But not in joy that brings excess,  
Only such joy as God permits.  

2 We have altogether  
Double cause and reason for cheerfulness,  
To praise God's name,  
Who through these two has prepared this marriage,  
Wishing that His goodness  
May steadily remain with her.  

3 And that they may live in peace  
In all that God's gracious hand provides her,  
Never straying from paths of virtue,

label: wedding
translation:
Sweet Maidens, stove fires,  
Dear Sandys,  
At the Lovers' door,  
Though you with sharp words  
Often come to kill  
With the sharp and keen No.  
2 But if you might once taste  
Some amusement  
That the faithful kiss drags after itself:  
I wager that you with both your hands,  
To those bonds,  
At the first request would grasp:  
3 While the Maidens whole nights,  
Full of thoughts,  
Often ponder to toss about,  
And sprinkle  
Their lap with joy’s dew.

label: wedding
translation:
Sweet Maidens, Stoke-fires,  
Valuable Saints,  
At the Lovers’ gathering,  
Although you with stern words,  
Often come to murder  
Through the sharp and keen No.  
2 But if you once might taste,  
Some amusement  
That the faithful kiss drags after itself:  
I wager that you with both your hands,  
To those bonds,  
At the first question grasped:  
3 While the Maidens whole nights,  
Full of thoughts,  
Often muse about tossing,  
And sprinkle  
Their lap with joyful dew.

label: wedding
translation:
I was sailing on the Rhine,  
I was there all alone,  
Only with a Saly leaf  
Black brown Magdalene,  
That was my little ship:  
How do you do, my Lord of Switsenburg,  
What good cheer do you bring, lyre, lyre,  
How do you do, ah, ah, ah  
Switsenburg, lyre, lyre,  
How do you do.  

2 And when I came ashore  
I was there all alone,  
Then all the little roosters crowed,  
Black brown Magdalene,  
And the bright day arrived;  
How do you do, etc.  

3 I came before my love’s door,  
I was there all alone,  
The little door was closed,

label: wedding
translation:
Not only to sleep
When God created Adam,
He also created
Eve, who slept beside him;
So that through Man and Woman,
The earthly lineage would increase,
Through the sweet, through the sweet, sweet, sweetly caressing,
That Bride and Bridegroom do.
2 Thus we have all together
Led forth Man Adam:
He was the first Father
Of the human Race,
From whom came Enoch, Lot, and Abraham,
Isaac, Jacob, Joseph,
Through the sweet, through the sweet, etc.
3 We are then by nature,

label: wedding
translation:
Tsa Trumpets and Trumpets  
Blow on a golden tone:  
Now Trumpet the laws of War  
Leave for Venus' Son,  
He pulls the steel from the body  
For his weapon a Woman:

label: wedding
translation:
Hey and do not be so sad,  
Let the joy come from above,  
It does not belong at any wedding celebration  
That one is sorrowful in spirit:

label: wedding
translation:
We come to sing,
For today is the time,
Not for precious things,
But for gain and for profit;
And we sing with the four of us,
For amusement and for pleasure,
To get through it all,
Through this extraordinary spree.
2 We wish everyone,

label: wedding
translation:
Friends want to arrange  
Some verses together,  
I pray no one grumbles,  
But prepare your voices.  
This little songbook,  
At the Groom's request,  
To be sung once, oh these things,  
Sweet as cake.  
O Bacchus’ tear,  
As sweet as cream or Zaan!  
No nectar can compare to you,  
You surpass in delight.  
A large pot full of rice and porridge.  
Oh! Oh! how you taste to me.  
2 Tsa Witteling wants to rinse  
Two flutes fresh and fine:  
For everyone must express their feelings  
Here by singing of the wine;

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Every person by nature  
Is fond of their own existence  
And fears death.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Every person gladly had happiness and peace,  
Freed from all disaster and woe,  
On earth, and also in Heaven.  

2.  
This is what we firmly wish each other.  
One never tires of wishing well.  
To which I also add my own.

label: wedding
translation:
1.
O how wise, and well-considered
Is that person, who directs all his deeds,
Words, and thoughts
Towards the blessed end of his life;
Who, taken by virtue's desire,
To obtain this end,
And so that he does not lose it,
Chooses help and good means.

label: wedding
translation:
Beautiful Jsabel,  
Understand my reason well,  
Help your servant out of the sorrowful trouble,  
Who day and night,  
With sighs and lamentation,  
Has stood at your door so many a night.  

All that you do,  
It is all lost,  
Depart from here, oh lover, with haste,  
Remain who you are,  
I keep my solitude,  
For I am too humble and you far too rich.  

But beautiful Goddess,  
Let go of your stubborn will,  
Even if I am rich, just show me again,  
My heart and goods  
I give to you with haste,  
As a true lover does to his beloved.  

Well, comrade,  
Tell me once truly,  
Why do you cast your eyes on me,  
Or is it for my honor,  
So true I am in faith,  
Therefore you wish to leave and never come back.  

Brown Magdalen,  
It is for your sweet appearance,  
For your brown eyes are like two stars,  
Your breasts pale,  
And your red mouth,  
Have so sorely wounded my young heart.  

Your sweet speech,  
Like a nightingale,  
I know all the young men do the same,  
It is only for the flower  
Of the daughters' maidenhood,  
And then the young men go and bear their fame.  

My pleasant love,  
I do not leave you in disgrace,  
There is a ring of diamond;  
And therefore do not let it go,  
Much has already happened,  
For you are the bond of all my sorrow.  

Now be joyful,  
Lover in honor and virtue,  
There is my loyalty and also my young youth,  
Come, let us go,  
All your sadness is gone  
Because through all your grief you have received comfort.  

Blow the loud horn,  
Isabel is the bride,  
Now stir shawms, drums, and flute,  
Wish this pair  
Luck in public,  
Who come together in honor and virtues.

label: wedding
translation:
1. Playmates, all together,  
Now want to drink clearly,  
Come pourer, give a Fluyt,  
To the bridegroom and bride,  
Drink neatly from your glass,  
And let the Roemers clink,  
In honor of this pair,  
Who are now with each other.  

2. Playmates, be glad,  
Want to clear your throat,  
Be cheerful in your actions,  
And give each other a kiss,  
Playmates, I wager that you  
Also sometimes like to crawl,  
On the sweet bedstead,  
And extinguish where you burn.  

3. Now bride, be at ease,  
The time will soon reveal,  
What you so desire,  
You shall receive tonight,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
When I think of this marriage  
That is firmly attached today,  
It seems to me that I hear everyone here say:

2.  
‘God gives His blessing to this couple,  
Already a little child within the year.’  
And then everyone begins to go

3.  
To sing joyfully from the bottom of their hearts.  
Now raise the glass to your mouth,  
Then one sometimes plays good tricks and good rounds.

4.  
Be cheerful, friend, with decency,  
And give the girls a kiss once in a while.  
And do not be unwilling and let it happen.

5.  
Ay see, ay see, how the bride is adorned!  
Ay see, how slowly she undresses,  
She still knows so little what it means:

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
How wondrous are the works  
Here on the earth’s ground;  
It is worth noting  
How things turn around:  
How the young people  
Join together.  
One knows what it means:  
The two make a pair.  
It may rightly happen,  
One cannot forbid it,  
When they enter together  
Into a lawful marriage bond.  
I wish them love and peace,  
May God keep them healthy.

2.  
Now that we, with companions,  
Also enter that state,  
I must tell you  
How it goes with our friend:  
He will soon get married,  
They are making a big celebration;  
Shortly there will be a wedding,  
And joyful spirits.  
Look, there he goes:  
How may that girl weep,  
That she with such a fellow,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
How wonderful are Straelen's sprays  
The Sin of Righteousness,  
And comes, like a Bridegroom, stepping,  
Most beautifully, out of the Bedroom,  
To me, his well-chosen Bride!  
Yes, come Lord. I stand ready,  
The Glow of my  
Lamp that flickered Yes, it shines,  
It is trimmed,  
And filled full with Oil.

label: wedding
translation:
I.
I feel dear desire inwardly pressing my soul,
To sing in spirit a marriage song
Of God's Son and his beloved Bride:
A great mystery from which so much salvation springs!
If only my heart and tongue were as skillful
As Solomon's and David's, who once before
Sang, in a divine manner,
Their Song and Psalm of this marriage bond.

label: wedding
translation:
Fair bride, receive this crown  
On your head and blonde hair,  
Rich with roses and laurel leaves,  
Oh, how beautiful love falls here!  
Who can tell its powers,  
And the flames it kindles?  
The most combative war heroes  
Fall too lightly before its arrow.

label: wedding
translation:
O Bridegroom, I see the hour approaching  
When you will bathe in wealth.  
I see how your heart now burns, but for  
that flame it cannot harm.

label: wedding
translation:
Could the burning fire of love ever  
be extinguished? No, let us not  
believe that, although the winter is cold and harsh.  

The fading sacrificial flame  
could not remain burning forever, although that temple,  
choir and walls, still glowed, each time nourished anew,

label: wedding
translation:
Could the burning fire of love
ever be extinguished? No, let us not believe that,
Though the winter is cold and harsh.

The glowing sacrificial flame
could not burn forever, Though the temple,
choir and walls, Blazed, yet still fed,

label: wedding
translation:
1.
What a wonderful life it is  
To be the Groom and Bride  
And to drink the cool wine,  
And to drink the cool wine.  
One might think, one would marry on purpose,  
Just to also have a Wedding trip.  
Playmates, follow the Bride,  
If you please, it doesn't hurt.

2.  
The first man and Eve  
They married each other:  
If you please, then follow her,  
If you please, then follow her.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Dear, hey, braid a wreath of Myrtle and Violets,  
For behind these hedges I had hidden myself.  
Hey, breeze, hey, breeze my youthful flower.  
Here, under these Willows and Poplar trees.  
Mars and Venus play, caught by Love  
And do not complain, if you wish to extend my little deer.  
My little sheep that roam hill and dale,  
But Rose, your beauty, you will find here already.  

2.  
Come now Ganymede, pour Nectar for the Gods,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Good evening, all that crowd.  
Sir, how wonderfully busy it is here.  
Do not be afraid, I am little Cupid.  
Won’t you buy some sulfur matches  
Or something else from my little box?  
Merchants, look freely at my stall:  
Would you like a blushing apricot  
Or a rose sweet with fragrance?  

2.  
A thousand other beautiful things  
I have in my bag:  
Would you like songs to sing,  
I have ones you have never read;  
Or does someone want to buy books,  
I have all kinds here:  
This is a little book, open it once,  
I bet you have never seen anything stranger.  

3.  
It is the charming wedding cake,  
Much sought after and well desired,  
Where many a clever fox  
Has often wasted his time.  
Here is another rarity,  
Nonpareil, it’s something unusual,  
It’s a tasty little sweet,  
Come on, lie down with the candle.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Hey, see the Bride, ribbon band with ornaments,  
Encircled with maiden faith,  
Longs to soon satisfy her desires.  
She blushes and the green turns dull!  
Well Nymphs, quickly, want to spread her the coach!  
Expose the bed of fresh green!  
Let these two there satisfy their desires,  
Quietly with their kisses.

2.  
But hold, say, don’t let him come so quickly  
To his beloved Bride!  
These lessons must first be properly taken:  
Bind him out of the room  
As long as he does not promise your sweet request  
Or whatever you desire for sweetness.  
If he promises it, hey, the maidenhood is worth  
The little pussy’s flesh.

3.  
If the pair is in bed, then no need is lost.  
Every couple sharpens their minds:  
Here is the right hour born of joy!  
What a playful handclap!  
Quiet, be still, the playmates will say it!  
Those are two breasts full of spirit  
That will well lay the entry of the joy of the feast.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Marrying the old way again!  
I don’t know any sleeves on this coat  
Whether it’s Hansje, Fransje, Tys or Bouwen,  
With Giertje, Kniertje or pretty Els.  
Hey, listen, Pleuntjen,  
My own buddy,  
It’s the old tune,  
I say it out loud:  
Here is a bride again.  

2.  
Already with four little feet on a bed!  
My little comrade, listen here,  
I’d bet a Daelder,  
Giertje has no Beguine’s flesh:  
A sweet comforter  
She now takes on,  
In the real monastery  
She now wants to go  
Where two pairs of shoes stand.  

3.  
I must highly praise that little sheep

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Pleasant maidens, happily go on  
Singing,  
Jumping,  
Just as it should be.  
Hey, lively one, cheer up,  
Be joyful and sweet!  
Or if anything is missing, just say a word!

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Woman bride, Woman bride, sit up and keep watch!  
There are only a few hours left,  
The end of your Bridal time approaches,  
Your union must last longer.  

2.  
You then enter the great Guild,  
The Snare, where so many long for,  
Where many a fault, too loose, too wild,  
And thoughtless, become trapped;  

3.  
Where many a maiden is judged  
Or lets herself be bought by some adornment,  
But when she comes to herself,  
She would wish to run away.  

4.  
We hope for better nature from you  
And wish that both of you  
- As well pleased in love gathered -  
Will never wish to part.

label: wedding
translation:
1.
Where two beloved ones marry,  
With mutual friends' counsel,  
There may be at the wedding  
Truly a happy company:  
Endorsed joy  
Fits both old age and youth  
Where the couple  
For many years  
Will then rejoice.

2.  
From this day united souls,  
Your loyalty is now fulfilled.  
What follows on your heels:  
A care for the offspring!

label: wedding
translation:
When a ship floats through the seas,
Tossed back and forth,
So is a lover's burden,
Increasingly uneasy,
Until he reaches the good harbor,
Of Venus's Bed,
To refresh his weary soul,
Where he plants his desires.
2. Cupid, that merry lad,
Hops slowly up and down,
And plays on his little flute,
To obtain his desire.
He resembles a king of power,
And has more strength than Juno,
Shows no mercy to anyone,
Mars is not respected by him.
3. He makes everything bow to him,

label: wedding
translation: SA; if you want to make joy: You wedding guests already, And now want to stop sadness, Make a joyful noise, Now that the new couple, Together with each other, Their minds, Now to love, Here are ready. translation:

Good comes after evil.

Torment, pressure, and pain, and cross,

translation: While on Earth nothing happens by chance, unless God, who sees all, rules it from the beginning by His hand and carries it out according to His will. translation:

translation:
What is the Wedding Joy to us

At the celebration that is almost over.

People sang and jumped with joy delighted,
People sang and jumped with joy.
This lasted then as short and as long

The joy went its course with time,
And parted from us:

label: wedding
translation:
The Creator, from whom the things,  
That the eye views with pleasure,  
Received their being,  
Out of a confused nothing:  
Who fashioned the first man of Earth,  
And thereafter,  
According to His wisdom, granted  
With a desired gift,  
That may descend with His blessing  
Upon you, blessed pair,  
Like a fresh rain;  
So that from year to year  
Your fertility may be evident  
In fruits, which in virtue  
And piety resemble you,  
Serving as mirrors for the youth.  
He also blesses the fruits

So that, as other plants,

Through vapor and clouds,  
To praise God for the salvation  
That you receive from Him.  
He blesses you with gifts,  
Which, whether in hardship or distress,  
Will nourish your souls,  
With comfort, until death.

label: wedding
translation:
Maria, Queen Maeght, who rides to marry Saint Anna,

To rest naked and bare

Oh, what a bliss that is!  
A state of greater worth!  
Rightfully for her, as they say,  
A Heaven here on Earth,  
Full of joy and sweetness,  
Like the Garden of Eden,  
Truly before this,  
Where the first pair  
Was completely and utterly delighted.  
That offers profit on all sides,  
In name, in deed, and life!  
First in name (but that is the least)  
She is thereby exalted,  
And marked in the number  
Of all who are counted.

translation:
You are now a sweet girl,

Never (I say) but alone  
Then, when one of you both

translation:

Say it again, yes!
Hey, I pray, come,
Look at your Susie with her gait.
See how they mutually kiss, caress, and stroke each other,

I bet in this state she can find more delight and joy in one hour,
Than in loneliness for a Year, even if it were two.
What is loneliness?
It is, as they say,
Poverty, and that is often described.

label: wedding
translation:

Should be joined on earth by the bond of marriage.  
Now, Lord, you also give me a desire that I long for,  
To enter into the state of marriage (according to your Commandment),  
Then please faithfully support me,  
Set my mind towards  
A holy and God-fearing union;

label: wedding
translation:
Come young Youth, and desire on this day,  
With Christ God's Son,  
He is the peak of all the peak lovers,  
His head is adorned with a crown,  
Decorated and enclosed with glory,  
He is a highly esteemed King,  
Who wields His scepter with power,  
Over the world.  
From Edom comes that great lord, the Lord,  
Whose love shines upon you,  
He is adorned with white sprinkled clothes,  
From Bosra, this valley  
From the Heavenly Throne, through faithful love,

And let that great Mediator,  
Yet comfort conquer.  
He is endowed with the most beautiful gifts,

His curly hair is curly-black like a Raven,  
His eyes like the Sun,  
His cheeks are as round as pomegranate,  
His scent smells like Nard.

label: wedding
translation:
Good luck, Groom and Bride,

I wish that the Lord may make you grow many,  
And that your lineage may bloom like the Olive tree,  
Glorious, beautiful, may God show,  
His faithfulness to you in this union.  
May God the Lord unite you,  
In sincere unity,  
You must steadily find grace,  
Before His Great Majesty,  
I wish that your house may be blessed by Him

I wish His angels constantly to hang around,  
Keeping watch over you by night:  
I wish that the Lord of Lords,  
Through this newly established Marriage,  
May multiply you on Earth,  
Like Abraham his servant,  
Who was made as numerous as the stars,  
Whose Golden Praise was proclaimed so far,  
As the Barens preserve,  
From the Ocean, their path.  
I wish that you may also bear offspring,  
That people may say of you,  
That you imitate Tobias and Sarah,  
On your wedding day,

translation:
BRuydegom and Bruyt together,

To the praise of the Lord see:

label: wedding
translation:

2. You newlyweds well known,  
I present to you a separation song,  
If it may serve as a lesson,  
That you begin and end this state  
In love to God's honor.  
3. Marriage is to be held dutifully;  
For help and comfort by day and night,  
God ordained it,  
So that the human race  
Would grow and increase.  
4. So now be united together,  
Do not let any person come between you;  
Be willing to bear with one another,  
It is of three beautiful parts,  
That please God and man.  
5. Friends, avoid quarrels and fierce strife;  
Where love is small, it arises quickly,  
And often bears misery;  
It would be good if everyone well considered their work  
And acknowledged their faults.

label: wedding
translation:
The Trumpets and Cornets,

He tears the steel from the body:  
For the sidearm, a Woman;  
Makes again trumpets,  
Steel bodies, bold and firm.  
The world was astonished,  
Granted eight months now,  
Where Echo still thunders,

label: wedding
translation:
When I remember the time I have spent with you,  
My spirit trembles, and my conscience shakes,  
And have I nearly outlived the world?  
Yet God be praise and thanks who gives me life.  
It happened in my time that these Netherlands  
Were devastated by violence, be it through robbery, burning,  
Insults, murder, blazing by the power of the Spanish hand,  
As Alva with his might surrounded the Netherlands.  
And did I not witness the fierce siege of Leiden,  
Which caused many people bitter lamentations,  
And furthermore, the Spanish violence used against our Christian strength,  
Throughout the entire Netherlands, almost suffocating in blood.  
But it pleased God to drive her away,  
So it is impossible for me to describe everything:  
The year sixty-five was when I first cried,  
The day of my birth which caused much trouble.  
That I did not at all expect to encounter my coming,  
My old worthy bride, whom I am about to greet,  
She is called Biedes Ians, old three and ninety years,

God be praised and thanked who has granted me these old years,  
Together with this portress to unite her in marriage:  
One hundred eighty-eight we bring together,

Thus I took pleasure in the young girl.  
So I married this cotton lady,  
I thought, what a lucky man I am now,  
I still had a thousand guilders in money,  
Which I gave to my little wife from The Hague.

All to live on my father’s land;  
Oh, sadly there was only a beginning,  
My little wife does not serve as a farmer.  
In the morning, when she was to go milk,  
She milked the milk all into her sleeve,  
And her patterned robe stood,  
Covered with spots smeared with clear cow dung.  
Her little hands up to the elbow,  
They were all smeared by the cow,  
Then she saw that girl from Amsterdam,  
So beautiful as if she came from the land of Egypt.  
Before I had had her two months,  
She looked like a soaked cat,  
Still she drank every day a full one,

Happy you! who now

2. Christ has exalted marriage

You should indeed live the marriage well,

That you give no cause for reproach.  

3. You have taken on this state,

I wish you great happiness,  
I wish you joy in your role.

translation:
Now let us all be thankful,

Thank Him with greater honor.  
Blessed be the Son of God,  
We also want to praise,  
Who frees us from sins,  
We truly are forgiven,  
Who has so kindly provided for us,  
In distress does not abandon us,  
Love Him in this life,  
He will not forsake you.  

3. We also thank the Holy Spirit  
For His visitation,  
For if He had not been with us,  
Our joy would have been tribulation:  
He is a comforter in grief.

translation:
I hear some news in these times,  
I hear some news in these times,

2. But whoever begins on this Earth

Which we accept from those
Who enter into the state of marriage,
Who now commit themselves
To live with their deed

In this state.

The Paradise.
