Or ambushing the finch with a net.
That is best done where no one sees.
The honking of the geese, and the cackling,
Of the hen, are his oracles,
And wondrous spells of Apollo,
Full of liver veins, throats of blowholes,
Latin decorates the apothecary's pots,
Just as the miller adorns the beautiful face.
But holla, hold on ... that serpent of serpents,
Also makes my wrong nuts crack;
I had to lament about true matrimony.
And how the wives, to please,
Bear the world of the husband,
So that it does not perish.
That great porter of the globe,
At our Town Hall, is just a little egg
In the number laughed at by the woman,
Who from evening till morning:
Will care for the world's durability,
And strives for the increase of the people.
Marriage is an old custom
For Adam had a wife out of necessity;
But tell me, how would that go,
If one had to do without the female sex?
Who then would, to gather people,
Ever beat the drum of marriage?
If the female sex also had to live alone,
(Like the poets separate the Amazons,
Sorted apart from the men)
Then it would be best to create the man.
Completely defenseless and without weapons.
Who then cares for the rose wreath?
An Amazon (so to speak)
Is but a bench without a blanket,
And a little candlestick without a candle.
A little field that remains fallow.

label: wedding
translation:
Brings joys to this Wedding feast,  
Wants to sing clearly at once,  
Everyone shows their greatest gifts,  
No one will be forced here,  
The Groom steps to the Marriage altar,  
With his beloved Bride indeed,  
Steps into the marriage elevated,  
Bound by the marriage bond,  
He gives his Bride the right hand,  
To live in love so.  
Steps to the Wedding Bed,  
The Evening Star begins to descend,  
Where love has the upper hand,  
There they will boast blessings,  
If you are filled with days,  
It is all contained in God’s counsel,  
What man has accomplished,  
Lives then according to God’s will and law,  
It is once set in His Book,  
Not always to live here.  
If you want Hymen’s Wedding gift,  
Now light the wedding torch,  
Juno sits on her golden Throne,  
To speak of love and faithfulness,  
She wants no bitterness in marriage,  
But loves love and peace sincerely,  
That is best on earth,  
It is a joy for Husband and Wife,  
To live without pressure and sorrow,  
Who thus gather in joy,  
Friends drink to health,  
Of bride, mother and father,  
And all the friends around,

label: wedding
translation:
He who follows the path of the Ancients,  
Enters the Genuine State,  
Never lets Love grow cold,  
You never yield to disaster or sorrow,  
The Blessing Sun and salvation,  
I wish you never fail,  
To live in Love, God wills to give,  
Long with wellbeing.  
The Bridegroom seems to hesitate,  
The Bride is afraid,  
Oh no, it is just pretense,  
They are fitting the last shoes,  
Although the Bride blushes,  
The Bridegroom is her comfort,  
Willing to live, it will bring joy,  
Through your offspring.  
Here comes Flora, Goddess of the Fields,  
With Juno from her Throne,  
Now the beginning shall be made,  
No gall is allowed here,  
For love must remain standing,

label: wedding
translation:
The Creator of all:
Who has created Man?
According to His Image's will,

label: wedding
translation:
O Blessed Holy Marriage,  
Who has described your praise,  
Humanity maintains you,  
Five thousand years and more,  
Thus is the state of marriage,  
Given to man by God,  
Approach it then with salvation;  
According to God's law and teaching,  
There was no rest before it,  
Because he tenderly embraced her:  
With honor and virtue,  
His heart truly reveals,  
None so eager for honor,  
To taste the loyalty of love,  
He receives the word Yes,  
And holds her dear and true.

label: wedding
translation:
When a ship floats on the seas,  
It is tossed to and fro,  
So are the troubles of a lover,  
Restless more and more;  
Until he reaches the good harbor,  
Of Venus' bed,  
To refresh his weary soul,  
Where he plants his desires.  
Cupid, that cheerful boy,  
Hops steadily up and down,  
And plays on his little flute,  
To obtain his desire;  
He seems a king of power,  
And has more strength than Juno,  
Shows no mercy to anyone,  
Mars is not valued by him.  
He makes everything bow to him,  
Fears neither knight, fire, nor steel,  
Which now Venus will witness,  
Cupid is General;  
He makes him raise the sword,  
He makes him strike the Samade,

label: wedding
translation:
Neighbors, just look out,  
How the bears are jumping,  
Tobert with his bride,  
Oh, what things indeed,  
It is no joy,  
Everyone appears very delighted.  
Hey, look at Tobert’s father,  
How he fusses with that little barrel  
And makes a lot of gestures,  
How he eyes that little bottle,  
Full of French wine,  
Oh dear! He wants to have a sip,  
Full of French wine,  
Now it’s time to be sweet.  
So then, talk, Tijs,  
Cheerfully stir your hands  
And cut some zeal,  
I’m salivating,  
Now a flute,  
Fall out with klink, klink, klorie,

label: wedding
translation:
BYget in our neighborhood is full of joy,  
Wangt Crelis Jangs’ Maertje is the bride,  
With Lou Jan Ceessen’s Piet, who in his youth,  
In all companies always stands out!  
Though old in years,  
He asks again,  
It seems his old age  
Gets its first joy back.  
Get Neel, you must be my playmate from today,  
Because today they are getting married;

label: wedding
translation:
If there is ever joy  
Here on Earth, it is Love,  
Provided there is nothing so delightful  
For Body, Heart, Soul, and Mind,  
As the Love that binds  
Faithfully between Man and Woman,  
Which, upheld by Virtues,  
Is the support of the earthly structure.  
For as infinite  
As the joy of Love,  
So too is its power on Earth necessary:  
For everything exists within it:  
There love is the sweetest sweetness,  
There love is the highest good:  
No one on Earth is miserable  
Who does not nurture Love.  
Ah, how exalted  
In happiness, united Two,  
Must henceforth your whole life  
Be, in Love and Holy Peace,  
How perfect is now your desire?  
Now your worries are dispelled,  
And your suffering extinguished.  
Thus spend the Days  
Of your pleasant Spring,  
So that you may bear fruit  
That no bitter discord spoils;  
For if Love always finds you,  
And you always cherish Love,  
You will one day see those happy days,  
Where you find eternal Salvation.

label: wedding
translation:
Afterwards Trijntje’s cheeks blush,
And with roses

label: wedding
translation:
Now dawns the joyful hour,  
That will heal the two loving hearts  
From their sorrows,  
According to the laws of nature.  
Hymen lights his torches  
As a sign of her passion,  
And rejoices with his poems  
At the wedding chamber.

Thus saw the Italian youth  
Prince Anthony marry Cleopatra  
On the Egyptian waters;  
These echo with his joy.  
He melted in the bliss of love,  
And scorned the Roman crown,  
When he caressed her beauty  
On Astarte's wedding throne.

Thus saw brave Greece  
The conquered Alexander  
Bowing before the lover,  
In the sight of the army’s shore,  
Ah, who can escape love,  
Who once saw all Asia  
Destroyed by Europe  
For the love of Helen!

label: wedding
translation: An object of joy, a kingdom, and a royal wife.

label: wedding
translation:
Bridegroom and Bride who today have entered  
Into the state of marriage,  
I wish that love, peace, and loyalty  
Always prevail with you.  
Love is regarded as the highest of virtues here on earth  
(With good reason):  
For it gives the greatest joys where virtues  
Are at their full strength.  
And her sister, noble Peace, if she finds  
A place in both your hearts,  
She will drive away quarrel and hasty disputes  
And make you united.  
Love and peace will faithfully produce and gather,  
Like two hands firmly joined,  
And two minds united, and pleasure,  
Two souls with each other.  
If you have rightly obtained this, with much blessing  
May the Lord of lords richly crown your state, dwelling with you,  
So that He may dispel all bitterness.  
Guests of the celebration, help me wish  
That these people...

label: wedding
translation:
Enamored Nymphs who spend your time,  
The sweetest of your life,  
In sorrowful loneliness waste away,  
If you wish to marry:  
Overthinking it for too long  
Can never quench the desire,  
And, oh Girl, your bloom  
Will fade if you ponder too long.

label: wedding
translation:
Play nymphs help the bride to bed,  
With a move,  
Thus no one's joy is hindered:  
For we have not come here  
To sit dreaming  
Dazed and dull.

label: wedding
translation:
Real couple goes, I pray do not delay  
Let us share a glass of wine with you,  
To bid farewell to all the joy that youth  
Nurtured in your maiden days.

label: wedding
translation:
Sweet Playmates,  
Companions of the Bride,  
Once more your clear little voices,  
Openly with the guests,  
Lead her along in the great Guild,  
Where people are busy quietly with joy;  
Where no maidenflower or little stalks  
Are mutually desired again.  
Do not turn her away,

label: wedding
translation:
Comes sweet youth braids green laurel  
In honor of this newly bonded pair,  
Let your spirit celebrate in pure joy,  
And now wish much happiness and fortune to her,  
Who now unites with him and is content:  
The heart, once recently hardened,  
Soften it through grace.

label: wedding
translation:
If you want to be joyful on my harp,
And play a new wedding song,
You who are gathered here,
Be joyful, all who see this:
As man and woman, in marriage and faithfulness,
As man and woman joined into one in common,
They were two, through marriage become one.
This sweet bond, to God's honor,
Makes the world widely rejoice:
Do not depart from it, but from those teachings,
Turn away from the false footing,
Troublemakers' evil and their counsel,
Troublemakers' evil flee, who burn Scripture,
And who speak ill of the true state.
Of his Bride, his Congregation,
Christ himself is the Bridegroom;
Flesh of his flesh, bone of his bone,
Is every believing person,
The image here of woman and man,

label: wedding
translation:
What is the innocent loneliness?  
What is the chaste maiden's life  
A bitter joy, that laughs and cries,  
A water-bubble, driven by the wind.  
The careful little duckling that in the brood  
Swims alone, finds no amusement;  
Her ward is her head and comfort,  
And if missed, it will soon quack.  
The simple little dove is sad,  
When it must walk unpaired in the cage:  
But when one brings the little drake near,  
How quickly then its beak opens.  
It caws shortly, it spins, it scratches, it digs,  
At once showing a different life:  
It is then very gladly content,  
While it gets kisses and may give them back.  
See Cloris, this is a symbol,  
To show you sweetly  
What joy plays around marrying,

label: wedding
translation:
Sweet girls turn your thoughts to love,  
Which you first offer as your beloved,  
So you may also rest as you wish, in the pleasures  
That your bride now enjoys.  
Is there anything sweeter for people to wish for here?  
No, indeed, it is the sweetness  
That even the greatest of the gods, if necessary,  
Must come down for.  
If you wish to soon proceed to live  
And to act as if you would  
That it happens to you yourself, do not flee,  
So that you will not regret it later.  
For it is often too late then to gain mercy  
From the evil  
That one has constantly committed,  
If you wish to give  
What I have advised.

label: wedding
translation:
We go before you, O ladies ripe with years!  
We go before you, you gentlemen still free!  
Follow, follow us, seek to match us,  
We go before you, what good does it do if you regret it?

label: wedding
translation:
Young hearts of GY gathered here with joy,  
Playthings, your little throats,  
Do not spare like little lambs;  
But once make a sound,  
To the honor of the Bride,  
And Groom, for they are both worthy.

label: wedding
translation:
All things down here  
Almost take a turn,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Come, Christ's friends, lift your eyes,  
Your heart, your soul from here on high,  
To behold the wonder,  
In our love that towards you arises,  
Very free love to His bride,  
Chosen and wished to marry:  
A tone, very beautiful,  
Made in singing, with leaping,  
From your heart,  
To the softening of your pain.

2.  
O blessed hour when Jesus came,  
And looked upon us, and then He took  
Us kindly in His arms,  
When in mercy  
And love He spoke: it is the time  
To have mercy on me,  
He spoke, and broke  
With His words, which charmed,  
Our soul,  
That it fell as if in powerlessness.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
What greater joy can there be,  
For man in earthly life,  
Than the bond of pure faithfulness,  
If one wishes to find the sweetest sweetness,  
One must unite in Marriage,  
The sweetest is between Man and Woman.  

2.  
Words that make the chest glow,  
Kisses that touch the heart,  
Sparks, full of lovely fire,  
Can ease all pain;  
Give a thousand joyful nights;  
Make years feel like an hour.

label: wedding
translation:
NOW it is time to sing,  
And in the round circles  
Once happily to jump.  
Saw now, bass and flute!  
Away now with all your little pains,  
Little hearts.  
Always lasts, sweet little desires,  
Kisses!  
Reinhart now has his Bride  
Finally in the desired boat.

label: wedding
translation:
NOW it is time to sing,  
And in the round circles  
Once joyfully to jump.  
Saw now, bass and flute!  
Away now with all your sorrows,  
Dear hearts.  
Always lasts, sweet little desires,  
Kisses!  
Reinhart now has his Bride  
Finally in the desired boat.

label: wedding
translation:
NOW it is time to sing,  
And joyfully to jump  
In circles round and round.  
Saw on now, bass and flute!  
Away now with all your sorrows,  
Dear hearts.  
Always lasts, sweet desires,  
Kisses!  
Reinhart now has his Bride  
Finally in the desired boat.

label: wedding
translation:
NOW is the time to sing,  
And to joyfully jump around in circles.  
Saw now, bass and flute!  
Away now with all your sorrows,  
Dear hearts.  
Always lasts, sweet little delights,  
Kisses!  
Reinhart now has his Bride  
Finally in the desired boat.

label: wedding
translation:
NOW it is time to sing,  
And in round circles  
Once joyfully to jump.  
Saw on now, bass and flute!  
Away now with all your sorrows,  
Little hearts.  
Always lasts, sweet little desires,  
Kisses!  
Reinhart now has his bride  
Finally in the desired boat.

label: wedding
translation:
Well-ventilated and lovely Amstel city,  
What do you boast of a thousand splendid castles,  
And ships that share all the world's treasures  
In your harbors,  
Or of a town hall, which, raised to Heaven,  
Shines as the eighth wonder,  
And proud churches, and an incomparable splendor  
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
Well-ventilated and splendid Amstel city,  
What do you boast of a thousand grand mansions,  
And ships that share all the world's treasures  
In your harbors,  
Or a town hall, which, raised to heaven,  
Shines as the eighth wonder,  
And proud churches, and an unparalleled grandeur  
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
Well-ventilated and magnificent city of Amsterdam,  
What do you boast of a thousand splendid palaces,  
And ships that share all the world's treasures  
In your harbors,  
Or of a Town Hall that, raised to Heaven,  
Shines as the eighth wonder,  
And proud churches, and an unparalleled splendor  
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
Airy and delightful Amstel city,  
What do you boast of a thousand stately castles,  
And keels, that share all the world's treasure  
In your harbors,  
Or of a town hall, which, raised to Heaven,  
Shines as the eighth wonder,  
And proud churches, and an unparalleled splendor  
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
Well-ventilated and glorious Amstel city,  
What do you boast of a thousand magnificent castles,  
And ships that share all the world's wealth  
In your harbors,  
Or a town hall, that, lifted to Heaven,  
Shines as the eighth wonder,  
And proud churches, and an unmatched splendor  
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
Airy and splendid Amstel city,
What do you boast of a thousand magnificent palaces,
And keels, that share all the world's treasures
In your harbors,
Or of a Town Hall, that, raised to Heaven,
Shines as the eighth wonder,
And proud churches, and an unparalleled splendor
Of dignity and power?

label: wedding
translation:
So Wester delighted,  
So Wester delighted,  
We sing and jump, with joy,  
So Wester delighted,  
While the wedding is prepared,  
So greater, so greater, so greater, so greater,  
The young couple,  
Now want to drink  
And sound fresh,  
So prepare your throat.  
The groom lies,  
The groom lies,  
While he awaits his bride for the match,  
The groom lies,  
The groom lies,  
And firmly holds on to a good night,  
The sweetest, the sweetest, the sweetest, the sweetest,  
To which he clings,  
It is the boy,  
Hastening with the bride,  
To their bed.  
So we then jump,  
So we then jump,  
First hand in hand with every man,  
So we then jump,  
So we then jump,  
And play on a grid and baking pan,  
Let be heard, let be heard, let be heard, let be heard,  
The loudest sound,  
Because who else  
Than Leander  
Gives more than his bride.  
With which he boasts,  
With which he boasts,  
She is the one who ignited his soul,  
With which he boasts,  
With which he boasts,  
And see how his bride winks,  
Sprinkles palm and roses, palm and roses, palm and roses,  
And violet,  
Sprinkles this yard  
And well-regarded,  
Their wedding bed.

label: wedding
translation:
Whoever marries for God's honor,
And begins for his Salvation,
Is blessed by the Lord,
Who so sincerely minds it.

2 Marriage, a point of our rest,
As it rightly may be called,
The pleasure of loving desires,
And Love that is not ashamed.

3 When one binds oneself in marriage;
Here going to unite with a Woman,
One rightly marries there,
Granting the marriage bond.

4 Dear ones, wish to stay together,
Let no sadness be seen here,
Here is the reward of sweet courtship,
Let us wish this couple happiness.

5 Lord Groom and Lady Bride elevated,
I wish you happiness and much prosperity,
That God may give you His blessing,
And His grace in abundance.

6 Groom, wish to love your Bride,
And give honor to the weak,
Therefore always be of one mind,
Live peacefully with her according to God's Teachings.

7 Bride, also wish to strive for this,
And live with your Man in peace,
Wish to call and regard him your Lord,
Just as Sarah did Abraham.

8 I wish that from your offspring,
By God, you may obtain children,
And that by God's grace,
You may raise them for your joy.

9 Those may be a source of happiness for you,
And be very pleasant to you,
Praised at your table and feast,
That we all wish you together.

label: wedding
translation:
Now sing with joyful sound,  
Guests who have gathered here together,  
Come rejoice and praise now already  
In honor of these two, who are paired here,  
And with a joyful spirit,  
Praise the Lord at this feast.  

2 Be cheerful and glad,  
And offer here true happiness,  
With joy and merriment,  
Use it all with gratitude.  
Let sadness not be seen here,  
But let mourning flee away.  

3 Notice with a proper mind,  
How things were in former times,  
How God from the beginning,  
Created man as it is written,  
How everything lay in darkness,  
And how it has now become.  

4 When God had thus created  
The globe of the world according to His desire;  
The animals, small and great,  
He created a master of them according to His will:  
So that he would be like a lord  
Of the cattle of which He was pleased.  

5 God also made,  
A woman from His own flesh and bone,  
Who would be a helper to him,  
For God said it is not good to be alone.  
Thus God devised it so,  
And made a woman from His flesh.  

6 Our bridegroom  
Has deeply considered in his heart,  
To see, after a beautiful flower,  
A remedy for his pain:  
And from the Lord his God,  
Here on earth to his fate.  

7 He has seen one,  
He was often seen to tread this path,

label: wedding
translation:
Nimphjes come, awake now  
And weave a wreath of laurels,  
And therewith adorn the heads  
Of this sweet blessed pair.  
2 Who have entered into marriage together,  
And are firmly bound by faithfulness,  
See how rightly our bridegroom  
Has found his dearest one.  
Address to the Bridegroom.  
3 A worthy pledge, a great treasure  
Born from God for your delight,  
Which scarcely can be understood,  
Chosen from many thousands.  
4 A wife, a wife to your liking,  
A mutual pledge of great value,  
With which you accept the marriage for your rest,  
Through love and faithfulness.  
5 Your second soul, your only fate,  
With whom you hope to live from now on,  
Who from the Lord your God  
Is given to you for your comfort.  
6 Just as Christ the head very well  
Is praised by His congregation,  
So, bridegroom, you must  
Know that she is the head of your wife.  
7 Rule her with good understanding,  
Always be peaceful with her,  
Know that for this reason the marriage bond  
Exists out of love and faithfulness.  
Address to the Bride.  
8 Wife, consider your husband dear and worthy,  
Never speak harsh words to him,  
Remember that you are paired for this reason,  
To live peacefully with him.  
9 Call your husband Lord like Sarah,  
Govern your household in order,  
For if you follow her here,  
You become her daughter.  
10 Always persevere in God’s teaching,  
Let your deeds be honorably begun,  
For through the chastity of women,  
Many a man has often been won.  
Guests to the Bridegroom and Bride.  
11 Be united like Ennias,  
Who had his wife for forty-three years  
Without either of them  
Being quarrelsome.  
12 And if you receive children from the Lord,  
Raise them according to God’s pleasure,  
In His holy teaching,  
That you may see good days from her.  
13 This we all wish for you,  
That God’s grace and great blessing,  
In abundance from year to year,  
Descend upon you like fruitful rain.

label: wedding
translation:
Happy is the person,  
Who, through the Lord's blessing,  
On Earth, after his wish,  
Has received a partner,  
To live peacefully,  
United in unity,  
Such a one God has given,  
A heaven on Earth.  
2 Who not for money or goods,  
But through honorable love,  
Her dearest sweetheart,  
With heart and mind,  
To love, honor, and fear,

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, Bridegroom and Bride,  
You are warmly welcomed,  
To this place,  
And with your company.  

2 You are bound by the bond of marriage,  
By giving your right hand,  
Observe the covenant,  
Made with your mouth.  

3 Be dear to your husband’s head,  
Always obedient in good:  
Love your wife,  
As you love your own body.  

4 The love between husband and wife  
Is a prevention of much quarrel,  
Follow the scripture  
Of Abram and Sarah.  

5 Happiness in the abundance of virtues,  
Happiness must increase your standing,  
Happiness and peace,  
By the end of the year, [come] from one of two.

label: wedding
translation:
The Creator made everything well,  
Created in the beginning, Heaven and Earth,  
With all sweet herbs,  
And also all the livestock very well paired,  
On the sixth day He certainly created  
Man, but he was still alone.  
And lived without a companion,  
In the Garden where all creatures triumph.  
But see, the Lord thought well,  
That He also desired a mate,  
Therefore He made it so,  
That Adam fell asleep.  
God took from his body  
A rib, and made a woman from it,  
Fully suited to the man,  
And also so that he would love her:  
For she was of his flesh and bone:  
Therefore they are two in one.  
Note here, good company,  
How glorious also is the state of marriage,  
For God, with sweet love,  
Has bound her in the spine,  
And said, be fruitful,  
And multiply the human race.  
The Scripture also plainly says,  
That Christ was also pleased with the state of marriage,  
Being a guest at a wedding  
At Cana, as John reports to us,  
He honored the union so much,  
That He turned water into wine.

label: wedding
translation:
The Creator in the beginning  
Created Heaven and Earth,  
Every animal with its family  
He also paired together,  
Man He made from Earth,  
Yet he was still alone;  
Then the Lord considered it important  
‘It is certainly not good  
That I will surely give him  
A helper as well,’  
Then the Lord made a Woman,  
From Adam’s Rib exactly,  
When he was lying,  
In sleep sweet and soft,  
Thus the Man obtained  
A Woman from his kind.  
This Garden, said God indeed,  
Is given to you people,  
Multiply, and be fruitful:  
Wildly cling to each other,  
With love and faithful evenness,  
Then I will surely  
Provide you with a long life,  
Thus spoke the Lord to her.  
Therefore, honorable Bridegroom,  
Will love your Wife,  
Do not be harsh towards her:  
But with understanding and care,  
Treat the Wife as a vessel fragile,  
Give honor to her,  
Then God the Lord  
Will stand by you in all need.  
And you, oh beautiful Bloom,  
Wife Bride, will obey,  
Keep your tongue in check,  
Do not seek dominion,  
Over your Man elevated:  
But be still instead,  
Then you will in this life  
Gain much good and fine.  
If you, O dear Two,  
Seek to live here thus,  
Then God’s peace  
Will always be present.  
And also His rich blessing  
Over you shall enkindle,  
Just as He likewise  
Did to Abram.  
I wish you Bridegroom  
That you may live thus  
With this your beautiful Bloom,  
Then God will exalt her,  
Like a beautiful Vine  
Will extend thus,  
At the Table in a crown  
Your Children shall stand  
Like sweet Olive shoots  
Bloomed very far and white,  
He has spoken well to the Lord,  
To Man,  
Who lives according to His will  
In love and unity,  
They shall be exalted  
To eternity.  
Good fellowship and grace  
Who here are at this Feast,  
Show yourselves joyful and sweet,  
And also cheerful of spirit,  
Playmates both,  
I wish that within a year  
Everyone will see led  
By their honorable Bridegroom.

label: wedding
translation:
Happiness, happiness,  
I wish you both,  
Already in the marital state,  
From the pressure of love,  
You are now very modest,  
To the highest degree,  
Through words of love,  
You are now very exalted.  
But only through God's wise counsel,  
Who grants it to everyone.  

2  
Happiness, happiness,  
I wish you, groom, with reason  
In the marital bond,  
For the pressure of love,  
You will kiss with contentment,  
Your spouse’s rosy mouth,  
Who assists you,  
In all your laborious complaints,  
That you endured at all times,  
Now she will remember.  

3  
Happiness, happiness,  
I wish you, delicate flower,  
Bride in this state,  
The preceding part,  
Your condition shows its change,  
To a higher degree,  
Showing love openly,  
For you both in unity,  
And would like with your dear companion,  
To approach the marital bed.  

4  
Happiness, happiness,  
I wish you both,  
Now truly in matrimony,  
Never heavy burdens  
Should come upon you:  
But within the year,  
A son clearly seen,  
Or daughter from your seed,  
This I wish you honorably,  
By the Lord’s grace.  

5  
Farewell, happiness,  
I wish you all together,  
Who are here at this feast,  
Once more happiness,  
You little playmates by name,  
And that you, without fear,  
With a joyful spirit,  
May receive your groom,  
This I wish you most of all,  
That the time not be long.  
I wish this dear couple,  
Together happiness and peace,  
In the honorable marital state,  
Where they have entered;  
And that God within the year,  
Wills to give them a little sprout,  
A son or daughter ready,  
Healthy and fresh in life.

label: wedding
translation:
Play girls stay,  
Why are you so quiet,  
Who now neither sing nor whistle;  
Why would you  
Also not once want to sing,  
In honor of the Bride?  
This creates dispute,  
Among all these young lads;  
Is your joy completely gone  
I think you would sleep well.  

Playm. Honorable Groom  
That we remain silent in peace,  
And do not offend you,  
This whole crowd;  
Which is full of cheerfulness,  
We listen to her song;  
Sleeping does  
Not harm us, understand this,  
Nor any sadness either,  
Although we sit quietly.  

Groom. Do you then now  
Care to be praised by your beloved,  
Set that now, your mind out,  
Wild with these young ones  
From now on indeed be cheerful,  
Or is the love completely gone  
With this bride,  
Then let me come to her.  
Here no dispute will arise,  
And will not be ashamed of it.  

Playm. No groom proud  
And do not want to talk about it,  
The courting does not  
Our bride here,  
We thus do not abandon her,  
This will not happen lightly,  
First sing a song,  
Nicely with your playmates,  
For you get your dear bride, see  
Do not be so restless on your side.  

Groom. Honorable play girls  
What difference does it make,  
That you now certainly say,  
That I would indeed  
Not sit by my beloved,  
She is after all my pure bride,  
I have with none...

label: wedding
translation:
I thank God the Father wise,
Very praiseworthy,
That He has also given me,
My rightful portion, and dowry,
Where I this hour,
With pure love hope to live.
How many a night
Have I with my beloved in pure love,
With supplication and entreaty,
Before she said yes,
That I would win her love.
I have, like Laban’s servant,
Jacob wise,
Served and loved
My bride these many years
Out of love?
Willing,
To be able to marry her.
Praise God for the time that is accomplished
That I awaited,
I have long been in sorrow,
That I this young shoot,
To my bride
And wife may enjoy.
Lord, grant that this day
May be,
For the salvation of us both,
Grant Your good blessing,
In abundance.
And would extend our love further.
O Lord, grant that this my bride
May be a shoot
Of Zerah’s daughter,
Honorable, friendly, wise and prudent,
Quiet and sweet,
Praised as a virtuous woman.
Would give me more and more,
Wisdom Lord,
So that I may govern well,
My household, and good housewife
Friendly and sweet,
As an honorable man with honor.
Grant that we may be capable
To live together,
Joyful as Jacob and Rachel:
And certainly as Tobias.
With Zerah pure,
In sincere love kindly.
Excuse me friends least and most
Here at the feast,
I wish that God will guide you
With His blessing without burden
For the happiness
That you wished for us both.

label: wedding
translation:
Marriage is a good thing,  
Instituted by the Lord,  
On Earth a Heavenly delight,  
As Scripture teaches us:  
For those two were before us,  
Praised on Earth,  
Man and Woman, living closely,  
As one through Love and loyalty.  
God made the first Man,  
Exalted in Paradise,  
So it is no small matter:  
As we find it written:  
For God, the Lord of honor,  
Makes from the lump of Earth,  
The faithful Man, then the Woman,  
Closely from this Man’s Rib.  
Therefore Friends, do not doubt,  
That Marriage pleases the Lord,  
For Christ, through His Wounds, sees  
Those who wish to honor it,  
When He at the Wedding Feast  
Was invited, He showed the first  
Wonders at Cana’s fine place,  
Turning Water into Wine.  
Therefore Bridegroom and Bride honorable,  
Will begin Marriage with God,  
Just like Abraham and Sarah soon,  
Will love each other,  
And like Isaac cheerfully,  
With his Rebecca kindly,  
And Tobias certainly, with Sarah pure,  
Live in the earthly realm.  
Bridegroom N: N: honorable,  
Will so live with your Wife,  
Just as these Forefathers there  
In the Olden times below,

label: wedding
translation:
What wonders the Creator wisely made,  
In the Garden of Eden,  
Or in Paradise,  
Of herbs and flowers,  
And all the creatures therein alike,  
Fish in the stream,  
And cattle in the clover grass.  
Even more wonders  
Are seen here yet,  
Everyone who is at peace  
Has their counterpart;  
The Creator of value  
Certainly makes man in his image  
From the clay of the earth,  
But see, he was still alone.  
Then God thought highly,  
It is truly not good  
That man should live  
Without a counterpart:  
Therefore He caused  
Adam to fall asleep with one,  
Then God took  
One of his ribs, common.  
Then God made alike  
From this rib a woman,  
To live with her,  
Faithfully in love,  
God also spoke in honor  
Be fruitful, it was said,  
And increase  
From now on the human race.  
Thus God exalted  
The first pair united,  
To live together,  
With love unburdened,

label: wedding
translation:
Lovers of the cool wine,  
Who are at the Feast of Bacchus,  
Together with you, nymphs proud,  
With joy and playfulness;  
Come now freely with a flower wreath,  
And join us now in the dance,  
And now Rosinde is the bride,  
Playing on violins with sound;  
Those gathered here,  
All in the cool wine.

label: wedding
translation:
Away with you who are inclined to be single,  
It is better to be paired,  
Than estranged from all friends,  
To live lonely on this Earth:  
For paired,  
For paired,  
Is the greatest joy on Earth,  
Is the greatest joy on Earth.  
The state of marriage always praised,  
Is also worthy of all honor,  
By the Creator Himself for this  
Established and declared right:  
For paired,  
For paired,  
Is the greatest joy on Earth.  
Much happiness then to those united,  
Be blessed on this Earth,  
Groom with your beloved  
Bride who is rightly worthy of you:  
For paired,  
For paired,  
Is the greatest joy on Earth.  
All the days of your life,  
May the Lord on Earth  
Show you His assistance,  
So that it remains declared to you:  
That paired,  
That paired,  
Is the greatest joy on Earth.  
Never must any misfortune  
Come upon this Earth,  
From your marriage bear fruits,  
Worthy of all praise and honor:  
Be paired,  
Be paired,  
With the greatest joy on Earth.

label: wedding
translation:
1 ONE Groom as a Hero:  
You must stand guard at night in the field;

label: wedding
translation:
Comes Singing Goddesses to Your Throne,  
With Parnassus by Venus' Son,  
Now sings dear Wedding Throne,  
Of Prince Weilburg with his Consort,  
Carolina, the Dutch Friend:  
Married under State and pure Love.  

Put off the mourning, you Netherlands!  
Of Your Deceased Governor,  
Here comes soon a joyful scene,  
The swift Fame that announces to us  
The marriage of Nassau's scion,  
That Carolina is the Bride.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
We all, who are gathered here,  
Must be joyful in spirit:  
To draw a line together,  
On this honorable Feast,  
And sing with a joyful sound:  
To the honor of Groom and Bride;  
Hurray! hurray! hurray.  

2.  
O Bride and Groom, so worthy!  
For your family and all  
Who are gathered here together.  
Have now a blessing?  
That we with heart, with soul and mouth,  
Wish you, from pure ground,  
Happiness! happiness! happiness.  

3.  
We wish that the All-Seeing Eye,  
Shines upon you from all around  
And sends its blessing from above:  
That it steadily descends;  
Like a gentle rain down!  
This we wish, and even more?  
Happiness! happiness! happiness.  

4.  
That is to say, within the year:  
A shoot that grows abundantly,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
All the Seven States-Companions,  
Bound together by Love;  
Are delighted about this March,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Here poured into a glass,  
In honor of this young couple,  
And drained to the bottom;  
To good success within the year.  

2.  
The brave bride will become a mother:  
Oh, see, the bride is about to deliver;  
One can read it from her being;  
Do you agree too, say groom.  

3.  
He nods, yes, I help wish this,  
And all that is sincere at once:  
Become the happiest of all people,  
Live many years in peace and quiet.  

4.  
Who will now support our bride;  
When the time comes to go,  
Playmate, you must accompany her,  
Girls have done their duty.  

5.  
Bring her freely to the bed,  
But be careful, no further hindrance,  
The rest that we still expect:  
Let the groom take care of it.

label: wedding
translation:
What is your little soul, O Doris! so afraid,  
My influence has moved the heart of Clorimene!  
Freedom has been taken from her by my power:  
Yes, you waste away because of the glow of her beautiful eyes.

label: wedding
translation:
Goddess! who sets my mind ablaze with love,  
I wither from love's glow for Chlorimene!  
My bride! my little soul's love! always entices me,  
To the shining lily-white, and the blushing red,  
Where do you lead me, o rightful goddess, my little soul;  
Caress me, o dear angel! in your bosom.

label: wedding
translation:
Let us, my Friend! My chosen one!  
O Doris! who robs my Freedom,  
Let our love always be heard by each other;  
That discord never extinguishes the flame.  
You have my Heart and I have yours:  
O Juno! bind them firmly as one,  
Request prosperity in the marriage  
Of Doris! and of Chlorimen.

label: wedding
translation:
Determine, O Brother, now your suffering;  
You overcome your chlorine!  
The Goddess of Love grants you her heart,  
And joy: against doubtfulness.

label: wedding
translation:
O Doris, thank Love,  
Your bride who grieved you,  
Also fades from a little love.  
Cease, Doris, your suffering,  
Your blonde Clorimeen.

label: wedding
translation:
JUyg, Sweethearts Juyg, entertain each other,  
Caress each other's heart and ears with song;  
And let both your pleasures be heard  
Before Juno's sacred altar:  
Both blaze in devoted love, and taste the sweetness of love,  
In full abundance.

label: wedding
translation:
Oh Sun! drive away the nasty Night,  
From my enamored senses;  
For dear Phyllis! whom I await;  
And eternally hope to love, ://:  
Will honor me with the sweetness,  
Of mutual love in abundance,  
To joy in my life. bis.  
Oh Sun! drive away the nasty night;  
For dear Phyllis whom I await,  
Will give me the yes. ://:

Let no rainstorm or wind,  
Delay my Phyllis:  
So that my soul finds no cause,  
To complain again and anew ://:  
Rise joyfully with Your noble radiance,  
Up to the high Heaven's expanse!  
Then I will live happily. bis.  
Oh Sun, quickly drive away the night,  
For lively Phyllis whom I await,  
Will then give me the yes. ://:

label: wedding
translation:
Come Damon come,  
My Bridegroom!  
Come to me in these regions.  
Be of good cheer,  
I know your passion,  
My harshness has softened.

label: wedding
translation:
O True Sun! target of Love!  
Scatter your Golden sparks,  
Let Lyris, and his soulmate,  
Proudly display their marriage vows:  
Lead, in the company of your radiance,  
Peace and playful joy to the dance,

label: wedding
translation:
Descend prosperity, descend and come to the Feast,  
You can increase the Bridegroom's joy,  
Your balm creates a joyful spirit,  
And turns the heart to Love:  
Strengthen for the well-being of the Pair, with your Velvet hand,  
This chaste Marriage bond.

label: wedding
translation:
Wants to delight this happy couple greatly,  
Love, which already governs their well-being;  
Make them flourish through your influence,  
Where virtue crowns their heads with laurel!  
Let their lives be inseparable,  
And strive for many more years,

label: wedding
translation:
What a sweetness, Bis.  
Bear the kiss-like marriage life:  
There the mind, Bis,  
Always renewed delight flows;  
Lyris has achieved his wish,  
Rightly he rejoices in that blessing,  
In the glow, Bis,  
That makes him fall in love again.

label: wedding
translation:
Now a glass emptied all around,
For the bridegroom is getting ready:
Good rye and long health
Turelure
May this love last long,
Robin Turelure.

label: wedding
translation:
Whoever wants to follow Love,  
I prefer to sweep cleanly,  
Especially when I may empty the glasses outside:  
One caresses their sweetheart, full of desire,  
While she kindly strokes and kisses,  
Like these two paired;  
I love the sweetness of the Wine,

label: wedding
translation:
HYmen come to adorn this Faithfulness,
From the high Helicon:

label: wedding
translation:
Much health and blessing,  
I wish these two: may luck never oppose them,  
In their united state:  
May they never suffer melancholy,  
No stomach or loin pain,

label: wedding
translation:
The God of Love who never spares time  
To seize hearts by force,  
Who with tireless diligence,  
Seeks to enchant soul and senses,

label: wedding
translation:
See, oh see the Groom kissing,  
Now time and love command him,  
To quench the fire of his flame,  
That she first showed him in the distance,  
Now he will taste the joy,  
That marriage gives,  
To blaze with love for each other  
Through an irresistible glow.

See, oh see the Bride once glance!  
Who embarrassed by honor’s shame,  
The lightning of the sparks of love;  
Reflected by her Groom:  
It is no jest, do not fear,  
Fresh Bride it will go well,  
Tomorrow you will be wiser,  
Yes, at every marriage window.

Feel the west wind blowing,  
Calm couple how Youth springs!  
See the wine foam in the glasses,  
And the table dances with joy,

label: wedding
translation:
NOW at least with Congratulations,  
Once sung for this Couple,://:  
That their Youth,  
Full of delight  
May always increase through joy,  
And its Fame never fade,  
But always grow Year after Year.  

That they may flourish in Wealth and Prosperity,  
In a House full of abundance, ://:  
And their Table  
Is always  
Well supplied with Meat and Fish:  
While the Glasses glow  
To the brim, bleeding with Grapes.

label: wedding
translation:
O marriage bond full of pure morals!  
O unbreakable loyalty,  
You may rightfully bear the name  
Of a renowned magnificent edifice.  

Love and peace are the foundations  
That bear and carry this vessel,  
So that it is never violated  
By envy and time, which gnaws at everything.  

Duty and piety are the walls,  
Raised to the top by diligent effort,  
To endure all storms  
Of adversity and struggle.  

The conscience aware of no deceitful acts,  
And unrestricted joy,  
Are spacious halls full of decorations,  
Dwellings for sincere virtue.  

The front gate, which is closed at night  
Against evil, is true humility,  
The roof that can withstand it all  
Is patient endurance prepared for pressure.

label: wedding
translation:
Let us now, with wedding sounds,  
Thank love for her favor,  
For this well-joined marriage,  
Through which mutual generations  
Cannot await with lust,  
Free from pressure and regret.  

See how the pair discover each other  
Through stroking, kissing, glancing,  
Now the eager hour has struck  
To reach the goal of love  
And to enjoy the joy  
That makes the bride a woman.  

But the groom, full of longing,  
Wishes to receive his bride through love,  
And the beautiful bride blushes.  
Fresh maiden, now do not fear,  
Tomorrow you will be wiser,  
There you will find the need for love.

label: wedding
translation:
I value Trouw far above  
d'Eenzame Levenstyd,  
Rightly can I praise her,  
I am delighted in my heart:  
I enjoy the sweet Love  
With my Soul's Friend,  
Both in my House and Gardens,  
Thus I live according to my desire.  

What more can I wish for,  
If I am well content,

label: wedding
translation:
Prepare yourself,
And make yourself ready:
To celebrate this feast, and adorn wreaths,
With the sweet name
Of two newly joined in their vow to proclaim their marriage.
Blow now, O Fame,
Koridon's Triumph, who comes to find his beloved
Full of love;
Offers his right hand,
To gain his friend as Wife
In the bond of faithfulness,
To receive her favor,
Whereby his pain is,
After much longing, once healed.

label: wedding
translation:
O you Miser! O you Slaves!  
Who have buried yourselves  
In perishable metal:  
When your sperm has run out,  
You can never buy life  
For all your treasures combined.  

All your sweat, all your toil  
Serves to diminish pleasure.  
It is as the proverb says:  
Great wealth will make us stumble  
In trying to reach a great treasure:  
Wealth does not prevent greed.  

So, my Songstress, let us withdraw  
From the courts, from the rich,  
From the constant pomp and splendor:  
Let us, with the farmer's lads,  
Praise the herding of the sheep,  
And cover ourselves with a cloak.

label: wedding
translation:
Do not now spare a string or joyfully strum,  
But faithfully follow the voice of the noble Lute:  
The Song, united with the playing,  
Depicted how Groom and Bride,  
United by loving minds,  
Shall live rightly through love.  

Old age, deprived of its green,  
Now feels a new Spring.

label: wedding
translation:
Happiness!  
That pressure,  
You never experience again;  
A steady blessing  
Drops upon your loyalty:  
Live in all peace,  
Young man and woman;  
May the day of today  
Never bring regret!  

The fleece,  
The yield,  
Of lambs and cows,  
May they overflow!  
By a full stable,  
May Heaven add  
Children in number,  
But true pleasure  
Above all.

label: wedding
translation:
Well, what kind of life is this?  
So many tables set;  
The house full of neighbors, nieces, nephews,  
Where I look, I see joy.  
Would one also hold a wedding here?  
Does one also dance a bride to bed?  

But what talk! Who thinks of marriage?  
Everyone is afraid that England  
Will carry away wife and children:  
Reading the post-newspaper  
Dims the flame of marriage;  
Courage, not love, prevails.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, folks, all of you.
We wish much blessing on the young couple.
Thank you all, come, take a seat around,
But hey, offer something to eat!
Come on now, said Aaltje, bring it to us.
You are right about that; I'll bring it to you, man.
Don't hold back on sugar, or is it too sweet?
It's tasty; it's just as it should be,
Now, would you like cake? or crispbread?
Or speckled treats? please choose something!
The one most used gives me the most pleasure;
There is the tobacco, the pipes are here.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, folk, all of you.
We wish much blessing to the young couple.
I thank you all, come, sit around,
But kyers, offer something to the mouth!
But now, says Aaltje, bring it to us.
You are right about that; I'll bring it to you, man.
Don't spare the sugar, or is it too sweet?
It's tasty; it's just how it should be,
Now blow the cake? or round rusk?
Or Spikkelatie? oh, do choose something!
The one most used gives me the most pleasure;
There is the tobacco, the pipes are here.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
The beauty of my soul's beloved,  
The radiance of my Nanette;  
Makes my heart blaze with Love:  
To whom I must eternally cling,  
To the object of my affection…repeat.  

2.  
Her clear voice, her lovely grace,  
Her shining little eyes sparkling bright:  
Her eyes pour into my tender heart,  
An indescribable sorrowful pain;  
Every day, hour by hour…repeat.  

3.  
My soul consumes itself in her glow,  
When I may touch her hand:  
Does not want to increase my pain,  
Nor burn away in her bosom,  
Oh, that does my soul so well….repeat.  

4.  
Oh, sparkling star that pierces me,  
Cease to shine any longer:  
Does not want to increase my pain,

label: wedding
translation:
1  
As Luciaan, that fair Shepherdess,  
Drove her chosen cattle to the field,  
Her Damon came to meet her,  
And he greeted her tenderly.       (repeat)  

2  
Go greet your dear Soulfriend,  
Oh, may I truly love you,  
For your beauty that shines upon me,  
Has captivated me with love.       (repeat)  

3  
For your little eyes like coral,  
Oh, if only I could paint your beauty,  
And your sweet little nose like ivory,  
Your beauty resonates throughout the world.       (repeat)  

4  
And your sweet fair cheeks,  
That are adorned with red roses,  
And your little neck like a Swan,  
You are even more beautiful than Diana.       (repeat)  

5  
Oh dear fair Shepherdess,  
Grant me your pure love:  
I shall always be faithful to you,  
As a true Man to his Wife.       (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
1  
What sweet joy,  
In my youth,  
What sweetness one has received,  
What a pleasure,  
I feel here,  
I live at ease in many dangers,  
I have married a sweet maiden,  
Who loves me in return,  
That sweet child,  
Considers me very well,  
She will always be beloved by me.  

2  
We are paired,  
Gathered in love,  
Not for money, but for the good life,  
Whatever meets us,  
Be it bitter or sweet,  
We are constant, preservation is also necessary,  
For the Creator who lives there,  
Gives everyone their food.

label: wedding
translation:
1.
Happy esteemed Prince and Princess,  
On this pleasant occasion,  
With the upcoming marriage:  
Everyone is delighted,  
Because the Prince receives that beautiful jewel,  
Carolina as his share,  
Full of virtue, full of virtue, full of virtue.  

2.  
At first, the sorrow was very great,  
And now full joy again:  
All that one now hears said,  
The Prince so full of virtue,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
The groom, like a hero,  
You go to the field at night.  
But before you fire the shot,  
First drink a bottle with me.  
Ha! ha! See,  
Love burns like straw.  

2.  
Make sure you, like a man,  
Have good gunpowder in the pan  
And then pour, with a full heap,  
The powder into the barrel.  
Ha! ha! etc.  

3.  
The loading rod, until it stops,  
Must go in and out repeatedly  
And make sure you pay attention here  
That you firmly fix the wad.  
Ha! ha! etc.  

4.  
Aim, for your pleasure,  
And give a brave four.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Now the Bride has danced after the Bed,  
Her Freedom is done.  
The Garland that crowned her  
Will now soon fade away.  
The Groom has promised that he  
Will fulfill his playmate’s part.  
Now, young lads, it is your right  
Pick now the first green.  

2.  
There goes the Bride to the Bedstead,  
How shocked is the Apron!  
Something strange is playing in her mind.  
Come, Groom, like a Hero,  
Enjoy now the reward meant for you,  
And help your dear bride,  
Win her in a sweet Struggle:  
The wedding Joy is over.

label: wedding
translation:
1. Clear sun, clear rays,  
Clear stars, clear light,  
I stand to think and ponder,  
About a beautiful face.  
Help me to continually bear my misfortune,  
Now seize with me to grieve,  
Today is the day of farewell,  
Oh what poverty, oh what affliction.

2. Bright is the clear morning,  
Dark is the evening time,  
For the day brings many worries,  
That takes away cheerfulness.  
Although I must lose your mouth,  
Which I otherwise cannot seek  
From the noble darkness,  
While my heart is always sorrowful.

3. Faithful Heaven, faithful earth,  
Behold my sorrowful state,  
While I am constantly hurt, my worth  
In my beautiful beloved’s bond,  
Great thought of my loneliness,  
For my eye does not cease to weep,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
When Philis awakens for singing,  
The sun delights him in its rising!  
The lovely morning hour,  
Spreads its scent around,  
The lovely morning hour,  
Spreads its scents around.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, playmates, now sing wildly,  
In honor of the united pair.

label: wedding
translation:
I.  
In the morning when he came to wake me  
With the creaking of the day;  
He came to pull at my window:  
While I was still lying in my rest;  
And I said, praised my love,  
Open your door for me?  
Where the day has already risen,  
bis  
Will you then quickly come to me as well.  

2  
My love, my chosen one,  
She spoke with a fearful sound,  
Do not be charmed by anyone else,  
Come closer to your Bride,  
Oh how lovely it is to love, bis.  
In the early morning hour;  
There you can hear the birds singing  
Around the globe.

3.  
My exceedingly beautiful Annetje,  
Said my Bridegroom,  
Do you want to place this Rose on my Bosom?  
I embrace you again.  
And so we kissed each other, bis.  
This lovely scent,  
So Annette weds Leander,  
In the prime of her bloom.

label: wedding
translation:
I.
A girl of about eighteen years,
Wanted to escape from her Mother,
Who kept her too strictly,
But she slipped away with a trot,
Go up; go down, keep going:
Let this girl run free,
Ah poor Naa, where are you fleeing to?
Or are you taking Jantje with you,
They run around, then here, then there,
Then the girl is ready and done.

2.
A suitor of about the same age
Whom her Mother intended
To marry her to,
But he was no friend to her?
Go up, go down, keep going;
Let this girl run free,
Ah poor Naa, where are you fleeing to?
Or are you taking your Jantje with you,
They run around, here, there,
So the girl ends up ready with Jantje.

3.
Already during the fair time they wanted to know,
She went out with another,
Who also loved her so tenderly,

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Festive Families,  
Brave Friends!  
Rejoice in honor of the dear couple,  
Who long loved each other,  
Live happily, year after year.  

2.  
Let us sing,  
Celebrants!  
Open our hearts to joy,  
No sorrow should surround us here  
Where the eye is kept on virtue.  

3.  
Fill the glasses,  
Let the Fools,  
Rage, shout, and swear freely,  
No loving heart can be surprised,  
By joy, Poetry grows.  

4.  
Live many years,  
Free from dangers,  
From pressure and suffering;

label: wedding
translation:
Ha, what fun comes from getting married  
Long live love! Long live love!  
Stijntjen won’t last much longer,  
Long live love! I’m enjoying myself.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome, to this Wedding feast;  
Joy let our hearts ignite,  
Where this day is so hypocritical.  
Yes, the joy penetrates our veins,  
At this cheerful Marriage Festival,  
Where we together approach the golden table,  
Each of us cheers with a happy spirit, bis.  
But is marriage so attractive,  
Is freedom not worth more?  
Surely to sigh in bonds,  
Is the saddest thing on this earth.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome, to this Wedding Feast;  
Joy makes our hearts glow,  
Since this day is so sincere.  
Yes, the joy penetrates our veins,  
At this cheerful Wedding Celebration,  
Where we gather around the golden table,  
Each of us rejoices with a happy spirit, until.  
But is marriage so attractive,  
Is freedom no longer worth more?  
Surely, to sigh in chains,  
Is the saddest thing on this earth.

translation:
Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome, to this Wedding-table;  
Joy makes our hearts ignite,  
Since this day is so hypocritical.  
Yes, joy penetrates our veins,  
At this merry Wedding Feast,  
Where we gather around the gilded table,  
Each of us cheers with a happy spirit, twice.  
But is marriage so attractive,  
Is freedom no longer worth it?  
Certainly to sigh in bonds,  
Is the saddest on this earth.

translation:
Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome to this Wedding feast;  
Joy may kindle our hearts,  
Though this day is so hypocritical.  
Yes, joy penetrates our veins,  
At this cheerful Marriage celebration,  
Where we together approach the golden table,  
Each of us rejoices with a happy spirit, bis.  
But is marriage so attractive,  
Is freedom no longer worth more?  
Surely to sigh in bonds,  
Is the most sorrowful thing on this earth.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome to this Wedding Banquet;  
Joy makes our breasts ignite,  
Since this day is so hypocritical.  
Yes, joy penetrates our veins,  
At this merry Wedding Feast,  
Where we all gather around the golden table,  
Each of us rejoices with a happy spirit, bis.  
But is marriage so attractive,  
Is freedom no longer worth more?  
Certainly to sigh in bonds,  
Is the most sorrowful on this earth.

label: wedding
translation:
Health and joy! - health and joy  
Be yours, O Virtuous ones!  
Health and joy! - health and joy!  
Be the reward of your virtue.

label: wedding
translation:
When our Griet, that dear animal,  
Was married to Hans Polier;  
When the end had come,  
And the wedding was done.  
Then Griet came to Hans,  
And spoke to her husband thus.

We are now as it should be,  
United in the state of marriage;  
We will never quarrel,  
Or hurt each other,  
No! that branch of the olive tree,  
Must always remain green.

label: wedding
translation:
Long healthy and joyful living /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Adversity and prosperity alike /  
Live in peace with each other. bis.  
That their love will not diminish /  
But always grow Day and Night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder it;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. bis.

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and happily /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Sorrows and prosperity alongside /  
Live together in peace. repeat.  
That their love will not diminish /  
But continue to grow Day and Night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder it;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and happily /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Sorrows and prosperity alongside it /  
May they live in peace with each other. (repeat)  
That their love will not diminish /  
But always grow day and night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder them;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and joyful /  
I wish for the young couple;  
Sorrows and prosperity thereunder /  
Live in peace with each other. (repeat)  
That their love will not lessen /  
But always grow day and night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder them;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and happily /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Sorrows and prosperity alongside it /  
Live in peace with each other. (repeat)  
May their love not diminish /  
But constantly grow Day and Night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder them;  
Love and loyalty remain in its strength. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and happily /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Sorrows and prosperity alongside that /  
They live in peace with each other. (repeat)  
That their love will not diminish /  
But steadily grow day and night /  
So that no quarrel will hinder them;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
To live long, healthy, and happily /  
I wish to the young couple;  
Sorrow and prosperity alongside /  
Live in peace with each other. (repeat)  
That their love will not lessen /
