So that no quarrel will hinder them;  
Love and loyalty remain in their strength. (repeat)

label: wedding
wedding
translation:
I once went through forest and green trees  
In the hottest part of the day, as the sun leaves the east,  
I came to a brook, there I saw approaching me  
A shepherdess, she walks very gracefully. bis.

label: wedding
translation:
The little rose, in very joyful spirits,
Offered to a violet her affection,
To pair with him once,
Although she was already without youth,
A flower of forty years.

label: wedding
translation:
Here comes the one about Marriage,  
Pay close attention to this Song,  
Yes, what I am about to tell,  
What marriage all demands,  
And also what happens in it,  
I will mention in this Song,  
Briefly, briefly, briefly.  
First, then, take care together,  
To avoid quarrels,  
For that is not pleasant,  
To live in hatred and envy,  
Avoid quarrels as much as you can,  
So that God grants you peace,  
For joy, for joy, for joy.  
Be patient with each other's faults,  
With pure love clear,  
Then you will see your wish fulfilled,

label: wedding
translation:
When Phoebus with his golden rays
Descends from the sky to us
Into Cherus' crystalline lap,
Then the sun shakes from your kisses
Accompanied by winks, caresses, kisses,
And the sweetest desire for pleasure grows.
Your desire finds its peak to climb,
Who will deny you such sweet pleasure,
Go ahead, to the soft bridal bed,
And quench your desire with sweet fluttering,
That joy on earth shines like heaven.
Go ahead, fulfill Nature's law
to the playmates.
Here you see two united souls
Kneeling before love's altar,
And offering sparks of love.
Playmates want to weave a garland
To hang on the Bride’s head,
As a sign of sweet gain.
Playmates want to ask the Bride tomorrow
How she behaved last night,
I bet she will be very happy.
Choose a good lover from your side,
And make him a happy victor,
For loneliness does not seem to exist here.

label: wedding
translation:
Way: Money is the soul of all matters.

Welcome, oh friends all together,  
Welcome, to this Wedding table;  
Joy makes our hearts ignite,  
For this day is so sincere.  
Yes, the joy penetrates our veins,  
At this cheerful Wedding Feast,  
Where we together gather around the rich table,  
Each of us rejoices with a happy spirit:/:

label: wedding
translation:
Way: Let us exalt and sing, etc.

Health and joy! - health and joy  
Be your portion, O newlyweds!  
Health and joy! - health and joy!  
Be the reward of your virtue,  
May no disaster or distress ever strike you,  
May no sorrow trouble you;  
But an untroubled happiness  
Accompany your marital yoke!

What a good—what a sweetness  
Your virtuous heart will taste!  
No, sorrow is not known  
Where love commands the heart.

label: wedding
translation:
The Roosje, very joyful in spirit,  
Bore to a Violet her beloved,  
To mate with him once,  
Although she was already without youth,  
A flower of forty years.

label: wedding
translation:
In the mornings when he came to wake me,  
With the first signs of the day,  
He came to pull at my window,  
While I was still lying in my rest;  
And I say my love praised,  
Open your door for me;  
For the day has already risen,  
Then hurry to me as well.

label: wedding
translation:
The beauty of my soul's friend  
The radiance of my Nanetten,  
Makes my heart blaze with love,  
Why must I forever struggle?  
After the object of my love.

label: wedding
translation:
Tune: l'Amour est un enfant trompeur, etc.

Now live the generous cheerfulness  
Among kin and friends.  
We see the wedding feast prepared,  
And have gathered here;  
For the strictest virtue never forbids  
Sharing in happiness and joy  
With those we love. :/:

Thus the bond is now made,  
That no one can undo;  
Once the bride has said yes!  
She cannot escape him;  
And if there is virtue in husband and wife,  
What then is unbreakable faithfulness,  
A far-reaching open prospect! :/:

label: wedding
translation:
While the Bird Choir in these final days  
By love's song enchants our heart  
Since all that lives seems to us to lament through love,  
And is urged on by nature,  
Who then does not praise at this moment?  
This much-promising marriage bond,            bis.

label: wedding
translation:
One early morning,  
Very cheerfully went out in the lanes,  
As in such a merry forest,  
Built close by a city.  
It was a lovely wood,  
Planted with tall trees,  
A lovely sound,  
There the orchard resounded.

label: wedding
translation:
If you wish to live happily in marriage,
O newlywed and youthful couple,
Then always obey reason,
And always follow its laws. bis.
Never let discord divide you,
While you show a graying being:
The devil rules where two quarrels,
The god of love where peace dwells. bis.

However tenderly you may love each other,
A small difference may sometimes arise,
One is not always of the same mind;
Man's will is precisely not woman's will. bis.
Then, before the storm of strife can rise,
Prevent it through sincere behavior:
The man should speak only through kisses,
The woman make amends with a smile. bis.

If you spend your days in this manner,
You avoid all household quarrels,

label: wedding
translation:
The joyful day has dawned,
On which you, O tender-loving couple!
Have lit your pure matrimonial torch
At Hymen's virtue-dedicated altar.
You see, wherever you turn your eyes,
The greatest joy on every face;
Yes, those gathered around this table of friends
Raise in their artless song
The blessing that such a union
Offers you in such ample measure:
Realize its full worth,
Happiness, united wife and husband!
Husband and wife! Wife and husband!
Realize its full worth.

We wish in your married life
That you, free from pain and sorrow,
This day, driven by pure passion,
May celebrate often, delighted and joyful.

label: wedding
wedding
translation:
How sweet is the place where love resides,  
That blazes young hearts!  
Where the palm crowns the maiden,  
Heavenly joy is tasted.  
It ignites and nurtures the fire of life,  
And makes the gifts of nature  
Perfect, perfect, perfect.

Lonely life withers there,  
Like a sorrowful plant,  
That never saw a sunbeam,  
Withers in the soulless sand.  
Love, fertile in its power,  
Binds and connects all earthly beings  
Together, together, together.

label: wedding
translation:
Raise your voice to your song, beloved friends!  
Freely express your joy:  
The celebration of this wished-for union  
Encourages us to pure delight. (repeat)  

To give praise to the youthful couple,  
And thanks for the most pleasant reception,  
Let everyone sing again and again:  
May they live long in prosperity! (repeat)  

The groom is not at all shy  
About how to caress his bride;  
And the bride does not resist this,  
For she finds all his teasing sweet. (repeat)  

Now as they head to the bridal carriage,  
Both hearts blaze with stronger fire.  
Long may the call of nature be followed!  
Long may their love live! (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
When a loving couple,  
Driven by pure love,  
Has united in harmony  
At the sacred marriage altar,  
They are called bride and groom,  
Generously, after their wedding,  
Welcomed as true people  
At the joyful wedding celebration.

Your friends, gathered here,  
Seated at the festive table,  
Who, now that you are paired as wished,  
Call you husband and wife,  
Rejoice in the happiness you enjoy;  
In celebrating your wedding,  
They fulfill their duty  
With a joyful song at the wedding celebration.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, grab the bottle (repeat)
In honor of the young couple,
Whose wedding is now being celebrated,
And who adorn this table with friends;
Come, grab the bottle! etc.

Fill your glass, (repeat)
And take it in your hand;
And then with a joyful mouth
Wish them well from the bottom of your heart:
Fill your glass! etc.

May that blessing (repeat)
Descend upon your heads!
May no adversity ever strike your house.
Live long, free from disaster and suffering.
May that blessing descend, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
When Cupid has led a youthful couple  
To the marriage altar,  
To marry there together (repeat),  
Then usually follows a generous reception  
From friends with a fine meal:  
This is called holding a wedding (repeat).  

This is a praiseworthy custom  
When entering the marriage trap:  
One would marry for this reason alone (repeat);  
But if one has food in their body,  
And sits as stiff as a stick,  
Then it is not holding a wedding (repeat).

label: wedding
translation:
Let us, oh guests! sing joyfully,  
At the feast of those united two:  
The feeling of joy of married ones  
Also shares its influence with our hearts.  
One wishes that blessing  
To be with their spouse  
In love and peace!

When, in the blooming of their years,  
A loving couple binds themselves,  
Then they taste the fruit of youthful pairing,  
The happiness of a heart that loves purely,  
It burns once fiercely,  
But remains continuously  
Friend and friend.

label: wedding
translation:
Here he now adds to sing of him,  
To whom I also dedicate my stringed instrument;  
For he indeed bestowed, O married ones!  
Your tender love with resilience.  
May he create pleasing melodies,  
Yes, melodies of a loving nature?  
And should I then not mention here,  
What sweet mingled pleasure brings! (repeat).

Yes! When the love torches blaze,  
Before the adorned lace of wedlock,  
Then he is quickly, at those amusements,  
With arrows of love at hand! -  
Enveloping the stream of desire with rays,  
Upon which the eye then blissfully rests,  
And causes those waves to descend,  
Into hearts, united by marriage. (repeat).

label: wedding
translation:
As a young and modest girl  
She is courted for the first time,  
Then she lowers her loving eyes,  
Not out of maidenly shame.  
The soft blush on her cheeks  
Is like a rose in color;  
But the fiery glow of that blushing  
Reveals her loving heart.

But when, in later years,  
She finds herself adorned as a bride,  
And sprinkles the blazing wedding altar  
With flowers and palm,  
She no longer needs to blush  
Because of youthful inclination,  
And the sparkling of her eyes  
Brings true joy to her husband.

label: wedding
translation:
Three cheers for happiness, united couple!  
What good fortune do you see preparing for you this day,  
Now that before the sacred altar of marriage,  
The wish of you both is crowned!  
The day, so much desired by you,  
The joyful day has come,  
That your love receives its reward!  
And you see your wedding torch lit.  

Yet, let us leave this wedding torch there:  
May it blaze in more beautiful verses;  
There stands a bit of a night candle ready over there,  
That gives enough light for your affairs.  
We can now safely take the marriage oath  
Out of the room:  
It is in the way here now, and besides,  
The sacrifice will likely succeed better without it.

label: wedding
translation:
Freshly sounding again,  
Before we all go to bed!  
Long may the drinking last!  
Long may the fun continue!  
No wedding would be complete,  
If no stream of noble wine,  
Which is the fire of love and friendship,  
Could flow along the table.

Freshly sounding again, etc.  
The beautiful time spent here,  
Requires a wish of 'good night!'  
Who has feasted, danced, kissed,  
Longs for sweet rest.

Freshly sounding again, etc.  
We, groom and bride,  
Must thank you, in conclusion,  
For the genuine hospitality and tender sweetness,  
Met at your table.

label: wedding
translation:
Friends! Let us be joyful,  
And decorate this table;  
While drinking the wine,  
And celebrating the wedding,  
No silent sorrow fits,  
Nor anxious remorse;  
Where flowers and palms are spread,  
True pleasure resides.

Pour once; the wedding joy  
Demands a full cup.  
Groom’s generosity, bride’s virtue,  
Are certain to us all:  
As we drink with a glass of wine  
To their prosperity,  
May no disaster in this desert  
Ever cause their courage to sink!

label: wedding
translation:
Much pleasure, newlyweds!  
We wish you on this day,  
Which each of us, full of longing,  
Has looked forward to for your state of well-being,  
May you always remain united in heart,  
Whether in prosperity or adversity,  
Show, in changing circumstances,  
Always an unyielding courage!  

Heaven's blessing, gentle prosperity,  
May it be the reward for your virtue,  
May fearful sorrow never strike your home,  
May its pure joy never forsake you,  
May care or worry never burden you,  
But undisturbed happiness,  
May it always grant you true pleasure,  
Under the soft yoke of marriage.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, let us toast!  
In honor of Groom and Bride,  
At the farewell,  
Everyone drinks their glass.  
See how the dear Bride smiles!  
She thinks it is already midnight...  
And that’s the time when... every guest,  
Joyfully returns to their own.

Come, let us, etc.  
The Groom counts every tick of the clock,  
Eagerly anticipating the moment,  
In which he... sees how every guest,  
Joyfully returns to their own.

Come, let us, etc.  
We wish the Bride in the struggle,  
The deepest obedience,  
And much pleasure in this night,  
Devised by Cupid.

label: wedding
translation:
Loved ones whom you bind together in marriage through love,  
I do urge you to consider well  
What you are about to begin.  
It is neither an ox nor a cow trade,  
Nor is it child's play;  
But it is quite a firmer knot,  
So consider it carefully.

It is not about: darling, come to bed,  
Nor butter for the tree,  
But worries upon worries prevent you,  
That awakens you from ten dreams:  
The care for the house and household needs,  
For the ash shovel and the tongs,  
For the sulfur stick and the chafing dish,  
That often frightens you.

Then you must provide for the cost;  
O heavy and stubborn debt!  
For if you have wine, you lack must,  
Or bacon, you lack head cheese.  
And if your marriage becomes fruitful,  
Stand ready then to wipe and watch  
The cradle and whatever else is lacking,  
And everything is expensive.

label: wedding
translation:
The trumpets and trumpets  
Blow in a golden tone,  
Now Trumpet leaves the laws of war  
For Venus’ son.  
He tears the steel from the body,  
For the silk gun a woman,  
Makes trumpets again,  
Steel hulls  
Bold and stiff.  

The world was amazed,  
Eight months have now passed,  
While the Echo still thunders from it  
And calls: the man of stone  
Makes sea and shore resound  
Through pure love’s fire,  
Trumpet will marry  
The cream of women  
In our land.

label: wedding
translation:
Just as a ship sails  
Through the rough waves  
Out of the untamed sea  
To a safe harbor,  
It shows its joy,  
Lowers its sails,  
Unfurls its pennants,  
And with a proud sound  
Thunders, rumbles, clatters as demanded.  
Its loud blasts  
To the Lord's praise  
For its safe journey.  

So let us also sing  
And perform plays,  
Provided that through the wilderness  
Of restless freedom  
The bridegroom, with desires,  
In the haven of rest  
Has happily brought his little ship  
This night,  
Where he is warmly welcomed by the bride,  
Who in her face  
Concludes the great joy  
Of his youth.  

Therefore, he is with reason  
Also full of happiness.

label: wedding
translation:
Hooray, with joyful sounds,  
We raise our song!  
Hooray for this loving couple,  
In the territory of Hijmens!  
May Cupid never move away,  
But for fifty years,  
With his bow and arrows,  
Free you from the oppression of Hijmens!

Weave flowers of gold and silver,  
Calmly, at your ease,  
So sits, after fifty years,  
The minstrel still on the roof!  
And if the storm of times  
Throws something down here or there,  
Then may dear love  
Bring you comfort and prosperity again!

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Joyfully sung once again,  
Cheerful and happy in spirit,  
The elderly heart sprung from joy,  
At the children's wedding celebration;  
Let us properly consider this,  
With a glass full of wine,  
To offer our well-wishes to them,  
This may surely also be appropriate.  

2.  
Then taste the greatest blessing on earth,  
Of this double marriage bond,  
Taste it with the fullest value,  
Taste it to the very last moment.  
Family on both sides as well,  
Heartily thought of by us;  
To rejoice in this union;  
A sip is raised to this.  

3.  
We too, who are here as friends,  
Share in the purest joy,  
Which is experienced in marriage;  
Pure love, faithfulness, and virtue,  
May accompany them enduringly  
Is the wish that opens the heart;  
To raise a glass to this,  
We drink united.

label: wedding
translation:
1.  
Let us now sing joyfully,  
It is at a fitting time,  
If one truly wants to seek joy,  
Let everyone here indulge in it;  
Never could one express it better,  
Than at the joyful Wedding Feast,  
Nothing can surpass the pleasure here,  
Clear and cheerful in spirit.  

2.  
Let the spirit not be dulled,  
Father! Mother! it is your comfort,  
That you may gratefully believe,  
At the Wedding of your tender offspring;  
The first year a fresh young son,  
The second year a young girl;  
That is a little crown on both heads,  
Drink, it is dedicated to the marriage.  

3.  
Look what a sweet little face,  
Is that a lovely little Bride indeed,  
Yes I well know that this little trait,  
Brings him near to her love;  
Because the Yes-word now obtained,  
Helps him back properly on his feet,  
So why wait so long in silence?  
Pour and drink in general.

label: wedding
translation:
It is also your motto, as I can suppose,  
That saying, renowned in faraway lands;  
I have always carried it on my shield:  
‘It is better to marry than to burn.’  
But, friends, if one considers it well,  
Many burn, but indeed do not marry!

Hail to him who can attach  
The glowing breast of a dear maiden as desired,  
True to the saying, bravely ventures!  
To weave a wedding wreath with her!  
But friends, if one considers it well,  
Many marry, but indeed do not burn!

Be that as it may, I judge with soul and mind,  
Him who on life’s winding paths  
Always holds the needle of true marital love  
At sixty-four degrees;  
Yes, friends, he is a worthy man  
Who can often burn for Hymen.  
May you, O Bride and Groom!  
With Cupid’s help stoke your little fire!  
Among those whose hearts glowed with love,  
Hymen’s bond was never broken.  
Then Cupid will surely guide your steps,  
Through life, along a flowerbed.

See, friends, wish and vow fulfilled,  
This may one freely believe,  
Even when they already walk on crutches,  
The wedding flame will not be extinguished in them:  
Then the heart still burns with every kiss,  
Just as... Vesuvius!

Come, now the cup in hand:  
Let us, friends, pour the nectar,  
And bring heartfelt cheers  
To the couple who burns so fiercely here!  
May no sorrow or disaster ever extinguish  
Their brightly filled wedding lamp!

And you, who always kept the cold breast  
Closed to Cupid’s arrows,  
Do light your fire for Venus’ Son  
In this fierce frost, won’t you?  
He is the best kindler  
Of all the lads in our land!

label: wedding
translation:
Rejoice, happy spouses,  
What completes bliss,  
Is enclosed in a bond,  
And that bond never breaks; bis.  
Hallelujah to Him who unites the dust,  
Who sanctifies the earth through a marriage,  
Who mingles the souls together,  
Not weaving them into breakable cords.

label: wedding
translation:
One will, one desire,  
And the heart full of pure love,  
Thus one may call the couple happy!  
Then gladly scatter  
The choicest of all flowers,  
Little flowers of Joy!

Unity and love  
Adorn the household,  
Even if you ever face bitter blows.  
Love in the heart  
Teaches to bear together,  
Heals all sorrow.

Bloom undisturbed  
Thus may your love continue!  
Where love dwells, there is blessing;  
And pure virtue  
Scatters along your paths  
Little flowers of joy!

label: wedding
translation:
One will, one purpose,  
And the heart full of pure love,  
Thus one may call the couple happy!  
Then joyfully scatter  
The choice of all flowers,  
Little flowers of Joy!

Unity and love  
Decorate the household,  
Even if you ever face bitter blows.  
Love in the heart  
Teaches to bear together,  
Heals all pain.

May your love flourish  
Uninterrupted!  
Where love dwells there is the blessing;  
And pure virtue  
Scatters on your paths  
Little flowers of joy!

label: wedding
translation:
Step in, loving couple, now inside,  
Into Hymen’s little boat,  
So neatly rigged by friends  
And girlfriends.  
Fear no high waves,  
Even if the water is hollow;  
For Hymen is an expert  
In navigating!  
Hurrah! Let your cheerful song sound, }  repeat.  
Even if the water is hollow! }  repeat.  
Your journey will surely be prosperous,  
For Hymen, as captain,  
Is always happy and brave,  
No matter the weather:  
Provided none of the fellow travelers  
Ever annoy him by grumbling;  
For those who trouble him too much,  
He throws them overboard.  
Hurrah! Let your song always sound, }  repeat.  
To Hymen’s cheerful board! }  repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
To the Groom be glory, cheers.  
His crown of love, victory! cheers.  
Long may the harmony last  
Of all friendship!  
Cheers, cheers, cheers!

O Bride! So full of grace, cheers.  
May love grant her all things, cheers,  
The highest joys of life!  
Let them also sing to her glory!  
Cheers, cheers, cheers!

To the Groom’s vigilance, cheers.  
And to the Bride’s constancy! cheers,  
Let all the cups ring out  
Brimming with grape juice!  
Cheers, cheers, cheers!

May all their affairs, cheers,  
Go prosperously and prosper: cheers.  
May they never have opposites,  
But joy in abundance!  
Cheers, cheers, cheers!

So flourish, by love’s grace, cheers,  
Their welfare, through life’s hardships, cheers,  
Up to the highest stars,  
Through ages of ages!  
Cheers, cheers, cheers!

label: wedding
translation:
To Roosland zoo we sailed,  
And there indeed lived my sweet love, bis.  
Already for my sweet love behind the little door,  
And there stood two orchard trees, sweet. bis.  

On one hung a Nutmeg,  
On the other a Clove, sweet,  
I thought I was courting such a rich one,  
But alas, it was a poor blood. bis.  

I took her by her little hand,  
And I kissed her so red and so sweet, bis.  
And I courted her so lovingly and so far,  
Yes, all the way to under the Orchard. bis.  

The Orchard was closed,  
And indeed no one lived there, bis.  
Nothing but the sweet Nightingale,  
And it flew there down from above. bis.  

Now we will tie the Nightingale,  
With its head under its foot, bis.  
So that it won’t tell anyone,  
What is done under this Orchard. bis.  

Now we will end the song,  
Yes, in honor of the Bridegroom and Bride, bis.  
And then cheerfully, joyfully resound,  
And so we drink our glass out. bis.

label: wedding
translation:
In honor of the Young Couple, hurray,  
United before the Altar of Marriage, hurray,  
Now sounds the joyful wedding song,  
that everyone offers in their honor.  
Hurray, hurray, hurray,  

Welcome to Hymen’s temple, hurray,  
Happiness in marriage, oh groom, hurray,  
Enjoy with your dear wife,  
The blessing of fidelity and conjugal love,  
Hurray, etc.  

The bride is honored: hurray,  
All that you expect in marriage, hurray,  
We rejoice in your triumph  
And in the choice of your Spouse  
Hurray, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
Dear Groom, dear Bride,  
You are now united together,  
In the joyful Marriage-Boat,  
You have taken your place.

label: wedding
translation:
When Cupid has led a youthful couple  
To the marriage altar  
To marry there together,  
Then usually follows a generous reception  
From friends at a proper meal,  
This is called holding a Wedding, indeed,

label: wedding
translation:
To the Groom be Glory, Hooray!  
His love crowned Victoria, Hooray!  
May the Harmonica last  
Through love and Friendship.  
Hooray! Hooray! Hooray! (repeat)

O Bride, always so full of Grace, hooray!  
May this bond still grant you Omnia, hooray!  
The highest joys of life!  
We sing to your Glory  
Hooray! hooray! hooray! (repeat)

To the Groom’s Vigilance, hooray!  
And to the Bride’s Constancy  
Let all the Cups resound  
With grape juice Pline sima,  
Hooray, hooray, hooray! (repeat)

That all their Affairs, hooray,  
May prosper and be successful, hooray,  
May they never have adversities,  
But joy in Abundance,  
Hooray! hooray! hooray! (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
There went a young man, neat and tidy,  
Hey, it was in May,  
He had set his sights on a girl,  
Hey, it was in May, so happy,  
Hey, it was in May,  

My angel! my darling! I love you so tenderly,  
Thus spoke the bridegroom happily,  
Come, give me your little heart and love me again,  
Hey, it was in May, etc.  

Then the bride gave him her hand, blushing so,  
Moved by compassion,  
And the kissing pledge with a clapping sound,  
He, it was in May, etc.  

Come, bridegroom! give your bride a kiss,  
For see, that is your right,  
You may do it six times over,  
Hey, it was in May, etc.  

Now we celebrate, and the courting is done,  
And sing joyfully and happily,  
And toast in honor of bridegroom and bride.  
Hey, it was in May, etc.  

And within the year raised in rank,  
Believe this prophecy:  
Man and wife sing a lullaby,  
Hey, it was in May, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
When our little bride, sweet and kind,  
Was asked to love,  
She quickly lowered her eyes.  
Though that question pleased her,  
And the blush on her cheeks  
Was just like a little rose,  
Now the glow of her blushing  
Shows the signs of her loving heart.  
Now that she stands in this state  
Adorned as a bride,  
And afterwards sprinkles the wedding attire  
With flowers and palm,  
She no longer has to blush  
Because of youthful shyness,  
But the glancing of her eyes  
Is true joy to her groom.  
Tender bride, your charms  
Are worthy of winning love.  
Then freely speak the language of the eyes  
To your chosen friend;  
He understands this silent speech  
And answers with a glance,  
Making this language increasingly spark  
In love more and more.  
Cherished couple! Always scatter flowers  
On your beautiful life's path,  
Remember that together you must  
Go on until its end.  
Even if a light cloud  
Once covers your prosperous sunshine,  
In your faithful united souls  
It must always remain clear,

label: wedding
translation:
When youthful blood flows through the veins  
Love and wine often elevate,  
When the fire of love ignites the heart,  
Everyone may well be joyful.  
We then want to ride off,  
And let the festive song resound,  
And raise it in honor  
Of bride and groom. b.

We first dedicate the song  
To the honor of the groom,  
And wish that you will long  
Rejoice in the blessing of love.  
May your bride soon crown your wish,  
At least within the year,  
Then playfully on your lap a son—  
This is the wish of the circle of friends. b.

We, dear bride, begin to sing;  
We dedicate our song to you,  
And wish that no tear of sadness  
Ever runs down your cheeks;  
If our desire is crowned,  
Then may it be decreed for you,  
That the spouse always shows such love  
As on the first night in bed. b.

We all wish, youthful couple,  
You the greatest happiness on earth,  
May you be spared from each other  
Many years in pure love.

label: wedding
translation:
Though the world may last so long,  
Many matters go the old way,  
Who will unfold this broadly? (repeat)  
The boy flatters, the girl sighs,  
And after the end of the courtship,  
One longs to marry.

But, groom, have you truly considered  
Though the girl is dear and gentle,  
Ah, the yoke of dear women. (repeat)  
It is, how gladly borne, a yoke,  
And if it ever pinches, happiness is gone,  
Then the sweetness of marriage is gone. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
The bride lives, the groom lives  
So the festive song is heard:  
that God gives them joy and prosperity  
And grants them their salvation;  
So sounds our song, joyful and glad,  
Yes noble couple, this we pray.

Yes, dear bride, this we wish,  
this wells from our hearts,  
May your life then flow soft and joyful,  
And remain free from sorrow,  
May it be free from disaster and pressure,  
And always a chain of happiness.

And also for you, o groom,  
The heart begins to glow,  
that God showers you with blessings  
And grants you prosperity.

label: wedding
translation:
What was the world without a woman, oh dear,  
How one would suffer from cold in winter, oh dear!  
But with a little wife by one’s side,  
One walks through every season,  
So happy, so happy, so happy. b.

I do not mean a troublesome wife now, oh no,  
One who loves quarrels and bickering, oh no.  
No woman who proudly waves the sceptre  
And whose rooster consistently crows king,  
Oh no, oh no, oh no. b.

I mean a soft and gentle wife, oh yes,  
Who never engages in household quarrels, oh yes,  
Who pleases her husband,  
And always lives in peace with him,  
Oh yes, oh yes, oh yes. b.

But then the husband must also prove, oh yes,  
That he values his wife, oh yes,  
That he does not give her too little,

label: wedding
translation:
There where a couple is united,  
By the sweet bond of love,  
There, there we gladly join in  
Cheering along with a tune,  
Let them marry, let them marry  
Marrying always brings joy,  

Marrying always brings happiness,  
Marrying always brings joy;  
For the guests at the Wedding,

label: wedding
translation:
Of all the celebrations here on earth,  
A Wedding is the most valued by us,  
One may then sing joyfully,  
One is delighted, one does not shy away from a glass,  
Many a toast comes in handy,  
To the health of the married couple!  

Of all the words here on earth,  
The word "I do" is the most valuable,  
Sometimes difficult to obtain;  
For many a beautiful lady, although she means well,  
Deprives her knight of joy and courage,  
By mysterious silence.  

Of every joy here on earth,  
The joy of marriage is the most valued,  
So surely thought the Couple;  
A wise decision gained the force of law,  
Now we rejoice in the wedding festivities,  
With songs of jubilation and a cup raised.  

Of all consolations here on earth,  
This consolation is the most valued by us:  
“May the Married Couple live long!”

label: wedding
translation:
Let us now sing the marriage song together, there is reason for it;  
For a couple of our friends  
Will soon experience  
What, what, oh how sweet,  
One encounters in the state of matrimony.  

People may say what they want,  
Our groom did not sit still,  
He had already tried for several years  
To pair with a loving woman,  
Finally love granted him  
A dear soulmate.  

Now we shall see  
How this young couple,  
For about a year after the wedding,  
Behaves as husband and wife,  
That, that is certain  
Something that is very necessary.

label: wedding
translation:
Those who must  
Taste joy on earth without loves,  
Are out of their mind.  
Or slow of blood.  
The boy who does not pursue any girl  
Certainly complains night after night;  
And tosses and kicks the covers apart,  
And longs for the morning.  

Those who must  
Taste joy on earth without loves,  
Feel pain inside,  
Are slow of blood.  
What is a girl without a man?  
A beautifully lacquered coffee pot,  
Of which, through carelessness,  
The spout was lost.  

Those who  
Want to taste joy on this earth without loves,  
Are foolish of mind,  
Are worthy of pity,  
That is seen in the poor blood,  
Who must live without a woman.  
Who stiffens their goods, who covers them?  
Who stops their woolen socks?

label: wedding
translation:
Wedding guests are bustling,  
And sing in honor of the cradle;  
Now cheerfully raised. repeat.  
For everything comes in its time.  
A boy or a girl said so,  
That doesn’t matter here. repeat.

Remember, dear Bride,  
And plan ahead in this matter,  
Don’t wait until the last moment:  
Have you settled your account,  
So that no time is lost:  
It is so often done at the last minute. repeat.

There must be something ready,  
Whether large or small,  
A cradle cannot be missed;  
A fire basket, blanket, etc.,  
For that there is absolutely no excuse,  
As you can surely guess. repeat.

A batch of pins, small and large;  
A pillow like a loaf of wheat bread,  
Trimmed with fine lace.  
Then handkerchiefs, bonnets, coarse and fine,  
And above all, there must be  
A bundle of navel bands. repeat.

The swaddling clothes too, don’t forget them,  
Make sure you have a generous supply;  
That will be welcome to you,  
The long gowns, remember them,  
Because if you want to go out someday,  
Your taste must be admired. repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Now the freedom has come,  
Mister the groom. Miss the bride:  
Are now married people; repeat.  
And soon everyone asks solemnly:  
How is Mister? How is Miss?  
Or how are the young folks? repeat  

You certainly expect, and rightly so,  
the greatest blessing on your marriage;  
we do so with you as well. repeat  
And wish that never before a bride  
Has stepped into a better boat  
than the one you have entered, repeat.  

We wish that in sunshine  
Your boat may be a yacht,  
To sail gently; repeat.  
But when there is a storm for you,  
May it become a steamboat, transformed,  
that flies ahead through the waves. repeat  

But also wish that in your boat,  
Even if your love is so great,  
There may still be room for friendship; repeat.  
So that when we see you go,  
We need not be left behind,  
But may drift along with you. repeat.  

Then when you look at your silver celebration,  
May it be, for all of us, a joyful occasion,  
Certainly appearing once again; repeat.  
But then take care, friends! that your table  
Is properly set  
by big and small! repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Marriage is a sweet cake,  
For those who eat it moderately,  
So, newlyweds! be brave,  
And don’t take too big a bite:  
Then you will have years of pleasure  
From the sweetness it offers you,  
And won’t turn choosing into scolding,  
Because that only brings sorrow. (repeat)  

Marriage is a currant bun,  
Not free from stones,  
But they cause us trouble,  
If one bites carelessly;  
So cheerful couple, take care,  
Don’t bite too deeply,  
But make sure to keep measure,  
Especially at the very beginning. (repeat)  

Marriage is a bunch of grapes,  
Tempting to the eye,  
But many went wild for it,  
Who grievously deceived themselves;  
So, newlyweds, just picked;  
Then the trial delight lasts long,  
That gave to many great rapture,  
The cause for joyful song. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Come, let us sing with joyful voice, hooray  
A song in honor of her and him, hooray  
Of the bridal couple worthy to us all  
Whose celebration has gathered us here together.  
Hooray! hooray, hooray,

label: wedding
translation:
That Mahomed forbids wine,  
Is not his wisest rule,

label: wedding
translation:
Now the journey will begin,  
The wedding boat must move on,  
Come Groom, step aboard,  
And take your Bride on board;  
Do not be shy of any storm,  
But boldly set out to sea,  
You do not have the wind against you  
And carry love with you.  
Love steers the little boat  
And keeps it along the coast,  
Come Groom, kiss your Bride,  
And travel at will and desire.  
Let flags and pennants wave,  
You will not tire of the journey,  
A breeze may blow.  
It blows, blessing upon you.

label: wedding
translation:
There was recently a sweet Bride,  
Hail to the Bride!  
She looked, oh! so lovely  
Boys, boys, boys, what a sweet Bride!

She sat next to her Groom,  
Hail, Groom, hail!  
And friends and maids all around,  
Boys, boys, boys, what a Groom!

Everyone was cheerful and joyful,  
Yes, truly joyful!  
And laughed and sang out of pure joy,  
Singing, singing, singing yes, out of pure joy!

Oh, they danced around,  
Oh yes, around,  
And stayed until the morning hour.  
Certainly, certainly, certainly, until the morning hour.

And they drank many a glass,  
Yes, many a glass!  
It was as if Bacchus himself was the pourer,  
Bacchus, Bacchus, Bacchus was the pourer.

But what did the sweet Couple do?  
What did the Couple do?  
They sat picking on each other,  
Hahaha, hahaha, hahaha, well well, that sweet Couple!

And what did the Groom do then?  
What did he do then?  
Well, he gave his Bride a kiss,  
Hahaha, hahaha, hahaha, yes, that’s what he did then.

But does this naughty existence not demand revenge?  
Yes, it demands revenge!

label: wedding
translation:
He who wants to praise the Marriage,  
Join in our song,  
And let his joyful tone rise,  
And sing his whole life long:  
More and more  
Close to honor,  
For what can give more joy  
Than the cheerful Married life,  
It is the most beautiful thing that exists,  
So everyone sing in rhythm.

With your dearest Soulfriend  
To be inseparably one,  
One in wishes, one in thoughts,  
Faithful in truth, not in appearance;  
It is a fate,  
Full of pleasure  
To read in each other's eyes  
What the soul's wish can be,  
It is the most beautiful thing that exists,  
So everyone sing in rhythm,

label: wedding
translation:
Everyone makes love and marries, and that’s good  
Wiedewiedewië bombom;  
That makes life soft and sweet  
Wiedewiedewie bombom.  
And if no one ever made love anymore  
Wiedewiedewie bombom, bombom  
Then we would lose all joy,  
Wiedewiedewie bombom.  
Gloria gloria, wiedewiedewie bombom bombom  
Gloria, gloria, wiedewiedewie bombom, repeat.  

And if people were to stop marrying, too  
Wiedewiedewie bombom,  
The world would go terribly,  
Wiedewiedewie bombom;  
There would be no end to our sorrow,  
Wiedewiedewie bombom, bombom.  
And surely we would no longer be here  
Wiedewiedewie bombom.  
Gloria gloria wiedewiedewie bombom bombom  
Gloria, gloria wiedewiedewie bombom.  

Here is yet another couple,  
Wiedewiedewie bombom,  
United with each other by love,

label: wedding
translation:
Let us raise the cheer,
In honor of the young couple,
Who today may enjoy,
The joyful marriage altar;
They want to sing the song,
That father Adam sang,
When at the beginning of things,
He gladly received Eve.
No matter how much may grow old,
Or change over time,
One remains ever faithful,
No matter how much else fades;
But the growing and multiplying,
That is also continued,
However matters turn out,
This remains according to the ancient law.

label: wedding
translation:
Pure love establishes its throne,  
On true loyalty and virtue,  
And weaves a noble crown of love,  
For well-united youth;  
Thus it binds their hearts and shapes their minds,  
To mutual faithfulness and love,  
With joy, with joy, with joy!  
And if that faithfulness can grant its favor  
What salvation is prepared for them,  
Then that desired day approaches,  
Which leads them to the Beyond;  
Then one sees in the Sanctuary  
Their names as Bride and Bridegroom,  
With splendor, with splendor, with splendor!  
The All-Goodness, who has united their heart and hand  
So closely,  
Confirms them in the Marriage state,  
To which their love aspires;  
Behold soon, o beloved Couple!  
Before the glorious Marriage Altar,  
United, united, united!

The blessing of God's fatherly hand,  
Crown your marriage lineage,  
Accompany your work and state of life,  
In glorious Amsterdam;  
Thus Family’s joy increases,  
Where everyone honors God's name the highest,  
What salvation, what salvation, what salvation!

label: wedding
translation:
Your covenant  
And this, stood,

label: wedding
translation:
Although this earth is no longer Eden,  
Although it breeds disaster and sorrow:  
Yet God's blessing still descends,  
On True Love and Fidelity.  
He who pursues only fleeting pleasure  
That flees at the moment of delight,  
That which pleases the eye's beauty,  
He does not know that Blessing,  
But wherever respect for virtue  
Is the nurse of Love,  
There that Blessing spreads salvation and joy,  
In the true family home:  
There love endures,  
Even under disaster and pressure,  
For there thrives the heavenly plant:  
Domestic happiness,  
What semblance of joy the world offers,  
In the adversity it cultivates,

label: wedding
translation:
Well Joostje! Are you tired of luxury? b.  
I hear you are going to get married,  
Well, my dear friend! watch out,  
Marriage has something to it!  
There is so much attached to it,  
Do well and take advice,  
And keep out of trouble,  
Freedom cannot be harmed,  
Because it suits the young.

label: wedding
translation:
A man is indeed a wanderer,  
If he lives without a wife;  
He walks and does not know what he wants,  
Now he has this whim, now that one,  
Sometimes he walks, sometimes he stands still,  
His heart is in mourning.  

He is like a mosquito,  
Always hovering around the flame,  
He spins like in a millwheel,  
He does not know who, or where, or what,  
Now he seeks this, now that again,  
Eventually becoming tired and stiff.  

What advice then for that wanderer?  
Nothing but a dear wife,  
Who shortens his empty time.  
Search, little friend! search for a dear girl,  
They can be found far and wide,  
Search, search for a dear wife.  

So our groom chose  
His bride as his companion,

label: wedding
translation:
What a wonderful life it is,  
To be the Groom and Bride,  
And to drink the cool wine,  
And to drink the cool wine;  
I think one would marry on purpose for it  
To also hold a little wedding trip,  
Playful boys follow the Bride,  
If you want to do well, then don’t hold back  

The first man and Eve,  
They married each other,  
If you want to do well, then follow her,  
If you want to do well, then follow her,  
For had our parents not done it,  
We would not even have gone to the wedding,  
Playful boys follow the Bride,  
If you want to do well, then don’t hold back  

I am amazed by it  
That you waste your green youth,  
So alone you waste it,

label: wedding
translation:
Good evening all you lot,  
Lord, how wonderfully busy it is here,  
Don’t be scared, I am little Cupid,  
Won’t you buy a sulfur stick now?  
Or something else from my little box,  
Merchants, look, my march goes freely through,  
Do you want a blushing apricot,  
Or a rose sweetly scented?  
A thousand other beautiful things  
I have in my magic bag,  
Do you want songs to sing,  
I have those you never heard before,  
Or does someone want to buy booklets,  
I have all kinds here,  
Here’s a booklet, open it once,  
I bet you’ve never seen anything stranger.  

It’s the nice wedding treat,  
Much sought after and well desired,  
Where many a clever fox  
Has wasted much of their time;  
Here is still a little rarity,  
A nonpareil, it’s something strange,  
It is a tasty sweet,  
Come, light it up with the candle.  
One can really contemplate that,  
Because it is truly worth seeing,  
Young ladies, don’t you want it,  
Young sir, are you up for it?  
Well, they’re holy maker cookies.  
And they taste wonderfully strange,

label: wedding
wedding
translation:
Who always kneels and always grumbles,  
Does himself much injustice,  
Does not fit in our circles;  
But, whoever loves conviviality,  
Finds pleasure in laughter and jest;  
May happily sing with us, b.  
The man who neither desires nor knows Friendship,  
Is certainly also an unpleasant fellow,  
Does not fit in our circles.  
But whoever aims for the sweetness of Friendship  
And tastes through it the purest pleasure,  
May happily sing with us, b.

label: wedding
translation:
Enjoyment makes us sing.
Nothing can restrain our hearts,
May it be welcome to you,
O bride and groom,
Hello, hello, hello, hello.
With us, it’s always like this.

Let us rejoice together,
Express thanks for the enjoyment,
That suits the wedding party,
Therefore be joyful in spirit,
Hello, etc.

Enjoyment is a blessing

Hello, etc.

translation:
The hymn of praise is sung, celebrants,

Girls together now scatter flowers,

What indeed is this humble earth,  
Without you, attractive marriage,  
You who eternally bind everyone's fate and value

In fulfilling our duty,

translation:
When the first couple united,

No salty tears flowed

Blessing  
This circle

Barren and harsh

label: wedding
translation:
Rejoice, Festive Companions!

Yes, may everyone, cheerful in spirit,

Rejoice, Festive Companions! etc.

Rejoice, Festive Companions! etc.

Rejoice, Festive Companions! etc.

label: wedding
translation:

Cozy, cheerful, common destiny,  
What lies enclosed within it;  
Where love always steers the rudder on board,  
There one sails smoothly and undisturbed,  
Hail, young Newlyweds! repeat.  

For wherever one always sees joy,  
It is not truly so,  
If women are missing. repeat.  
And therefore let, at this reception,  
The greatest glory of our language,

But dear Bride! oh noble flower,

A zero, and with that amen; repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Friends! Wherever we may part,

But the pleasure granted to us,

Whatever time or fate may offer us
In our next life,
The true joy given to us here,
Friends! that is not forgotten. repeat.

From the fullness of the heart,
We who surround you

Now awaits you the marriage bed,
And a ... hurrah! ... quiet, gentle ....

translation:

Ah poor Na, where are you fleeing to,

Pick up, put down, move on,

Ah poor Na, where are you fleeing to?  
Or are you taking Jantje with you,

label: wedding
translation:
Now we sing with joyful sound, Hooray!  
The song for Groom and Bride, Hooray!  
Everyone fills their glass to the brim,  
And calls out in full blazing joy:  
Hooray, hooray, hooray!  
But marriage is no small matter, Oh no!  
The choice is no light task, Oh no!  
So we do not approve any marriage,  
Before you first make your confession to us,  
Oh no, oh no, oh no!  
Getting married sometimes turns out costly, Oh woe!  
And the limping horse comes last, Oh woe!  
Then no weeping helps, no complaining helps,  
It is all thought of too late,  
Oh woe, oh woe, oh woe!  
Speak Groom! Do you truly mean it, Say yes,  
What you have said to the Bride? Say yes,  
Do you promise us, for the dear child,  
Always to be the most faithful friend?  
Say yes, say yes, say yes!  

Do you promise loyalty in love and sorrow? Say yes!  
Whatever may oppose you on earth? Say yes!  
Do you promise as a worthy husband,  
To be faithful to her until death,  
Say yes, say yes, say yes!  
My friends! You have heard it, Hooray!  
The good Groom keeps his word, Hooray!  
But now it is the Bride's turn,  
Then only is the marriage approved;  
Then it’s hooray, hooray!  
Speak Bride! Were you inclined to marriage? Say yes!  
Have you always loved the Groom? Say yes!  
Do you acknowledge your husband as guardian and lord,  
As Eve once did Adam?  
Say yes, say yes, say yes!  
Do you promise on life’s winding path, Say yes,  
Always to lovingly support him? Say yes!  
Do you promise him your love and loyalty,  
As is fitting for the noble wife,  
Say yes, say yes, say yes.

label: wedding
translation:
We sing freely,  
We sing happily,  
Loving always for honor,  
Loving makes us joyful,  
At the pouring of the nectar wine,  
O Loves!  
Descend from your throne,  
Look favorably down upon us.

Hover in this circle,  
Always among one another,  
And shoot your arrows;  
For wherever you shoot your arrows,  
There we are under your rule,  
There is joy,  
And all rejoice,  
With Bridegroom and Bride!

Courtship,  
And wooing,  
Are a foretaste of the sweet;  
That one enjoys in the true state;  
Which offers us the fruit of loving;  
For what we do,  
A kiss or a peck  
Tastes better than anything.

Io vivat!  
This grape juice,  
Is not poured in vain!  
We all ring from around,

Kisses her neighbor’s husband,

label: wedding
translation:
Enjoyment makes us sing,  
Nothing can control our hearts,  
Be welcome,  
O Bride and Groom!  
Hello, Hello, Hello, Hello, ) repeat.  
With us it's always like this. ) repeat.  

Let us rejoice together,  
Express our thanks for enjoyment,  
It suits the wedding celebration,

Hello, Hello, etc.

Enjoyment is a blessing,

Hello, Hello, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
Welcome, Friends! to this table, ha ha!  
Where now joy presides, ha ha!  
We know no care nor pain,  
But want to be truly merry once,  
Ha ha, falderala. repeat.  

Do not be shy but freely reach out, ha ha!  
Eat bravely and drink cheerfully to your fill, ha ha!  
Eat deliciously even and kiss each other,  
In honor of this young Couple,  
Ha ha, falderala.  

Well Groom! stay healthy for a long time, ha ha!  
And above all, eat your fill, ha ha!  
If you want to see a dear sprout,  
Then there will be work involved,  
Ha ha, falderala.  

Yes, Bride! it is granted to you too, ha ha!  
Even if you put on such a prim mouth, ha ha!  
Within a year there will be joy at the top,

Ha ha, falderala.  

The means of your existence, ha ha!  
A glass full can be raised to that, ha ha!  
We wish you a moderate share,  
Too much is unhealthy,  
Ha ha, falderala.  

Come Friends! let’s clink glasses around, ha ha!

That after this green, their silver, gold,

label: wedding
translation:

label: wedding
translation:
Come, Friends! Let us sing,

In honor of the Young Couple.  
See the Betrothed rejoice,  
Let us also express our praise

With harmonious sound.  
We too will strum the strings,  
And wish you happiness,  
Soon the little boat will sail,  
That delights your heart;

Much good fortune, o young people!  
And if you set sail from the quay,  
Then let your worries fade,  
And much luck at sea.  
Betrothed! All sing  
Like Bride and Bridegroom,  
Everything smiles upon you,  
And welcomes you warmly;  
Your Parents and your Friends  
Are in ecstatic spirits,  
All celebrating, united as one,  
Your joyful Wedding feast.

label: wedding
translation:
Whose heart glows for purest pleasure,  
Seeks a dear wife,  
To whom he is steadfastly bound,  
By pure love and loyalty;  
He searches with a joyful mind,  
For an object worthy of him,  
For nothing on earth is as sweet,  
As truly being paired. (repeat)  
Rejoice then, the loving pair,  
Who heart to heart unite,  
And let the cups ring together,  
For Groom and Bride!  
This hour, which fulfills our wish,  
Is truly worth a festive toast,  
For nothing on earth is as sweet,  
As truly being paired. (repeat)  
Live, Groom! live in love and joy,  
With the Bride your friend,  
Who joyfully delights your heart

Make sure a year soon comes to an end,  
That you also gather something young,

Flourish for the welfare of your existence,  
Prosperity above all,

So may a joyful procession play

And the heart tells you, nothing is so sweet,

label: wedding
translation:

Congratulations on your wedding celebration,

Come, let us clink glasses once more, repeated.  
All that we wished for you was sincere,

However harsh nature may appear,

I have only this youthful Couple,  
Our joyful gathering together,

Beloved ones! then step into the boat,

Even if the journey is sometimes full of ice,  
With it you make a safe voyage,  
Whatever the eye may see. repeated.  
Even if sometimes it blows a bit,  
So that the boat turns around,  
You have nothing to dread; repeated.  
If pure love and faithfulness are at the helm,  
Whoever has ever sailed in their company,

Happiness, oh Groom! with this day,

Rejoice on this sailing trip,

translation:

translation:

translation:

How will the Bride then wear earrings,  
It will suit her well,  
It will look sweet,

This will teach her quickly,

Groom, also such a sly fellow,

Be patient, it will be soon;  
Come now, dear! there is what you wait for,  
What you wait for,  
The Bride smiles,  
The joy is written in her eyes,  
Enjoy yourselves,  
Enjoy yourselves,  
Taste much joy together.  
Listen, we will not fail;  
When we come to see the child,  
To congratulate you on that,  
Our heartfelt wishes are there,  
Then the Groom will be the godfather;  
The Bride the godmother, oh then shall;  
Oh yes, then shall,  
I think already,  
Your joy has risen to its peak,  
We expect much,  
We expect much,  
Delicious sugared almonds and cinnamon.

label: wedding
translation:
What a comfort it is in our days,
Where many lament
The old customs that are fading,
That the old-fashioned marriage

Endures through every fashion;
Love certainly understands its matter well,
It always provides fun for boys and girls,
And oh! oh! how clever is that rascal. (repeat)

Again a Groom and a Bride,
Who no longer lose sight

There they stand ready, to sail on the waves
Of the sea,
Where so many have already crossed.
Come, friends! let us wish them blessing and peace,

For oh! oh! they are sailing across the sea. (repeat)

Sail cheerfully onward, oh dear people!
All our wishes
Accompany you on your journey,
And if you sometimes feel a little bump,
May the little marriage boat

Never be visited by a storm.
But I do not fear that anything will disturb your sailing,
For love and friendship go aboard with you,
And oh! oh! then it will go well. (repeat)

So may your well-united life
Give you all the good things
That one alone cannot enjoy;
It is said there is no happiness on earth
As valuable
As the sweet pleasure for woman and man.
I believe it, for many have preceded you,
There are already others ready for the boat again;
And oh! oh! they are looking forward to it. (repeat)

We wish that you may find it so,
Beloved friends!
And always be joyful in your fate;
That once leaning on a little cane,
You may still praise the yoke of marriage
For its sweet pleasure.
And rejoice, surrounded by prosperity and joy,
In honor of love, which gladdens the heart,
And oh! oh! of grayness and youth. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Who ever saw a merry feast of friends,  
Where no singing took place,  
Especially when at the generous reception,  
One celebrates grand festivities;  
Let us then begin,  
To a Dutch festive tune,  
The heartfelt Celebration Song,

By us then, this first song

We wish that you may long  
Enjoy the most blessed fate,  
Enjoy all that marital fidelity

Thus your Bride will be the happiest woman,  
You a happy man. bis.  

We too, we perform our duty  
As well as we can,  
This song is thus directed by us  
To you, dear Bride!  
Always enjoy lasting and full  
What conjugal happiness increases,  
May the most blessed fate be your lot,  
With your Husband. bis.  

And now finally, o band of friends!  
Observe the old custom,  
Bring the cup to the worthy couple,  
Well-meaning offered;  
Well then, fellow diners! come,  
Pour in and drink up,  
To the welfare of Bride and Groom,  
Of Groom and Bride. bis.

label: wedding
translation:
Where is one more joyful  
Than at a wedding celebration?  
Fun, fun, always fun,  

Be cheerful, As with these,  
Fun, fun, always fun,  
Is now a rule for us.

label: wedding
translation:
Those who love marriage, those who love marriage,  
They love a pure joy,  
Though one had house and farm and gold,  
It is less without being married,  
Those who love marriage, those who love marriage,  
They find a wonderful fate.

The marriage bond, the marriage bond  
Is good in joy and sorrow,  
There one shares every blessing together,  
It makes life pleasant for us.  
The marriage bond, the marriage bond,  
Is good in joy and sorrow.

label: wedding
translation:
Who is there, joyful in spirit,
Who does not gladly raise his song of praise,
On this so joyful feast,
Of Bridegroom and Bride!
The Couple, who meets our eye here,
Is truly worthy of that praise,
May they remain in prosperity and adversity
Long saved for each other. repeat.

Enjoy, O Bridegroom and Bride!
The greatest happiness on earth,
That nothing ever hinders your delight,
Freed from disaster and distress,

Step happily into the marriage boat,
It carries you along a smooth path,
Sincere loyalty and marital love
Scatter roses along your way. repeat.

Live contentedly, healthy and happy,
Many years together,
May every day be blessed,
Your blessed state undisturbed;
Marital bliss increase your joy,
Though it be your fate on earth,
And remain as a reward for your virtue,
Long paired together. repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Io vive!
Io vive!
Hail to this celebration of friends,
Let no one ask about age or youth,
Each heart be open to joy,
Io vive! etc.
Beware care and sorrow,
Avoid our hearts,
Be far away from here, grief.

In this Wedding Hall
No other speech is known than jubilation,
Beware care and sorrow, etc.
The generous joy,
That delights the heart,
Descend among us
With festive song and cheerfulness,
Let this day be dedicated to joy,
The generous joy, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
How blessedly the bond of marriage  
Binds a loving couple as one,  
When the bright torch of true love burns;  
All sorrow flies away;  
The good man, at all times  
Faithful to virtue and duty,  
Always finds the reward of his diligence  
In the arms of his wife. (repeat)  
This priceless marital happiness  
Falls, Groom! to your lot,  
You face no binding yoke of marriage,  
But gentle and sweet caresses,  
And what a pleasant prospect

When she, unseen and unspied,  
Embraces her husband in her arms. (repeat)  
The joyful happiness, united couple!  
Dwell in your household,  
A marriage sprout will crown within a year  
Your mutual tender love;  
May you both live long and happily together,  
Equally worthy of each other,  
And bear the honorable family name,  
The most beautiful name on earth. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
O friends! Sing now loudly,  
To the praise of Bridegroom and Bride,

But if the singing goes smoothly,  
And arises from no dry throat,  
Then one must drink cheerfully, repeated.  
The Bridegroom is a clever fellow,  
Yes, he is quite resourceful,  
That will bring him joy; repeated.  
He shows himself, when he kisses and caresses,  
A friend of Mother Eve’s image,  
And that is why he paired up, repeated.  
He has well courted his Bride,  
And wished for the wedding time,  
He could no longer delay; repeated.  
And spoke, “my dearest darling!  
Might I already be married to you,  
Oh, please set the day.” repeated.

The Bride, a maiden of genuine kind;  
Is exactly a perfect match for him,  
Well worthy to be praised; repeated.  
Her youth promises him joy,  
So she will be in the wedding garden  
A blooming little rose. repeated.  
Though she looked modestly ahead,  
When he spoke yes, she did not say no,  
She was willing to take a chance, repeated.  
The betrothal is now quickly done,  
And they enter companionship,  
For all their days of life. repeated.  
Hail to you, o Bridegroom and Bride!  
We all wish, in conclusion,  
May your prosperity grow long, repeated.  
May you also, o tender-loving Couple!  
Be three within a year,  
And see your marriage flourish. repeated.

label: wedding
translation:
Whose heart glows with purest delight,  
He joyfully and gladly celebrates  
The festival that binds hearts together,  
Already of this couple, with me,  

Let him once join me in spirit,  
To Dutch rhythmic sound,  
The heartfelt festive song,  
For Groom and Bride.

label: wedding
translation:
Come then, friends! Raise your glasses,  
Sing united from a free heart,  
The wedding feast is given to us,  
Which bears so many cares;  
Let your songs resound loudly,  
Now that you see each other,  
Once to drink to the youthful couple,  
Forget this not, forget not the friendship,  
The first toast then truly be, ) repeat.  
To this happy, to this happily united couple, ) repeat.

May both efforts remain united,  
Devoted to the blessed marriage,  
Man and Woman show this power,  
Led by the glow of love,  
Spread yourselves under the wedding shade,  
Be content in your position,  
Support each other in sorrow,  
Faithfully reach out your hand to one another.  
The second toast then sound loudly again ) repeat.  
To the Groom, to the Groom and his Bride. ) repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, now clear from the chest  
Our festive song must sound,  
But singing brings thirst,  
Singers must drink.  
Fill the glass to the brim,  
Then it is indeed at hand,  
See what a lovely little shine, ) repeat.  
It is a lovely little wine. ) repeat.  
Everyone take part in the joyful song;  
Join the chorus,  
Let us not keep our joy silent!  
Let the neighbors hear it,  
How on this happy celebration,  
Forgetful of worries, we are joyful,  
Let the festive song rise, ) repeat.  
No one may be silent here. ) repeat.  
Hail to you, Bride! Health and joy  
We wish you from the heart,  
All that gladdens your heart,  
Never yield to sorrow.

Taste what increases your happiness,  
Always taste with your spouse,  
Prosperity everywhere, ) repeat.  
Joy, health, and blessing. ) repeat.  
Hail to you, Groom! at all times  
May your fortune increase,  
May sorrow silently pass your door,  
May it turn its back on you;  
Misfortune remain always strange to you,  
In all that you undertake,  
Then you both can always, ) repeat.  
Delight in your fate. ) repeat.  
Then rejoice, Friends! joyful in spirit,  
Let the sound of rejoicing be heard,  
At the pleasant wedding celebration,  
May the joy be pleasing.  
Blend the clinking of the cups,  
Joining in friendship,  
Then raise the glass, ) repeat.  
May the Bridal Couple live. ) repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
When the first couple joined together,  
In Eden's flowery garden decided,  
No festive toast yet echoed around,  
For see, it had no dining companions.  
But the feathered creatures did not remain silent,  
And came to crown that marriage with praise,  
Yes, they sang a striking wedding song,  
To show joy to the jubilant couple. (repeat)

The field was the green wedding feast,  
On which no cook displayed his art,  
There was no roast nor fish to be found,  
But the tasty fruit from woods and valleys.  
Clear water flowed instead of wine,  
Yet love might crown the celebration.  
One can be joyful with little,  
If love and joy inhabit the heart. (repeat)

Praise still be to these later times,  
Now we see the table adorned with taste,  
Sometimes one is so greatly delighted,  
When a large company may celebrate.  
Though Adam might crown his marriage  
With sips of mild water,  
We prefer to use grape juice,  
To show the marrying couple more joy. (repeat)

You then, who are paired today,  
We wish to toast in your honor,  
Your wine will not be spared by us,  
It is to drink to your health.  
If love already makes you happy,  
May friendship also inhabit your heart,  
We bring you this cup of wine,  
To show you our friendship. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Come, Celebrants! sing a song,  
To increase your joy,  
The pleasure that is offered us now,  
Comes to us clearer than before our eyes,  
And wishes the youthful couple much happiness,  
And heartfelt joy in married life,  
Although the marriage path is sometimes steep,  
It must often offer them contentment. (repeat)

We wish that this joyful day  
May caress you for many years to come,  
And if friendship can do anything,  
Oh, may we then share in their well-being;  
May virtue adorn your marriage bond,  
And make your fidelity in domestic life,  
Through duty you enjoy welfare and peace,  
Then continue to strive for that with love. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Take up the friendship cither,  
Tune the strings joyfully,  
May marriage bring you laurel leaves,  
Blessings be upon you pairs;  
Step joyfully into marriage,  
Love brings you the purest gain,  
Bride and Groom both!  
May your separation be long delayed.

I wish you the purest marital happiness,  
Youthful spouses!  
Have for your duties all that is precious,  
That may surround you;

Step joyfully into marriage,  
Love brings you the purest gain,  
Bride and Groom both!  
May your separation be long delayed.

Parents! Brothers! Sisterly kin!  
Make your wishes heard,  
Not without love in all,  
Nothing will disturb this.  
Step joyfully into marriage,  
Love brings you the purest gain,  
Bride and Groom both!  
May your separation be long delayed.

label: wedding
translation:
You are welcome, twice.  
The table is prepared;  
The dessert is elegantly arranged,  
So that everyone eagerly grasps the utensils,  
You are welcome, twice.  
Proceed to the feast.  

Pour into the glass, twice.  
In an ecstatic state,  
And drink with bride and groom,  
The heartfelt welcome,  
Pour into the glass, twice.  
Up to the brim.

label: wedding
translation:
Started  
Songs of joy  
Must be repeated;  
From our singing,  
The echoes  
Must resound through the halls;  
Hail to married life,  
Given to us on earth,  
Hail to that love, hail to that fidelity,  
For both Man and Woman.

Acting alone,  
Walking alone,  
Cannot soothe the heart,  
To a heart,  
Joy and sorrow

To steadily share;  
Only that can give,  
The blessed married life,  
Hail to that love, hail to that fidelity,  
For both Man and Woman.

With burdens,  
And dangers,  
One often has to struggle,  
For the worries  
Of tomorrow,  
Often suffering too much;  
The blessed married life  
Will bring relief,  
Hail to that love, hail to that fidelity,  
For both Man and Woman.

label: wedding
translation:
Rise, oh Feast Companions!  
It is time to go,  
It has not saddened us,  
But hear the bells ring;  
Now it is time to part,  

Yet against all sorrow,  
One can still endure a little longer,  
Until the farewell of the Dessert.  
Hello, hello, hello, hello!  
With us, it always goes like this, bis.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, friends! All rise,  
With a delighted heart,  
And let us raise the closing song,  
As our farewell greeting.  
We are here at this Wedding feast,  
So cheerful and so glad,  
As friends have gathered together,  
May this be enough. (repeat)  

Now let us think of the time,  
That governs all our actions,  
And let us close this ceremony  
Now with a farewell song.  

A farewell song in both notes,  
Binds the friendship bond,  
So that the end crowns the work,  
And each reaches out their hand. (repeat)  

Where friends are gathered,  
So united and well,  
With the pouring of a good glass of wine;  
Time flies by so fast;  
There one says with delighted spirit,  
No matter how one laughs about it,  
At the end of a wedding feast,  
In the morning; good night. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Well man! What have you made up for me there,  
And started,  
Are you really getting married today?  
I hope it will be lies,  
Because I would fear,  
If it’s certain tomorrow, you’ll regret it;  
But looking at your clothes and face,  
I consider everything fairly true,  
And oh, oh! you are out in the cold.  (chorus)

Look at that young woman’s eyes,  
What power  
There is in them for love,  
If I wanted to complain about you even more,  
I’d surely get beaten,  
Because both of you are itching with desire now;  
How your cows and sheep cry out for revenge,  
That you now make your own lambs,  
Oh, oh, where have you ended up?  (chorus)

But, friends! It must be so;  
You can read it,  
It’s all written in the book at the City Hall,  
How Japik Stok [and] Trijntje Helder,  
Hans in the cellar,  
Now go playing right up to their crucifix;  
Because look for a boy or for a girl here;  
Then one is called Motje and the other Vaer,  
And oh, oh! then people look so strange.  (chorus)

How will little Motje have to pee,  
And have to wash,  
The diapers of that little child,  
And in order to raise that sheep big,  
Jaep has to stand watch,  
Or else he would do something else;  
And if he thinks of going to sleep in the evening;  
That little woman winks at him so flirtatiously,  
Oh, man! man! what have you done.  (chorus)

label: wedding
translation:
Love will live,  
To a sweet plague of youth,  
He shoots a little wound,  
It grants heavenly joy.  

This feast prepared for us by Love,  
Dedicated to joy,  
And whoever here kneels, grumbles, or sulks,  
We will drive them out.

label: wedding
translation:
Who is the jewel of this celebration? The Bride.  
Who awakens in us that spirit of joy? The Bride.  
Who is it that greets us happily,  
And makes us sing so cheerfully?  
The Bride, The Bride, The Bride.  

Who is it that smiles so kindly? The Bride,  
Who is the most highly esteemed here? The Bride.  
Who is it, the subject of this song?  
Who offers every praise and honor?  
The Bride, etc.  

Who is it, adorned so beautifully? The Bride.  
Who is it that everyone celebrates? The Bride.  
Who is it that keeps young and old here  
So happy and elated?  
The Bride, etc.  

Who is it that captivates us all? The Bride.  
Who is the little flower that blooms here? The Bride.  
Who is the most welcome here?  
The little rose of the Bridegroom?  
The Bride, etc.  

Who is the little flower of the bouquet? The Bride.  
Who is it that Cupid boasts about? The Bride,  
Who is it that shines with brilliance,  
On whom the Bridegroom proudly triumphs?  
The Bride, etc.  

Who is the precious jewel of marriage? The Bride.  
Who is it for whom garlands are woven? The Bride.  
Who is it for whom everyone toasts  
And drinks to well-being in triumph?  
The Bride, etc.

label: wedding
translation:
In hearts that blaze with love,  
The Spouse is a balm for their pain,  
Can greater joy be tasted by a mortal,  
Than to be paired according to his wish,  
The joy, the earthly bliss,  
Is surely love alone. (repeat)

Love defies the cruelest torments,  
No suffering is too heavy or bitter for it,  
It weaves through the days of human life,  
The purest delight of nature.  
Come, let us sing the praise of faithfulness,  
Now you are both husband and wife. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Do you remember that time so dear,
When you were joined together,
Twenty-five years ago,
Do you remember that happy time,
When you were united together
Before the human marriage altar?

label: wedding
translation:
We cheer freely, we cheer gladly,  
With a clear mind,  
In honor of Bride and Groom,  
All who breathe sing welcome,  
We cheer freely, we cheer gladly,  
At the Silver Wedding celebration.

Everyone sings, everyone sings,  
And wishes, oh Silver couple!  
May all go equally well for you,  
And may misfortune never strike you,  
Everyone sings, everyone sings,  
May you live long always together.

Each one, joyful in honor and virtue,  
Rejoices in this wedding hall,  
And sings the praise of the Silver couple,  
With blood relatives and circle of friends,  
Each one, joyful, in honor and virtue,  
Rejoices in this triumph.

And everyone wishes, and everyone wishes,  
You a joyful spirit;  
May you long still savor as Husband and Wife,  
The blessing of marital loyalty,  
We celebrate freely, we celebrate gladly,  
Your Silver wedding feast.

label: wedding
translation:
Come, Friends! Come, sing now joyfully,  
With a cheerful spirit,  
Set your worries aside for now,  
And breathe freely at this celebration,  
Joyfully celebrating the solemn jubilee,  
Already of this Silver Jubilee. repeat.

Long live the Groom! Long live the Bride!  
This be our motto all,  
That God may grant them prosperity for long,  
And surround them with His blessing,  
Who has called them together,  
By their mutual choice. repeat.

See how the fire of love at this hour  
Still shines from their eyes,  
Though the autumn day is usually harsh,  
Here it is spring in the end,  
Here it is still true, where nature  
Shines forth in all its splendor. repeat.

May many years, blessed Couple!  
Prepare for you the most fortunate fate,  
And celebrate with friends and children;  
Protected by the Almighty,  
Once again the joyful jubilee year  
Of your Golden time. repeat.

label: wedding
translation:
Raise the glasses to the mouth,  
Let us cheerfully toast,  
Willingly to the bottom,  
Drink him empty;  
For the bridegroom, who now happily,  
With his bride by his side,  
Celebrates the blessed union ) twice.  
Of twenty-five years. ) twice.

It was no abundance of wealth,  
That could please them,  
But a cheerful spirit,  
Born for each other;  
They defy adversity.  
Household quarrels were steadily prevented,  
Always to live in virtue, ) twice.  
Was the aim of both. ) twice.

label: wedding
translation:
At the ringing of the festive songs;  
Is now celebrated the joyful day,  
On which you, by sweetest longing,  
Were once truly united in marriage;  
A silver wreath encircles your hair,  
O worthy couple! so tenderly loved,  
And the number of twenty-five years,  
Adorns the bond that unites you. (repeat)

Notice from the joyful tones of friends,  
The sigh that flows from the heart,  
That to crown your pure union,  
Glows with love and gratitude;  

See generous joy spark in the eyes;  
While this fulfills your wish,  
And here, for the gift of your well-being,  
Savors the unlimited pleasure. (repeat)

Remember the most blessed pairing,  
Long unburdened by disaster or care,  
Where even despite the burden of years,  
Your fate turns the heart to joy.  
Thus you, arm in arm linked,  
Cheerfully travel life’s path,  
Richly blessed by heaven,  
And thus guided to the grave. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
At the clinking of the glasses,  
At the sparkling of the wine,  
One gladly wishes to repeat  
Why we are gathered together.  
Friends! Raise your voices,  
Sing and drink,  
Twenty-five years have flown by,  
May this number still increase,  
May this couple stay together,  
Long live the Silver Wedding couple!

Many joined together,  
Were united by marriage,  
But not all have experienced  
The privilege granted to this couple.  
Therefore, raise your voices,  

Sing and drink.  
May the little marriage boat navigate,  
Turn in all directions,  
Contentment was always there,  
Long live the Silver Wedding couple.

Children! Friends! Table companions!  
Who find yourselves at this celebration,  
Let joy arise,  
Which surely everyone loves,  
Without compulsion,  
May it still long  
Make us remember this generous celebration,  
And grant the Silver Couple,  
A memory full of pleasure,  
May both their fates always be blessed.

label: wedding
translation:
Now hearts and feelings come together,
At the celebration so rarely held on earth,
Of this worthy couple, who now together
Celebrate here their Silver Wedding year. twice.

The blessed marriage, which has delighted their hearts,
Gives them anew a heavenly joy,
As one soul and mind, the God of love
Leads their happiness back into the holy temple. twice.

label: wedding
translation:
Cheerful, oh friends!  
See how the cup shines,  
Empty the glasses,  
Rejoice now and drink.  
All of nature drinks along with us,  
The sun, the moon, and mountain and sea,  
Come, let us follow that high path,  
And continue the drinking.  

Cheerful, oh friends! etc.  
The earth drinks the rainwater,  
Which contains more than gold,  
And through its desire to drink,  
Constantly gives us flowers and fresh fruits.  

Cheerful, etc.  
The infant drinks at mother's breast,  

The nectar that carries the powers,  
And grows and blooms continually from that drink,  
To the joy of the elders.  

Cheerful, etc.  

The young man then drinks heavenly joy,  
When he kisses the red lips  
Of the girl who so gladly  
Wants to walk the path of life with him.  

Cheerful, etc.  

The man, no matter how burdened with care,  
Feels his heart then beat with happiness,  
When, after work, with pleasure,  
He sits with the woman and the glass.

label: wedding
translation:
The day of the joyous celebration is here,  
The joy sounds loudly,  
In honor of this worthy couple,  
Adorned with silver crowns.  
Raise your song, o friends!  
Sing merrily, again and again,  
So that your cheerful festivity  
Sounds in honor of the Silver Pair. (repeat)  

The groom, esteemed for his virtue,  
As well as his worthy bride,  
They receive our best wishes,  
With lively accompaniment;  
Yes, certainly upon the generous reception,  
They are now welcome here,  
As they proudly appear once again  
As bride and groom. (repeat)  

How many honest and virtuous pledges  
Adorn your marriage crown,  
And contribute to the joy of the bond,  
Your marriage as a reward;  

Their grateful heart is devoted to you,  
So noble for your virtue,  
Behold how that heart is now delighted,  
And rejoices in your happiness. (repeat)  

May you both live long, blessed with well-being,  
Finding gentle cause for joy,  
Let this joyous celebration be for you a symbol,  
Of the marriage’s flower garden;  
Proceed together with a clear mind,  
To many more years,  
And celebrate once the Golden wedding,  
As joyfully as this hour. (repeat)  

O marital love! O highest joy,  
That delights the human heart,  
O friendship! pure soul’s delight,  
To you both our song honors;  
Weave mirth and palm into a victory crown,  
Share your influence with us,  
For you resound the happiest tone,  
At the universal hurrah! (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
Come, Guests of the Celebration!  
See how the cup shines,  
Empty the glasses,  
Cheer now and drink;  
Drink now in honor of the worthy couple,  
Who today see themselves united in the finest way  
For twenty-five years,  
Come, let us pay them tribute.

Come, Guests of the Celebration! etc.  
In celebrating this Silver Wedding,  
Remember the Groom and his wife,  
The sweetness enjoyed in their marriage,  
Although it is not spoken of to us.

Come, Guests of the Celebration! etc.  
Happy Groom and Bride!  
Grant that our wish unfolds for you,

Enjoy health, peace, and quiet,  
Until a ripe old age:

Come, Guests of the Celebration! etc.  
That you may once again experience the day,  
On which you celebrate your golden wedding,  
And God’s dear blessing  
Accompanies your remaining time.

Come, Guests of the Celebration! etc.  
Come, let us now drink to conclude,  
The last glass offered with goodwill,  
To the undisturbed sacred joy  
Of Groom and Bride.  
Come, Guests of the Celebration!  
See how the Bride and Groom wait,  
Let us wish the couple  
The joy of the first night.

label: wedding
translation:
Heave up, heave up, oh band of friends!  
Sing with joyful spirit,  
With me the song of happiness,  
At the solemn Silver Celebration.  
That we here celebrate gladly tired,  
Come, let us pay our tribute  
To this so worthy couple.  

Yes, our hearts beat happily this hour,  
For the happiness you enjoy,  
Now you have twenty-five years,  
In joy and sorrow,  
In love and grief, in bitter and sweet,  
Shared together, and abundance  
Always accompanied you everywhere.

label: wedding
translation:
For a truly happy married life;  
Their hearts and tune now paired together,  
The blessing, this couple given by God,  
Is rarely found on this earth;  
Come, let us freely dedicate our songs  
To this couple, on their Silver Jubilee. (repeat)

Let, feast companions! let your joy  
Express itself in this festive hour;  
The marriage that joyfully fills their hearts,  
May their first love's fire never fade;  
The God of love, leads gladly,  
Anew into their Hymen’s temple. (repeat)

See how the bridegroom, elated  
With joy, looks at his dear bride;  
See how from the bride’s loving eyes  
Love continually shoots for her bridegroom.  
As one soul, as one mind,  
Their hearts still glow with loving passion. (repeat)

Surrounded by their marriage bonds,  
Here at this solemn festive reception,  
They see their joy increase even more,  
That—let it be our heart’s language—  
That God, oh couple! For many more years,  
May spare you both for their wellbeing. (repeat)

label: wedding
translation:
You, worthy Couple! add the joy,  
Already you reap, while the strength of youth  
Still shines with spring foliage. (repeat)  
Though time flies swiftly by,  
It granted you prosperity, strength, and glow,  
Whatever power may conquer. (repeat)
